title: HYDERABADI CHICKEN DUM BIRYANI RECIPE | हैदराबादी चिकन दम बिरयानी बनाने का तरीका | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
This is just the beginning of Biryanis of India.

Tell me in the comments which biryani you want me to cover next. I'll be reading every suggestion, and hopefully, together, we'll build the Welcome to “BIRYANIS OF INDIA” a brand new YFL series! 🇮🇳♥️

There are recipes... and then there are dishes that become a part of a country's identity.

For me, biryani is one of them.

The more I travelled, cooked and met people across India, the more I realised that there isn't just one biryani. Every region has its own story. Every community has its own technique. Different rice, different spices, different cooking methods, different philosophies. And somehow, every single one is beautiful in its own way.

That's why I wanted to make this series.

Not just to show you recipes, but to understand why each biryani is the way it is. Where it comes from. What makes it unique. The little details that often get lost when a recipe is reduced to a one-minute video.

This is my tribute to one of the greatest dishes our country has given the world.
These videos are longer than what we usually watch today, because some things deserve time. If you truly love food, I hope you'll enjoy sitting through the journey as much as you'll enjoy cooking the recipe at home.

I genuinely can't wait to take this journey with all of you.

Lots of love,
Sanjyot ❤️

title: KATHIYAWADI Lasaniya Bateta | SPICY, GARLICKY Aloo Recipe | काठियावाड़ी लहसुनि आलू | Sanjyot Keer
description:
Kathiyawadi Lasaniya Bateta is a fiery, garlicky, and tangy Kathiyawadi classic recipe that's packed with bold flavours. The base of the recipe is a delicious lasoon ki chutney cooked with a simple tadka and the dish turns out absolutely incredible. It pairs perfectly with bajre ka rotla, or some khidchdj and this season is one of the best times to enjoy this spicy yet comforting dish.🌶️🧄🥔

I've shared the full written recipe below, so do give it a try and let me know how you like it. Happy cooking! ❤️

Crafted with care. Rooted in tradition. Designed to last 🤎 Our Wooden Masala Dani blends timeless craftsmanship with everyday functionality, keeping your spices fresh and your kitchen elegant.
Add a touch of heritage to your cooking ❤️

🛒 Available now:
Website: https://curaahome.com/products/curaa-wooden-masala-dani
Amazon: https://amzn.in/d/8ixtD2f

Full written recipe - Lasaniya Bateta
Prep Time: 15–20 minutes 
Cooking Time: 20–25 minutes 
Serving: 4-5 people 

Ingredients:
Spiced Potatoes
BOILED BABY POTATOES | उबले बेबी आलू 500 GRAMS 
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1 TSP
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/4 TSP
BLACK SALT | काला नमक 1/4 TSP
GROUNDNUT OIL | मूंगफली का तेल 1–2 TSP
Garlic Paste
GARLIC | लहसुन 35 GRAMS
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 2 TBSP
SPICY RED CHILLI POWDER | तीखी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1 TBSP
CORIANDER POWDER | धनिया पाउडर 1/2 TBSP
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/4 TSP
CUMIN POWDER | जीरा पाउडर 1/2 TSP
SALT | नमक A LARGE PINCH
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED
Final Cooking
GROUNDNUT OIL | मूंगफली का तेल 2–3 TBSP
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा A LARGE PINCH
ASAFOETIDA | हींग 1/4 TSP
ONION | प्याज़ 1 LARGE SIZED (CHOPPED)
TOMATO | टमाटर 1 MEDIUM SIZED (CHOPPED)
SALT | नमक A PINCH
HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED
SPICED POTATOES | AS PREPARED
HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED (TO ADJUST CONSISTENCY)
GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला A PINCH
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
Method:

To marinate the potatoes, add kashmiri red chilli powder, turmeric, black salt, and groundnut oil to the potatoes and mix well so every potato is evenly coated with the masala. Set aside.
Take a mortar and pestle and add the garlic with a large pinch of salt. Pound it into a coarse paste. 
Add the Kashmiri red chilli powder and spicy red chilli powder and mix it into the pounded garlic. This is basically the garlic chutney base that will majorly flavour the dish. 
Transfer this to a bowl and add coriander powder, turmeric, and cumin powder. Add water gradually and mix into a thick paste.
Take a kadhai on high flame and add groundnut oil. Let it get warm. 
Add the spiced potatoes and fry on high flame, stirring as you go, until they develop a light texture on the outside, about 3–4 minutes. Transfer them to a bowl and set aside. This step is optional but it helps to develop a nice texture.
In the same oil, add cumin seeds and hing and let them crackle. Add the chopped onion, stir, and cook on high flame for 3–4 minutes until the onion softens.
Add the prepared garlic paste along with the chopped tomato and a pinch of salt. Stir and cook on high flame for 2–3 minutes until the raw smell of the garlic fades completely.
Add hot water to bring the masala to a thick, slightly loose consistency. Cover and cook on medium flame for 3–4 minutes until the tomato is fully cooked through and no raw smell remains.
Add the fried potatoes and mix well so everything comes together. You can serve it dry at this stage, but if you want a little more gravy, add a splash of hot water and stir. The consistency should be semi-thick, coating the potatoes without being watery.
Taste and adjust salt. Finish with a pinch of garam masala and mix through. Add fresh coriander and stir lightly.
Your garlicky, spicy and super delicious Lasaniya Bateta is ready. Serve hot with bajre ka rotla, rice or khichdi.

title: Stuffed BAINGAN MASALA Recipe | BHARLI VANGI | महाराष्ट्रियन भरवां बैगन की सब्ज़ी | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
Maharashtrian-style Bharleli Vangi, tangy, spicy, bold, and packed with flavour. This Bharleli Vangi pairs perfectly with a hot rice bhakri and is one of the best lunch dishes to enjoy during this monsoon season. I've shared the easiest way to make this traditional recipe at home, so do give it a try and I'm sure you'll enjoy it.
The full written recipe is shared below. Happy cooking! ❤️

Our BlendLab - Designed to make everyday kitchen tasks faster, easier, and more enjoyable

🛒 Buy on Amazon: https://amzn.in/d/1X1FhAu
🌐 Curaa Website: https://curaahome.com/products/curaa-blendlab

Full written recipe - Maharashtrian Style Bharleli Vangi

Prep Time: 10-15 minutes 
Cooking Time: 25–30 minutes 
Serving: 3–4 people

Ingredients:
BRINJAL | बैंगन 6 NOS. / 300 GRAMS
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED
SALT | नमक 1 TSP (FOR WASHING)

Stuffing Masala
DRY COCONUT | सूखा नारियल 1/3 CUP / 25 GRAMS (GRATED)
GINGER | अदरक 1 INCH
GARLIC | लहसुन 7–8 CLOVES
WHITE SESAME SEEDS | सफ़ेद तिल 1 TBSP
PEANUTS | मूंगफली 1/3 CUP / 55 GRAMS
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL
GODA MASALA | गोडा मसाला 1.5 TSP
KANDA LAHSUN MASALA | कांदा लहसुन मसाला 2 TBSP
TAMARIND PULP | इमली का गूदा 2 TSP
JAGGERY | गुड़ A PINCH
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/4 TSP
SALT | नमक TO TASTE

Gravy
GROUNDNUT OIL | मूंगफली का तेल 3–4 TBSP
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1/2 TSP
MUSTARD SEEDS | राई 1/2 TSP
ASAFOETIDA | हींग 1/4 TSP
CURRY LEAVES | कड़ी पत्ता 10–12 NOS.
ONION | प्याज़ 1 LARGE SIZED (CHOPPED)
HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)

Method:
Use a paring knife or peeler to peel the skin from the tops of the brinjals. Trim the stem slightly, keeping it intact so the brinjal holds its shape while stuffing.
Make a cross cut from the base of each brinjal, going about three-quarters of the way up, do not cut all the way through. While cutting, check each one for any worms inside.
Dissolve salt in a bowl of water and place the cut brinjals in it. This cleans them well and lets a little salt seep in. Leave them soaking while you prepare the masala.
To make the masala for stuffing and gravy, heat a frying pan on high flame, then lower the flame. Add the grateddry coconut, ginger, garlic, and white sesame seeds. Dry roast on low flame, stirring continuously, until the coconut turns light golden. Transfer to a bowl and let it cool completely.
In the same pan, add the peanuts and roast on medium flame until golden brown. Transfer to the same bowl and let everything cool completely.
Once cooled, transfer to a mixer jar, add fresh coriander, and grind to a coarse mixture, not too fine.
Transfer to a bowl and add goda masala, kanda lahsun masala, tamarind pulp, jaggery, turmeric, and salt. Mix everything together well. Taste and adjust salt. Your stuffing masala is ready.
Take the soaked brinjals out of the water and pat them dry with a clean cloth. Open the cross cut gently and stuff each brinjal tightly with the masala, pressing it in well. Repeat for all the brinjals. Keep any leftover masala aside, it will go into the gravy.
Heat a kadhai on high flame and add groundnut oil. Once hot, add mustard seeds, cumin seeds, hing, and curry leaves and let them crackle. Add the chopped onion and cook on high flame, stirring, until it softens.
Carefully add the stuffed brinjals into the kadhai and cook on high flame for 3–4 minutes, turning gently, until they pick up a little colour on the outside.
Add the leftover stuffing masala and mix carefully so everything comes together. Add hot water and stir gently to bring the gravy together.
Cover and cook on low flame for 8–10 minutes until the brinjals are completely soft and cooked through.
Open the lid and check, the vangi (brinjals) should be perfectly cooked and the masala will look rich and thick. If you prefer a little more gravy, add hot water and stir gently to your preferred consistency.
Taste and adjust salt. Finish with fresh coriander and mix lightly.
Your super delicious Maharashtrian Style Bharleli Vangi is ready. Serve hot with bhakri or roti and steamed rice.

title: Punjabi CHIKKAD CHOLE | MUST-TRY Chole Recipe | पंजाबी चिक्कड़ चोले | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
This Punjabi Chikkad Chole turns out incredibly rich, smooth, and packed with flavour. ✨ Pair it with bread kulchas and green chutney, and you have the ultimate comfort meal. The full written recipe and my special chana masala recipe are mentioned below. Happy Cooking! ❤️

Say hello to the BLP 2.0, your new kitchen superstar that makes cooking easier, faster, and way more fun 🥰🚀

Happy shopping! 🛍️ 
🛒 Buy on Amazon: https://amzn.in/d/00oVb9g

Full written recipe - Punjabi Chikkad Chole

Prep Time: 15–20 minutes + overnight soaking 
Cooking Time: 35–40 minutes 
Serving: 3–4
Ingredients:
Boiling Chickpeas
CHICKPEAS | काबुली चना 1 CUP (WASHED & SOAKED OVERNIGHT OR 8–10 HOURS)
POTATO | आलू 1 MEDIUM SIZED 
BAY LEAF | तेज़ पत्ता 2 NOS. 
CINNAMON | दालचीनी 1 INCH 
BLACK CARDAMOM | बड़ी इलायची 2 NOS
CLOVES | लौंग 5–6 NOS
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED
Paste
GARLIC | लहसुन 10–12 CLOVES
GINGER | अदरक 2 INCH
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 3 NOS.
FRESH CORIANDER STEMS | हरे धनिये की डंठल A HANDFUL
CURD | दही 1/2 CUP
Tempering & Cooking
MUSTARD OIL | सरसों का तेल 2–3 TBSP
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1/2 TSP
HING | हींग 1/4 TSP
ONION | प्याज़ 2 SMALL SIZED (CHOPPED)
chickpeas MASALA | छोले मसाला 1.5 TBSP
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
BOILED CHICKPEAS | उबले छोले AS PREPARED
Second Tempering 
GHEE | घी 2 TBSP
CAROM SEEDS | अजवाइन 1/2 TSP
HING | हींग 1/4 TSP
KASURI METHI | कसूरी मेथी 1 TSP
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS. (SLIT)
GINGER | अदरक A HANDFUL (JULIENNE)
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1 TSP
Finish
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
Method:
Add the soaked chickpeas and potato into a pressure cooker. Tie the whole spices into a small muslin potli and add that in as well. Add water until it sits about an inch above the chickpeas. Close the lid and cook on medium flame for about 15 minutes. Switch off the flame and let the cooker depressurise naturally.
Once open, check the potatoes should be fully cooked and the chickpeas soft. Discard the potli. Remove the boiled potatoes and a small handful of the chickpeas into a separate bowl and let them cool down enough to blend.
Once slightly cooled, transfer the reserved potato and chickpeas to a mixer jar. Add ingredients of the laste and blend into a completely smooth, fine paste and set aside.
Heat a kadhai and add mustard oil, let it smoke properly. Lower the flame and add cumin seeds, hing, and the chopped onions and cook on high flame, until they turn light golden brown.
Lower the flame and add the prepared paste, stir it slightly and then add chana masala and salt. Cook on high flame, roast the masala until the masala stops sticking to the kadhai.
Add the boiled chickpeas along with the water and mix well. Bring everything to a boil. 
Once it comes to a boil, cook for 7–8 minutes. You will see the consistency thicken up beautifully. Taste and adjust salt. Switch off the flame.
Heat a tadka pan and add ghee. Once hot, add remaining ingredients of the second tempering and then pour the tadka directly over the chole and mix well.
Finish with fresh coriander and mix.
Your masaledar Chikkad Chole is ready. Serve hot with bread kulcha, bhature, or puri.

Special Chana Masala
Ingredients:
WHOLE SPICES
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 2 TBSP
CORIANDER SEEDS | धनिया 3 TBSP
BLACK PEPPERCORNS | साबुत काली मिर्च 1 TBSP
CLOVES | लौंग 5 NOS.
BAY LEAVES | तेज पत्ता 3 NOS.
CINNAMON STICK | दालचीनी 2 INCH
STAR ANISE | चकरी फूल 1 NO.
BLACK CARDAMOM | बड़ी इलायची 3 NOS. 
GREEN CARDAMOM | हरी इलायची 5-6 NOS.
MACE | जावित्री  1 NO.
SPICY RED CHILLIES | तीखी लाल मिर्च 4-5 NOS.
POMEGRANATE SEEDS | अनारदाना 3 TBSP
KASURI METHI | कसूरी मेथी 1 TBSP
FENUGREEK SEEDS | मेथी के बीज 1/4 TSP
SALT | नमक A PINCH
POWDERED SPICES
DRY MANGO POWDER | आमचूर पाउडर 1 TBSP
BLACK SALT | काला नमक 2 TSP
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1 TBSP

Method:
Set a pan on high heat, add the whole spices once the pan gets hot, lower the flame & dry roast until fragrant.
Once the aroma starts to release, add kasuri methi, methi seeds, salt into the pan, toast them as well along with other spices, after a minute transfer it in a plate and cool down to room temperature.
Transfer the cooled spices to a grinding jar, and add amchur powder, black salt & kashmiri red chilli powder, grind into a fine powder, you can also grind to a coarse powder as per your preference. 
Your special chickpeas masala is ready, store them in an airtight container in a cool and dry place

title: Mushroom in Pepper Garlic Sauce with Butter Onion Rice | मुह्सरूम ग्रेवी और बटर राइस | Sanjyot Keer
description:
This is one of my personal favourite meal combinations. The pepper garlic sauce is rich, smooth, silky, peppery, and garlicky, the same kind of flavour-packed sauce that we all love poured over a good sizzler.

Paired with delicious butter and brown onion rice, this combination turns out incredibly delicious and satisfying. You all have always shown so much love to my meal-combo recipes, and this one is incredible too. The full written recipe is mentioned below in the description, do give it a try. Happy Cooking! ❤️

Veg Sizzler Recipe: https://youtu.be/vkplVK6MszA?si=1eZL0xfpuPzbpB9d

Amazon: https://amzn.in/d/0dgVvpr5

Full written recipe - Mushrooms in Pepper Garlic Sauce with Butter Onion Rice

Prep Time: 15–20 minutes 
Cooking Time: 35-40 minutes 
Serving: 3-4 people

Ingredients:
Pepper Garlic Sauce
BUTTER | मक्खन 35 GRAMS
REFINED FLOUR | मैदा 35 GRAMS
WATER | पानी 500 ML
VEG STOCK CUBE | वेज स्टॉक क्यूब 1 NO.
CELERY | सेलरी 1 TBSP 
GREEN CHILLI SAUCE | ग्रीन चिली सॉस 1 TBSP
GARLIC PASTE | लहसुन की पेस्ट 1 TBSP
DARK SOY SAUCE | डार्क सॉय सॉस 1 TBSP
SUGAR | शक्कर 1.5 TBSP
BLACK PEPPER | काली मिर्च 1 TSP 
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
Mushrooms
BUTTON MUSHROOMS | बटन मशरूम 350 GMS 
BUTTER | मक्खन 2 TBSP
GARLIC | लहसुन 2 TBSP 
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS. 
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)

Method:
To make the pepper garlic sauce, take a deep pan and add butter. Melt it on low flame, then continue cooking it on the lowest flame until it turns brown and nutty. Do not rush this on high heat, the low flame is what gives it that nuttiness without burning.
Add the refined flour and stir it in well. Cook the flour on low flame for 4–5 minutes, stirring continuously, until it is cooked through and smells nutty.
Now begin adding water gradually, stirring constantly as you pour so no lumps form. Once it is smooth and lump-free, add the remaining water and stir well.
If lumps end up forming in the sauce, you can just strain the sauce once using a strainer to get rid of the lumps and make the sauce completely smooth.
To flavour the sauce, add the crushed stock cube, chopped celery, green chilli sauce, garlic paste, dark soy sauce, sugar, freshly crushed black pepper, and salt. Mix well and let the sauce simmer for 7–8 minutes until it thickens to a spoon-coating consistency. Taste and adjust if needed and your delicious garlic pepper sauce is ready, set it aside until you saute the mushrooms.
For the mushrooms, if a mushroom is large, trim the stem slightly and cut it into quarters. If it is small, just trim the stem and halve it. Do not cut them too small as mushrooms shrink significantly as they cook.
Heat a frying pan well on high flame. Add butter and let it melt and get hot. Add chopped garlic and finely chopped green chillies and sauté for a few seconds.
Add the mushrooms and cook on high flame until they get some colour on them.
Once the mushrooms are coloured, place the sauce back on the flame and add the mushrooms directly into it. Mix well and cook together for 2–3 minutes.
Finish with fresh coriander and mix through. Your Mushroom in Pepper Garlic Sauce is ready.

Butter Onion Rice
Ingredients:
ONION | प्याज़ 2 MEDIUM SIZED 
BUTTER | मक्खन 2–3 TBSP
OIL | तेल 1 TSP
SALT | नमक A PINCH
COOKED RICE | पके हुए चावल 4 CUPS
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
SALT | नमक A PINCH 
Method:
Cut the onions in half and cut them into thick slices, then roughly separate the layers.
Heat a frying pan on high flame. Add butter and oil and let the butter get hot. Add the onion slices along with a pinch of salt and cook them well, stirring regularly, until they are lightly browned and caramelised.
Add the cooked rice, fresh coriander, and a pinch of salt. Toss everything together well so the rice gets coated evenly with the butter and onions. Taste and adjust salt.
Your Butter Onion Rice is ready. Serve it alongside the Mushroom in Pepper Garlic Sauce.

title: Paneer Malai Kofta recipe WITHOUT Cream, Butter & Oil! बिना तेल के पनीर मलाई कोफ्ता | Sanjyot Keer
description:
Think Malai Kofta needs loads of cream, butter and oil to taste incredible? Think again.

This No Cream, No Butter, No Oil Malai Kofta is every bit as rich, creamy and indulgent as the restaurant version, but made in a much smarter way at home.

Instead of relying on heavy cream and butter, I’m using California walnuts to create a silky, velvety smooth gravy that’s packed with flavour while also adding natural richness. The paneer koftas are air-fried instead of deep-fried and are made with California walnuts for added nuttiness, texture and goodness.

There are so many recipes that we enjoy regularly which can easily be made lighter without compromising on taste. This is one of my favourite examples.

I use California walnuts quite regularly in my cooking because they’re incredibly versatile. From gravies, pestos and chutneys to salads, smoothies and desserts, they blend beautifully into everyday recipes while adding nutrition and amazing flavour.

Give this recipe a try and let me know what you think. I have a feeling this version might become your new favourite Malai Kofta!

If you enjoy the recipe, don't forget to Like, Share and Subscribe to Your Food Lab for many more recipes like this.

title: PAHADI-STYLE Aloo Ke Gutke | UNIQUE Aloo Sabzi Recipe | झटपट आसान पहाड़ी आलू के गुटके | Sanjyot Keer
description:
Pahadi-style Aloo Ke Gutke, one of Uttarakhand’s most beloved recipes, known for its bold, rustic flavour and comforting simplicity. Made with a freshly ground spice paste and a simple tempering, this dish is incredibly easy to put together yet tastes absolutely delicious.

Serve it with some hot puris and raita, and you’ve got a meal that’s ultimate comfort on a plate. You seriously need to try this one! The full written recipe is mentioned below in the description. Happy Cooking! ❤️✨

A sleek stainless steel masala dani that keeps your everyday spices fresh, organized, and within easy reach ❤️ 

Flipkart - https://www.flipkart.com/curaa-spice-set-stainless-steel/p/itm349fa807a85dc 

Full written recipe - Pahadi-Style Aloo ke Gutke
Prep Time: 10 minutes 
Cooking Time: 20–25 minutes 
Serving: 2-3 people

Ingredients:
For Paste
FRESH CORIANDER STEMS | हरे धनिये की डंठल A LARGE HANDFUL (ROUGHLY CUT)
GINGER | अदरक 2 INCH (ROUGHLY CUT)
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 3 NOS. (TOPS TRIMMED, ROUGHLY CUT)
CORIANDER SEEDS | साबुत धनिया 1 TBSP
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1 TSP
Aloo
MUSTARD OIL | सरसों का तेल 2–3 TBSP
BOILED POTATO | उबले आलू 4 MEDIUM SIZED
Tempering
MUSTARD OIL | सरसों का तेल 1 TBSP
JAKHYA | जख्या 1/2 TSP
ASAFOETIDA | हींग 1/4 TSP
DRY RED CHILLI | सूखी लाल मिर्च 2 NOS.
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/4 TSP
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1/2 TSP
CUMIN POWDER | जीरा पाउडर 1/2 TSP
HOT WATER | गरम पानी A SPLASH
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)

Method:
To make the paste, rim the tops of the green chillies and roughly cut them. Roughly cut the ginger and coriander stems as well. 
Add everything into a mortar and pestle along with coriander seeds and cumin seeds. Pound into a coarse, textured paste and set it aside.
Cut the boiled potatoes in half and then into small dices. Make sure the potatoes are cold before cutting, this helps them hold their shape. And try to use pahadi potatoes or potatoes that have less sugar content. 
Take a cast iron kadhai and add mustard oil. Heat it until it smokes properly. Once it is smoking, add the diced potatoes and fry on medium-high flame, stirring as you go, until they pick up a light golden colour on the outside. This step is optional but gives the potatoes a much better texture. Once done, transfer them to a bowl and set aside.
In the same kadhai, add fresh mustard oil and heat it well. Add jakhiya, hing, and dry red chillies and let them crackle.
Add the prepared coarse paste and stir it in. Add turmeric, Kashmiri red chilli powder, and cumin powder along with a splash of hot water. 
Stir well and cook the masala well until the raw smell fades and the oil starts separating again.
Add salt, stir, then add the fried potatoes. Mix well so every piece is coated with the tadka, and cook over medium high flame until the potatoes get coated with the masala.
Your simple yet incredibly flavourful Aloo ke Gutke is ready. Serve hot with naachni roti, puri with some cucumber raita or you can also serve it as a side with dal chawal.

title: Unique Sindhi BESAN KI SABZI | MUST-TRY Recipe | सिंधी बेसन टिक्की की सब्ज़ी | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
Sindhi-style Besan Ki Sabzi ✨ A unique and much-loved staple in many Sindhi households. Perfectly fried, flavourful besan tikkis are cooked in a simple dry onion-tomato gravy with everyday spices, creating a sabzi that’s incredibly comforting and satisfying.

It’s one of those humble recipes that proves simple ingredients can make something truly special. Enjoy it with roti or sindhi dodo, either way, it tastes delicious. The full written recipe is mentioned below in the description. Do give it a try. Happy Cooking! ❤️

Crafted with care. Rooted in tradition. Designed to last 🤎 Our Wooden Masala Dani blends timeless craftsmanship with everyday functionality, keeping your spices fresh and your kitchen elegant.
Add a touch of heritage to your cooking ❤️

🛒 Available now:
Website: https://curaahome.com/products/curaa-wooden-masala-dani
Amazon: https://amzn.in/d/8ixtD2f

Full written recipe - Seyal Besan ki Sabzi

Prep Time: 10-15 minutes 
Cooking Time: 25–30 minutes 
Serving: 3-4 people

Ingredients:

Besan Tikki
BESAN | बेसन 100 GRAMS
ONION | प्याज़ 1 SMALL SIZED (CHOPPED)
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 1 NO (CHOPPED) 
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर A PINCH
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1/2 TSP
BAKING SODA | बेकिंग सोडा 1/8 TSP
OIL | तेल 2 TBSP
ANARDANA | अनारदाना 1 TSP
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
WATER | पानी 1–2 TBSP
OIL | तेल AS REQUIRED (FOR SHALLOW FRYING)

Seyal Masala
OIL | तेल 1–2 TBSP
ASAFOETIDA | हींग A PINCH
ONION | प्याज़ 3 MEDIUM SIZED(CHOPPED)
TOMATO | टमाटर 2 MEDIUM SIZED(CHOPPED) 
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 1–2 NOS(CHOPPED) 
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/4 TSP
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1 TSP
CORIANDER POWDER | धनिया पाउडर 1 TSP
ROASTED CUMIN POWDER | भुना जीरा पाउडर 1 TSP
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)

Method:
Besan Tikki
Start by making the tikki dough. Take a large bowl and add besan, chopped onion, green chilli, salt, turmeric, Kashmiri red chilli powder, baking soda, oil, anardana, and fresh coriander. Mix everything together well.
Add water gradually, a splash at a time, and knead into a tight dough. It should be firm and hold its shape; don't add too much water.
Take a small portion of dough and shape each one into a round, flat tikki. Press them gently so they are even in thickness.
To fry, heat oil in a pan to around 160–165°C. Once the oil is at temperature, carefully add the tikkis in and fry on medium-low flame. Keeping the flame medium-low is important here so the besan cooks through from the inside while the outside turns light golden brown. After 4–5 minutes you will see them puff up and colour nicely.
Once light golden, transfer them to a strainer to drain.

Seyal Masala
Heat a kadhai and add oil. Once hot, add hing and let it sizzle briefly. Add the chopped onions, stir, and cook on high flame until they soften and turn pink.
Add the chopped tomatoes, green chillies, salt, turmeric, Kashmiri red chilli powder, coriander powder, and roasted cumin powder. Mix well and cook the masala for 2–3 minutes until onion and tomatoes release the water.
Gently pace the fried tikkis into the masala. Cover the kadhai, lower the flame completely, and let everything steam together for 8-10 minutes. This is what allows the tikkis to absorb all the masala and soften from inside.
Once done, open the lid and mix gently, coating each tikki well with the masala. Taste and adjust salt (if required). Add fresh coriander and mix lightly.
Your Sindhi-style Seyal Besan ki Sabzi is ready. Serve hot with roti/phulka or sindhi dodo.

title: GUJARATI Green Moong Dal | INSTANT COOKER Recipe | गुजराती हरी मूंग दाल की सब्ज़ी | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
Gujarati-style Green Moong Dal, garlicky, tangy, and finished with just a touch of sweetness to bring everything together beautifully. Made without any soaking, this is a wonderfully simple recipe that comes together with just a pressure cooker and a quick tempering.

Light, comforting, and packed with flavour, it’s the kind of everyday meal you’ll keep coming back to. The best way to enjoy it is with some rice and Gujarati kadhi. The full written recipe is mentioned below in the description, do give it a try. Happy Cooking! ❤️

A sleek stainless steel masala dani that keeps your everyday spices fresh, organized, and within easy reach ❤️ 

Amazon: https://amzn.in/d/0eL0ESDW
Website: https://curaahome.com/products/curaa-steel-masala-dani

Gujarati Green Moong Dal

Prep Time: 5-10 minutes 
Cooking Time: 20-25 minutes 
Serving: 3–4 people

Ingredients:
Pressure Cooking Moong
WHOLE GREEN MOONG | साबुत हरी मूंग 1 CUP
TOMATO | टमाटर 2 MEDIUM SIZED 
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED (1 INCH ABOVE MOONG)
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/4 TSP
OIL | तेल 1 TSP
SALT | नमक A LARGE PINCH

Garlic & Chilli Paste (for tempering)
GARLIC | लहसुन 8–10 CLOVES
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2–3 NOS. (ROUGHLY CUT)
SALT | नमक A PINCH
GARLIC | लहसुन 7–8 CLOVES

Tempering & Cooking
GROUNDNUT OIL | मूंगफली का तेल 2–3 TBSP
MUSTARD SEEDS | राई 1/4 TSP
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1/4 TSP
ASAFOETIDA | हींग 1/4 TSP
DRY RED CHILLI | सूखी लाल मिर्च 2 NOS.
CURRY LEAVES | कड़ी पत्ता 10–12 NOS.
GARLIC | लहसुन 7–8 CLOVES 
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/4 TSP
RED CHILLI POWDER | लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1/2 TSP
CORIANDER POWDER | धनिया पाउडर 1 TSP
CUMIN POWDER | जीरा पाउडर 1/2 TSP
HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED
SUGAR | शक्कर 1 TBSP (OR AS REQUIRED)
LEMON JUICE | नींबू का रस OF 1/2 LEMON
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)

Method:
Add whole green moong to a strainer and rinse with water once or twice. No soaking needed for this recipe.
Transfer the washed moong to a pressure cooker. Cut a cross on the top of each tomato, remove the eye, and add them in whole, no need to chop them. 
Add water until it sits about an inch above the moong. Add salt, turmeric, and oil. Close the lid and cook on medium flame for 4–5 whistles.
Once done, switch off the flame and let the cooker depressurise naturally. When it does, open the lid and check, the moong should be fully cooked and the tomatoes completely soft.
Peel the tomato skins off, they will come away easily at this stage. Use a fork to mash the tomatoes directly into the moong. Your base is ready. Set this aside.
Now, in a mortar and pestle, add garlic, roughly cut green chillies, and a pinch of salt. Pound into a coarse paste and set aside.
Now take 7-8 more garlic cloves and add them to a chopper and roughly chop them as well.
Heat a kadhai on high flame until properly hot. Add groundnut oil and let it heat well. Add mustard seeds, cumin seeds, hing, dry red chillies, and curry leaves and let them crackle.
Add the roughly chopped garlic and sauté briefly until lightly golden then add the coarse paste and stir for a few seconds until the raw smell disappears, do not cook for a long time.
Lower the flame completely. Add turmeric, red chilli powder, coriander powder, and cumin powder. Give it a light stir, then add the cooked moong and mix well so everything comes together with the tempering.
Add hot water to adjust the consistency, it should be slightly runny, not too thick, not too watery. Bring it to a gentle simmer.
Taste and adjust salt. Add sugar and lemon juice for the sweet and tangy balance that is the character of this dish. Mix well and taste again.
Finish with fresh coriander and mix well.
Your comforting, garlicky, tangy-sweet Gujarati Rassawala Mag is ready. Serve hot with rice and gujarati kadhi.

title: Cooking with MADHURI DIXIT, TRIPTII DIMRI & DHARNA in the YFL Kitchen | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
This ones special! ♥️

The legendary Madhuri Dixit ma'am is in the YFL kitchen and honestly it is a dream come true! I've admired her ever since I was a kid. She is one of India's greatest actors and performers of all time, and cooking along with her in my kitchen is a moment I'll always cherish.

Joining her are Triptii Dimri and Dharna Durga, and what fun we have had! 

Triptii is such a true foodie, I was surprised to see her love for 'Desi Ghee' and her love for food is completely real. Her philosophy is simple: eat what you love and burn it all in the gym later. 😂

And then there's Dharna. If you've ever watched her content, you'll know exactly what I'm talking about. The moment she walks into a room, the energy changes. There are laughs every few minutes and to see her perform on the big screen is a treat to watch! 

We cooked some delicious Moringa Sattu Parathas, shared food memories, talked about favourite comfort foods, travel, cooking and a lot more.

title: INSTANT & HEALHY Dahi Toast Sandwich Recipe | नाश्ते के लिए आसान दही टोस्ट सैंडविच | Sanjyot Keer
description:
Dahi Wala Toast Sandwich 🥪✨ A super easy, no-guilt breakfast made with a creamy hung curd filling loaded with veggies and toasted to perfection. It’s light, satisfying, and packed with flavour without feeling heavy.
The filling is incredibly versatile too, you ca enjoy it as a dip, use it as a spread, or customise it with your favourite veggies and seasonings. I’ve shared the easiest method along with all the tips to get it just right. The full recipe is mentioned below in the description, do give it a try. Happy Cooking! ❤️

Power meets precision in every blend!
Say hello to the BLP 2.0, your new kitchen superstar that makes cooking easier, faster, and way more fun 🥰🚀

Happy shopping! 🛍️ 
🛒 Buy on Amazon: https://amzn.in/d/00oVb9g 

Creamy Dahi Toast Sandwich
Prep Time: 10-15 minutes 
Cooking Time: 5 minutes 
Serving: 3-4 toast sandwiches
Ingredients:

For Hung Curd
CURD | दही 500 GRAMS
Filling
BELL PEPPER | शिमला मिर्च 1 MEDIUM SIZED.
SPRING ONION GREENS | हरे प्याज के पत्ते 1 NO.
SWEET CORN | स्वीट कॉर्न 1/3 CUP (BOILED)
OREGANO | ओरेगेनो 1/2 TSP
RED CHILLI FLAKES | रेड चिल्ली फलैक्स 1/2 TSP
SALT | नमक TO TASTE

For Assembly
BREAD | ब्रेड AS REQUIRED
BUTTER | मक्खन AS REQUIRED
SANDWICH MASALA | सैंडविच मसाला TO TASTE
Method:
Start by making the hung curd. Place a strainer over a large bowl, make sure there is a gap between the strainer and the base of the bowl so the whey can drain freely. Line the strainer with a muslin cloth.
Add the curd and gently squeeze and press it so the whey drains out. You will see the whey (liquid from the curd) getting collected in the bowl below, you can use it later when kneading dough for rotis or in dal and other curries.
Once a sufficient amount of water has been squeezed out, open the muslin cloth and mix the curd once and repeat the process of squeezing.
You will end up with a thick, cream-cheese-like hung curd. Transfer it to a bowl and set it aside.
Take one wedge of each colour of bellpepper, roughly cut them, and transfer to a chopper. Pulse until finely chopped. You can also just use 1 whole bell-pepper of any colour.
Take only the green part of the spring onion and chop it as well.
Add the chopped bellpeppers, spring onion greens, boiled corn, oregano, red chilli flakes, and salt to the hung curd. Mix everything together well. The creamy dahi filling for the sandwich is ready.
Place a pan on medium to medium-low flame and let it heat up. While it heats, assemble the sandwich.
Butter the bread slices generously on one side. Spread the chutney evenly over the buttered side.
Add a generous spoonful of filling and spread it out evenly across the bread. Sprinkle sandwich masala on top. Keep in mind the masala already has salt in it, so you do not need extra and now place another slice on top.
Once the pan is hot, apply a little butter directly on the pan and place the sandwich in. Press it down gently with a plate so it toasts evenly.
Once the bottom side is beautifully golden and crispy, apply butter on the top side and flip the sandwich over. Toast the second side the same way until it is evenly golden.
Your crispy, creamy, golden Dahi Sandwich is ready. Serve it with the chutney alongside.
SandwichChutney
Ingredients:
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया 2 CUPS
GREEN CHILLIES | हरी मिर्च 7–8 NOS.
GARLIC | लहसुन 7–8 CLOVES
CURRY LEAVES | कड़ी पत्ता 8–10 NOS.
SANDWICH MASALA | सैंडविच मसाला A LARGE PINCH
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
BREAD | ब्रेड 1 SLICE
ICE CUBE | बर्फ 1 NO.
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED
Method:
In a mixer jar, add fresh coriander, green chillies, garlic, curry leaves, sandwich masala, salt, a slice of bread, an ice cube, and a little water. 
Grind into a smooth, fine chutney. The bread helps give it body and keep the chutney thick so that it does not make the bread of the sandwich soggy.
Delicious and perfect, street style Sandwich Chutney is ready.

title: INSTANT Mirch Ka Achaar | Punjabi Home-Style Pickle Recipe | इंस्टेंट मिर्च का अचार | Sanjyot Keer
description:
This Instant Punjabi Mirch Ka Achaar is my mom’s and family’s special pickle recipe, one that’s been loved at home for years. ✨ 
It’s super easy to make, packed with spicy, tangy flavours, and has that beautiful aroma of saunf that makes it truly irresistible.

I personally love having it with laccha paratha, but it pairs wonderfully with almost any meal and instantly elevates the experience. 

The full written recipe is mentioned below in the description, do give it a try. Happy Cooking! ♥️

Our BlendLab - Designed to make everyday kitchen tasks faster, easier, and more enjoyable
🛒 Buy on Amazon: https://amzn.in/d/1X1FhAu

Punjabi Style Mirchi ka Achaar

Prep Time: 20–25 minutes 
Serving: 1 jar (250 gms 
Mirchi)

Ingredients:
Mirchi
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 250 GRAMS (LIGHT GREEN VARIETY)
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED
SALT | नमक A LARGE PINCH 
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर ¼ TSP  

Achaar Masala
MUSTARD SEEDS | राई 2 TSP / 7.5 GRAMS
FENNEL SEEDS | सौंफ 2 TBSP / 15 GRAMS
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 2 TBSP / 15 GRAMS
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1 TBSP / 7.5 GRAMS
SALT | नमक 2 TBSP / 37.5 GRAMS
METHI KURIA | मेथी कुरिअ 1 TBSP + 1 TSP / 15 GRAMS
MUSTARD OIL | सरसों का तेल 150 ML

Tempering
MUSTARD OIL | सरसों का तेल 2 TBSP
ASAFOETIDA | हींग 1/4 TSP

For Preserving
MUSTARD OIL | सरसों का तेल 25-50 ML

Method:
Start by washing the mirchi. Take a bowl, add all the mirchi, and pour in enough water to cover them. Add a large pinch of salt and a small pinch of turmeric and clean them well. Strain and shake off the excess water.
Spread a clean, dry cloth on a large tray or parat and lay all the mirchi out on it. Pat them dry completely, there should be no moisture left on them at all.You can let them sun dry or you can keep them under a fan for 10-15 minutes.  Any moisture will shorten the shelf life of the achaar, so take your time with this step.
Once fully dry, cut the mirchi. Remove the top of each mirchi, cut it in half lengthwise, and then halve it again. If your hands are sensitive, rub a little oil on them before you start, or wear gloves.
Transfer all the cut mirchi into a clean, completely dry bowl. Make sure every utensil and bowl you use from this point has no moisture in it.
In a spice grinder, add the mustard seeds and grind them finely. Transfer to a clean bowl.
Now add the fennel seeds to the grinder and grind them coarsely, you want some pieces fully crushed and some still a little coarse. This texture is what makes the achaar pleasant to eat and gives you bites of saunf through it.
To the bowl, add the ground mustard, coarsely ground fennel, Kashmiri red chilli powder, turmeric, salt, methi kuria, and mustard oil. Mix everything together well until you have a uniform achaar masala.
If you cannot find methi kuria, lightly crush whole methi dana and use that instead.
Add this masala to the cut mirchi and mix well so every piece is evenly coated.
Take a pan and heat mustard oil in it until it is properly hot. Add hing and let it sizzle briefly, then pour this directly over the masala-coated mirchi. Mix well immediately so the tempering coats everything evenly.
Transfer the achaar into a clean, dry, airtight glass jar. Pour mustard oil over the top to cover the achaar completely. Give it one final mix.
Your spicy, tangy Mirchi ka Achaar is ready. Serve this with lachha paratha or alongside any meal.

title: Instant White Rava Dhokla | Quick and Easy Breakfast Recipe | इंस्टेंट रावा ढोकला | Sanjyot Keer
description:
Instant Rava Dhokla ✨ A super easy breakfast recipe made with jada rava and dahi, perfect for days when you want something quick, wholesome, and delicious. Soft, spongy, and made without any lengthy fermentation, this one comes together effortlessly.

I’ve shared all the tips and tricks to help you get it just right, along with a special green chutney that pairs beautifully with it. The full written recipe is mentioned below in the description, do give it a try. Happy Cooking! ❤️

Our BlendLab - Designed to make everyday kitchen tasks faster, easier, and more enjoyable! 
Plus, for a limited time, you’ll get ChopLab Lite absolutely FREE on our website!

🌐 Curaa Website: https://curaahome.com/products/curaa-blendlab 

Instant White Rawa Dhokla 

Prep Time: 5-10 Minutes (10-15 Minutes of resting)
Cooking Time: 12–15 Minutes 
Serving: 3–4 people

Ingredients:
Batter
COARSE RAWA | जड़ा राव  1 CUP
SOUR CURD | खट्टा दही 1 CUP
WATER | पानी 1/2 CUP + 1 to 2 TBSP
GINGER & GREEN CHILLI PASTE | अदरक और हरी मिर्च की पेस्ट 1 TSP
SUGAR | शक्कर 1/2 TSP
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
GROUNDNUT OIL | भुनी हुई मूंगफली 1 TSP 
ENO | ईनो 1 TSP
Topping
FRESHLY CRACKED BLACK PEPPER | ताज़ी काली मिर्च TO TASTE
RED CHILLI POWDER | लाल मिर्च पाउडर A PINCH

Method:
Start by making the batter. Take a large bowl and add coarse rawa and sour curd in equal quantities. Use coarse rawa here, the fine variety won't give you the same texture. Try to use sour curd specifically, as it is what replicates the fermented flavour in the batter.
Add water, ginger and green chilli paste, sugar, and salt. Whisk everything together until you get a smooth, flowing batter. Cover and let it rest for 10–15 minutes.
While the batter rests, you can make the chutney. 
Now get your steamer ready. Fill it with water, cover it, and set it on the gas to heat up. Always pre-heat the steamer before putting the dhokla in, this is what helps it rise properly and gives it a better texture.
Now check the batter. You'll notice it has thickened as it rested. Add water, a little at a time, and mix until you get a smooth pouring consistency again. About 1–2 tbsp should be enough.
Grease the steamer tray well with groundnut oil, cover both the base and the sides. 
Now add groundnut oil and eno to the batter and mix immediately and thoroughly. You will see the batter begin to aerate and rise slightly. Do not let it sit after this, pour it straight into the greased tray and spread it out evenly. Keep the thickness moderate; you don't want it too thick.
Sprinkle freshly cracked black pepper and red chilli powder evenly over the top.
Place the tray into the pre-heated steamer and steam for 12–15 minutes. To check, insert a knife , if it comes out clean, the dhokla is done.
Take the tray out and let the dhokla cool slightly before cutting. This helps it set and makes it easier to slice cleanly.
Add lemon juice to the chutney and mix just before serving.
Your soft, spongy Instant White Rawa Dhokla is ready. Serve it with the chutney alongside.

Green Chutney
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया 1 CUP (PACKED)
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 4–5 NOS.
GINGER | अदरक 1 INCH
ROASTED PEANUTS | भुनी हुई मूंगफली 1 TBSP
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1/4 TSP
CURD | दही 3 TBSP
SUGAR | शक्कर 1 TBSP
LEMON JUICE | नींबू का रस OF 1/2 LEMON
ICE CUBE | बर्फ 1 NO.
SALT | नमक TO TASTE

Method:
In a mixer jar, add fresh coriander, green chillies, ginger, roasted peanuts, cumin seeds, curd, sugar, ice cube, and salt. 
Grind into a smooth, fine chutney. 
Taste and adjust. Add lemon juice just before serving.
Your special green chutney is ready, it pairs perfectly with any Gujarati Snacks

title: Maharashtrian Unique-Style BHINDI Recipe | Shengdanyachi Bhendi | शिंदान्याची भिंडी | Sanjyot Keer
description:
Try this Unique Maharashtrian Style BHINDI recipe!!! 

Shengdanyachi Bhendi is a beautiful way to prepare bhindi, the peanuts add a crunchy nutty element which goes so well with Bhindi and it is so easy to prepare! 

One of my favourite ways to cook Bhindi, I totally recommend! Happy Cooking ❤️

Links of Bhindi Recipe:1.) https://youtu.be/rSfhuO5hvX0?si=Ele5unviL29Fa__0
2.) https://youtu.be/3SCrf1FW70w?si=oSqVs2_hiy-cwUvu
3.) https://youtu.be/0_g_8FPr3Ag?si=6-GpW6aSneyw07_V
4.) https://youtu.be/de1ppj9L4Hk?si=8dQtVf5gGHoYA2De 

Maharashtrian Style Shengdanyachi Bhindi
Prep Time: 10–15 minutes 
Cooking Time: 12–15 minutes 
Serving: 3–4
Ingredients:
Peanut Masala
PEANUTS | मूंगफली 3 TBSP
DRY COCONUT | सूखा नारियल 2–3 TBSP 
GARLIC | लहसुन 5–6 CLOVES
GINGER | अदरक 1 INCH 
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 1 NO. 
CURRY LEAVES | कड़ी पत्ता 10–12 NOS.
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1/2 TSP
FENNEL SEEDS | सौंफ 1/2 TSP
Bhindi
BHINDI | भिंडी 250–300 GMS 
Cooking
PEANUT OIL | मूंगफली का तेल 1–1.5 TBSP
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/4 TSP
SPICY RED CHILLI POWDER | तीखी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1/2 TSP
OIL | तेल 1 TBSP
SALT | नमक A PINCH
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर A PINCH
LEMON JUICE | नींबू का रस OF 1/2 LEMON
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
Finish
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
Method:
Start by roasting the peanuts. Heat a pan on high flame, once hot reduce the flame  to low and add the peanuts. Dry roast slowly until they are evenly golden brown. Transfer to a bowl and let them cool completely. 
Once cool, add them to a mixer jar along with grated dry coconut, garlic, ginger, green chilli, curry leaves, cumin seeds, and fennel seeds. Grind into a coarse paste  not too fine, you want some texture. The masala is ready.
Now for the bhindi, pick medium sized ones. Wash them well, then wipe completely dry and let them air dry under a fan for a few minutes before cutting. This step is important, wet bhindi turns slimy when cooked. Once dry, trim the tops and cut into dices.
Heat a kadhai on high flame and add peanut oil. Once hot, add the prepared peanut masala along with salt, turmeric powder, and spicy red chilli powder. Mix well and cook on medium flame for 2–3 minutes until the masala is well cooked and fragrant. Transfer to a bowl and set aside.
In the same kadhai, add a little more oil and heat it. Add the cut bhindi along with a pinch of salt and a pinch of turmeric. Sauté on high flame for just 1 minute, then cover and cook on low flame. After a while you open and stir once,Stirring too much breaks the texture of the bhindi. Don’t cover again, cook with the open lid for 1–2 more minutes so the bhindi gets a little more texture and any remaining stickiness dries out.
Add lemon juice, this also helps cut down the stickiness of the bhindi. Add the prepared peanut masala and fresh coriander. Mix gently on the lowest flame and then switch off. Taste and adjust salt. Finish with fresh coriander.
Our flavourful, nutty Maharashtrian Style Shengdanyachi Bhindi is ready. Serve this along with some phulka or varan bhat along with some salad.

title: Quick & Easy PAPAD KI SABJI | RAJASTHANI Home-Style Recipe | राजशतानी पापड़ की सब्ज़ी | Sanjyot Keer
description:
Rajasthani Papad Ki Sabji ✨ A quick and unique sabji that comes together in just a few minutes, yet tastes absolutely incredible. It’s perfect for days when you’re bored of the usual everyday sabzis and want to make something comforting but different.

You can enjoy it with roti, rice, or paratha, and the best part is how simple and flavourful it is. The full written recipe is mentioned below in the description. Happy Cooking! ❤️🍛

A sleek stainless steel masala dani that keeps your everyday spices fresh, organized, and within easy reach ❤️ 

Shop Now:
Amazon: https://amzn.in/d/0eL0ESDW
Website: https://curaahome.com/products/curaa-steel-masala-dani

Papad Ki Sabji
Prep Time: 5–10 minutes 
Cooking Time: 20–25 minutes 
Serving: 3–4
Ingredients:
Prep
ONION | प्याज़ 1 MEDIUM SIZED
GINGER | अदरक 1 INCH
GARLIC | लहसुन 3–4 CLOVES
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS.
Papad
URAD DAL PAPAD | उड़द दाल पापड़ 4–5 NOS.
GHEE | घी AS REQUIRED
Spice Mix
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1/2 TSP
FENNEL SEEDS | सौंफ 1/2 TSP
CORIANDER SEEDS | धनिया 1/2 TSP
Curd & Spices
CURD | दही 2 CUPS
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/2 TSP
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1 TBSP
CORIANDER POWDER | धनिया पाउडर 1 TSP
GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला 1/4 TSP
Cooking
GHEE | घी 2 TBSP
CRUSHED SPICES | कुटे हुए मसाले AS PREPARED
DRY RED CHILLI | सूखी लाल मिर्च 1 NO.
HING | हींग 1/4 TSP
KASURI METHI | कसूरी मेथी 1 TSP
ONION | प्याज़ 1 MEDIUM SIZED AS PREPARED
SPICED CURD | मसाला दही AS PREPARED
HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED
SALT | नमक IF REQUIRED
SUGAR | शक्कर A PINCH
Finish
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL
Method:
Start by roughly chopping the onion, ginger, garlic, and green chillies and add them into a chopper and finely chop them. 
Heat a tawa and add a little ghee. Place the papads on it and press down evenly from all sides, roasting until done. 
Transfer to a board and roast the remaining papads the same way. Once all are done, roughly break them into pieces.
In a mortar and pestle, coarsely crush the cumin seeds, fennel seeds, and coriander seeds, you want them crushed but not powdered.
In a bowl, add the curd along with salt, turmeric powder, Kashmiri red chilli powder, coriander powder, and garam masala. Whisk everything together well until completely smooth with no lumps.
Now heat a kadhai on high flame and add ghee. Once the ghee is hot, reduce the flame to low and add the crushed spices, dry red chilli, hing, and kasuri methi. Let them crackle briefly. Add the chopped onion mixture and cook, stirring, until the onions turn light golden brown.
Once the onions and masala are well cooked, add the spiced curd. Keep the flame low, this prevents the curd from splitting. 
Stir continuously and gradually increase the flame. Cook until the ghee separates, which will take about 6–8 minutes.
Add hot water to reach your preferred consistency, this sabji is best when it is slightly loose and coating, not too thick. Taste and adjust salt. Add a pinch of sugar and mix well.
Add the broken papad pieces, mix gently, and switch off the flame immediately. The papads will slowly soak up the gravy and soften slightly as they sit, that is exactly what you want. Finish with fresh coriander and mix well.
Our Quick and Easy, flavourful Papad Ki Sabji is ready. You can serve this along with your choice of bread or rice along with some garlic chutney and salad.

BLACK PEPPER | काली मिर्च TO TASTE

Method:

Start by finely chopping the onion and green chillies together in a chopper.
Take a small pan that you can serve the shakshuka in directly, make sure it has a lid.
Heat ghee in it and once hot, add the chopped onions and green chillies. Cook on high flame until they turn light golden brown. 
Once they reach that colour, reduce the flame to low. Roughly cut the tomato and chop it finely in the same chopper.
Add the finely chopped tomato along with salt, turmeric powder, Kashmiri red chilli powder, coriander powder, and garam masala. Mix well and cook the masala and tomatoes through, adding a splash of hot water in between if needed. 
Once everything is well cooked, add fresh cream and mix lightly on low flame, do not cook it too much, just bring it together. Taste and adjust salt.
Spread the masala evenly across the base of the pan. Using a spoon, make 4 small wells in the masala for the eggs. Switch off the flame. 
Crack each egg into a small bowl first and then gently slide it into a well, this way you have control and the yolk stays intact. Do this for all 4 eggs. Season with salt and freshly crushed black pepper. 
Cover with the lid and cook on the lowest flame possible.
Cook until the whites are just set, about 3-4 minutes on low flame. You can cook them a little longer if you prefer more well-done eggs, but the yolks are best when still slightly runny. Switch off the flame and serve immediately from the pan.
Our delicious,creamy, comforting Desi Shakshuka is ready. You can serve this along with sliced bread or any bread of your choice.

title: Kathal Masala Curry Recipe | EASY JACKFRUIT Sabji |आसान कटहल मसाला करी बनाने का तरीका | Sanjyot Keer
description:
Kathal Curry ✨ A super delicious curry made using raw jackfruit, with a rich, silky gravy and beautifully cooked kathal that absorbs all the flavours perfectly. I’ve shared all the tips to help you get the best texture and consistency at home.

It tastes incredible with roti, paratha, or even rice, making it a truly comforting meal. Do give this recipe a try, the detailed written recipe is mentioned below. Happy Cooking! ❤️

Say hello to the BLP 2.0, your new kitchen superstar that makes cooking easier, faster, and way more fun 🥰🚀

Happy shopping! 🛍️ 
🛒 Buy on Amazon: https://amzn.in/d/00oVb9g
Swiggy: https://www.swiggy.com/stores/instamart/item/5I2JAHGXDG 

Kathal Curry
Prep Time: 15–20 minutes 
Cooking Time: 35–40 minutes 
Serving: 4–5
Ingredients:
Pressure Cooking the Jackfruit
RAW JACKFRUIT | कच्चा कटहल 350–400 GMS
OIL | तेल 1 TSP
SALT | नमक 1/2 TSP
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/4 TSP
HOT WATER | गरम पानी TO COVER
Gravy Base  
MUSTARD OIL | सरसों का तेल 1–2 TBSP
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा ¼ TSP
ONION | प्याज़ 3 MEDIUM SIZED
GARLIC | लहसुन A HANDFUL
GINGER | अदरक 2 INCH
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS.
TOMATO | टमाटर 2 MEDIUM SIZED
FRESH CORIANDER STEMS | हरे धनिये की डंठल A HANDFUL
SALT | नमक A LARGE PINCH
HOT WATER | गरम पानी A SPLASH
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED (FOR BLENDING)
Frying the Jackfruit
OIL | तेल 3-4 TBSP
Cooking the Curry
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा A PINCH
CINNAMON | दालचीनी 1 INCH
GREEN CARDAMOM | हरी इलायची 3 NOS.
BLACK CARDAMOM | बड़ी इलायची 1 NO.
BAY LEAF | तेज पत्ता 1 NO.
MACE | जावित्री 1/2 NO.
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/4 TSP
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1 TBSP
SPICY RED CHILLI POWDER | तीखी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1 TSP
CORIANDER POWDER | धनिया पाउडर 1 TBSP
HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED
Finish
GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला A PINCH
ROASTED KASURI METHI POWDER | कसूरी मेथी पाउडर A PINCH
LEMON JUICE | नींबू का रस OF 1/2 LEMON
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A LARGE HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
Method:
Start by pressure cooking the jackfruit. Heat a pressure cooker on high flame and add oil. Once hot, add the cut jackfruit along with salt and turmeric powder. Stir on high flame for 1–2 minutes.
Adding oil while cooking reduces the stickiness of the Kathal.
Add hot water, cover, and cook on medium low flame for 1 whistle. Once done, switch off the flame and let the cooker depressurise naturally.
By the time the jackfruit cooks, prepare the gravy base. 
Roughly cut the onion and tomatoes then, heat mustard oil in a pan on high flame until it begins to smoke. Add cumin seeds and let them crackle. Add the roughly sliced onions and cook on medium high flame, stirring, until they turn golden brown. It will take 7-8 minutes.
Once the onions are well browned, add the garlic cloves, roughly cut ginger, green chillies, roughly cut tomatoes, coriander stems, and a large pinch of salt. 
Stir well and cook on high flame for 2–3 minutes until the tomatoes start to soften. 
Add a splash of hot water, cover, and cook on medium flame for 7–8 minutes until the tomatoes are completely mushy. Switch off the flame and let the mixture cool completely. 
Once cool, blend into a fine, smooth paste with a little water.
Once the cooker has depressurised, open the lid, the jackfruit should be perfectly cooked through. 
Drain it and transfer to a bowl. 
For a better texture, heat oil in a kadhai on high flame and fry the jackfruit pieces in batches for 3–5 minutes until they turn light golden brown. Drain and set aside.
In the same kadhai, remove a little excess oil if needed. Add cumin seeds, cinnamon, green cardamom, black cardamom, bay leaf, and mace and let them crackle. Add the prepared gravy paste along with salt, turmeric powder, Kashmiri red chilli powder, spicy red chilli powder, and coriander powder. 
Stir well and cook until the oil separates, this will take about 5–6 minutes. 
You will know it is ready when the masala stops sticking to the kadhai and the oil rises to the top.
Add hot water to bring the curry to your desired consistency and mix well. Cover and cook for 5–6 minutes. 
Once you take off the lid, you will see a beautiful rogan on top. Add the fried jackfruit pieces, mix gently, and cook for 2–3 more minutes. Taste and adjust salt.
Finish with a pinch of garam masala, roasted kasuri methi powder, lemon juice, and fresh coriander. Mix well.
Our delicious, home-style and velvety smooth Kathal Masala Curry is ready.

title: Cheese Chilli Garlic Chutney Pav Recipe | मसालेदार चीज़ चिली गार्लिक पाव | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
Cheese Chilli Garlic Chutney Pav 🌶️🧄🧀✨ An indulgent Mumbai-style snack loaded with amazing flavours and textures. A spicy lehsun ki chutney tossed in butter and coriander coats soft laadi pav, which is then stuffed with cheese, masala peanuts, onions, lemon juice, and a touch of meethi chutney.

Spicy, garlicky, tangy, sweet, crunchy, and cheesy all at once, this one turns out absolutely incredible. The detailed recipe is mentioned below, so do give it a try. Happy Cooking! ❤️

Website: https://curaahome.com/products/blendlabpro

Cheese Chilli Garlic Pav
Prep Time: 10–15 minutes (30 minutes soaking time)
Cooking Time: 10–15 minutes 
Serving: 6-8
Garlic Chutney
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च 20 NOS. (DESEEDED & SOAKED FOR 30–60 MINUTES)
WATER | पानी 3–4 TBSP
GARLIC | लहसुन 50 GRAMS
GROUNDNUT OIL | मूंगफली का तेल 3–4 TBSP
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा A PINCH
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
SUGAR | शक्कर A PINCH
PAV BHAJI MASALA | पाव भाजी मसाला A LARGE PINCH
BLACK SALT | काला नमक A LARGE PINCH
GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला A PINCH
VINEGAR | सिरका 1 TSP
Method:
Start by prepping the Kashmiri red chillies for soaking. Cut the tops off and slit each one down the middle so the seeds fall out easily. Once deseeded, soak them in hot water for 30 minutes to 1 hour until they are fully plump and soft.
Once soaked, transfer the chillies to a mixer jar. Add water and garlic and blend into a fine, smooth paste.
Heat a pan on high flame and add groundnut oil. 
Once hot, add a pinch of cumin seeds and let them crackle. 
Add the prepared paste along with salt and sugar. Stir well and cook on medium high flame until the chutney turns crumbly and the oil begins to separate. 
Cover and cook on medium low flame for 7–8 minutes. Take the lid off, you will see the oil has risen to the top and the chutney has a beautiful, deep texture.
Add pav bhaji masala, black salt, garam masala, and vinegar. Mix well and taste and adjust salt if needed. Let the chutney cool completely before transferring to an airtight jar for storage.
This chutney keeps well in the fridge and can be used across multiple recipes. Make a full batch and store it, it only gets better with time.
Cheese Chilli Garlic Pav
Prep Time: 5–10 minutes
Cooking Time: 5 minutes 
Serving: 1-2

Assembly
PAV | पाव 2 NOS.
BUTTER | मक्खन 1–2 TBSP
CHILLI GARLIC CHUTNEY | चिल्ली गार्लिक चटनी 1–2 TBSP 
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
CHEESE SLICE | चीज़ स्लाइस 2 NOS.
ONION | प्याज़ AS REQUIRED (CHOPPED)
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A SMALL HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
MASALA PEANUTS | मसाला मूंगफली A HANDFUL
LEMON JUICE | नींबू का रस AS REQUIRED
MEETHI CHUTNEY | मीठी चटनी 1 TBSP
Method:
Slice the pav down the middle. Heat a tawa and add butter. Once it melts, add the chilli garlic chutney and fresh coriander directly onto the tawa and stir briefly to combine. Spread it across the surface of the tawa. 
Place the pav face down onto this butter and chutney mixture and press firmly so it soaks everything up. Close the pav and press again so both sides are well coated. Switch off the flame and open the pav.
Layer with cheese slices, chopped onion, fresh coriander, masala peanuts, a squeeze of lemon juice, and meethi chutney. Close and serve immediately.
Our spicy, cheesy, garlicky Cheese Chilli Garlic Pav is ready.

title: INSTANT Raw Mango Chunda Recipe | Aam Ka Chunda | इंस्टेंट कच्चे आम का चुंडा | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
Instant Raw Mango Chunda 🥭✨ A quick and easy recipe that turns out incredibly delicious and can be stored for up to a year. Sweet, tangy, and packed with flavour, it’s the perfect way to make the most of raw mango season.

You can enjoy this chunda with thepla, paratha, or simply take a spoonful alongside any meal, it instantly elevates the flavour and overall experience of the food. I’ve shared all the tips and details so you can make it easily at home.
The full written recipe is mentioned below in the description, Happy Cooking! ❤️

Our Wooden Masala Dani blends timeless craftsmanship with everyday functionality, keeping your spices fresh and your kitchen elegant. Add a touch of heritage to your cooking ❤️
🛒 Available now:
Amazon: https://links.yflhome.com/wooden-masala-dani-amazon

Full written recipe - Instant Mango Chunda

Prep Time: 10-15 minutes
Cooking Time: 30–35 minutes 
Serving: Makes 1.5 KG (APPROXIMATELY)

Ingredients:
RAJAPURI RAW MANGO | राजापुरी कच्ची कैरी 1 KG 
SALT | नमक 1 TBSP
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1 TSP
POWDERED SUGAR | पिसी हुई चीनी 1 KG
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 2 TBSP
CUMIN POWDER | जीरा पाउडर 1 TBSP
CINNAMON | दालचीनी 1 INCH
CLOVE | लौंग 2 NOS.

Method:
Start by taking Rajapuri raw mango. Using Rajapuri raw mango gives better results,if not available, Totapuri works well too. 
Peel it well with the help of a peeler, then cut off the tops. 
Roughly cut around the seed and grate everything using the thick holes of a grater, not the fine ones. 
You want long, thick shreds. Grate right up to the seed as well, it grates easily. Once all the mangoes are grated, you should have approximately 750-800 grams after peeling and de-seeding.
Add the grated mango to a clean kadhai. Add salt and turmeric powder and mix well. 
Now add the powdered sugar in 3–4 batches, mixing well after each addition. Once all the sugar is added and well mixed, cover and let it rest for 5–10 minutes.
After resting, uncover and start cooking on medium flame, stirring continuously. At this stage the consistency will be very thin, it will thicken as it cooks. Keep stirring and cooking for about 25–30 minutes.
To check if it is ready, drop a little syrup onto a plate, let it cool completely, and press it between your fingers, it should form a single string that breaks immediately. That is the right consistency for chunda. 
Switch off the flame and let it cool completely to room temperature. You will see it thickens beautifully as it cools.
Once at room temperature, add Kashmiri red chilli powder, cumin powder, cinnamon, and cloves. Mix well. The colour will deepen beautifully. Taste and adjust the spices if needed.
Tip: Always add the spices after the chunda has cooled down completely, adding them while it is hot can make the spices taste bitter and sharp rather than well rounded.
Transfer into clean, airtight glass containers and label with the date. Our delicious, tangy-sweet Instant Mango Chunda is ready.
Serve this with thepla, paratha, or simply take a spoonful alongside any meal.

Full written recipe - Navratan Korma

Prep Time: 30 minutes 
Cooking Time: 45 minutes 
Serving: 4-5 people

Ingredients:
Base Gravy
ONION | प्याज़ 3 MEDIUM SIZED (ROUGHLY CUT)
GINGER | अदरक 2 INCH
GARLIC | लहसुन 8–10 CLOVES
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS.
CASHEW | काजू 25–30 NOS.
MAGAZ | मगज़ 1 TBSP
BAY LEAF | तेज पत्ता 2 NOS.
CINNAMON | दालचीनी 1 INCH
BLACK CARDAMOM | बड़ी इलायची 1 NO.
GREEN CARDAMOM | हरी इलायची 2–3 NOS.
SALT | नमक A LARGE PINCH
FRESH CORIANDER STEMS | हरे धनिये की डंठल A HANDFUL
HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED

Navratan (Nine Ingredients)
CARROT | गाजर 1 MEDIUM SIZED (DICED)
CAULIFLOWER | फूलगोभी 1/3 CUP (FLORETS)
FRENCH BEANS | फ्रेंच बीन्स 4–5 NOS. (DICED)
SWEET CORN | स्वीट कॉर्न 1/3 CUP
GREEN PEAS | हरी मटर 1/3 CUP
CASHEW | काजू 8 NOS. (HALVED)
RAISINS | किशमिश 1 TBSP
MAKHANA | मखाना 1/3 CUP
FRUIT COCKTAIL | फ्रूट कॉकटेल 1/3 CUP
PANEER | पनीर 100–150 GMS (DICED)

Final Cooking
OIL | तेल 2 TBSP
HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED
FRESH CREAM | फ्रेश क्रीम 2 TBSP
BUTTER | मक्खन 2 TBSP (OPTIONAL)
WHITE PEPPER POWDER | सफेद मिर्च पाउडर A PINCH
ROASTED KASURI METHI POWDER | कसूरी मेथी पाउडर A PINCH
GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला A PINCH
SUGAR | शक्कर A LARGE PINCH

Method:
Start with the base gravy. For that add roughly cut onions, ginger, garlic, green chillies, cashews, magaz, bay leaves, cinnamon, black cardamom, green cardamom, a large pinch of salt, fresh coriander stems, and enough hot water to a deep pot. Cover and cook on medium flame for 15 minutes.
By the time the gravy cooks, prep the vegetables. Peel and dice the carrot into small, even pieces. Cut the cauliflower into small florets and trim the tops and dice the french beans to the same size. Keep the sweet corn, green peas, halved cashews, raisins, makhana, fruit cocktail, and diced paneer ready alongside.
Bring a pot of water to a boil and add the carrot, cauliflower, beans, sweet corn, and green peas along with a pinch of salt. Boil for 3–4 minutes, then drain and transfer to a bowl. Set aside.
By now the gravy will have cooked through. Switch off the flame and let everything cool completely. Once cool, remove and discard the bay leaves, black cardamom, and cinnamon. Transfer everything else to a blender and blend into a completely fine, smooth puree.
Heat oil in a kadhai. Once hot, add the halved cashews, raisins, and makhana and fry on low flame until they turn lightly golden. Transfer to a bowl and set aside.
Now strain the blended gravy directly into the same kadhai. Stir well, add hot water to adjust the consistency, and simmer the gravy for 3–4 minutes until it is silky and smooth.
Add the boiled vegetables, fried cashews, raisins and makhana, fruit cocktail, and paneer. Add fresh cream, butter, white pepper powder, roasted kasuri methi powder, and garam masala. 
Mix everything gently and taste. Adjust salt and add a little sugar if needed. Cook for 3–4 minutes so everything comes together beautifully. 
The gravy would be lightly thin so, by the time you serve it is perfect, usually as the gravy sits the gravy tends to get thick because it has cashew.
Our rich, creamy Navratan Korma is ready. Serve it with your choice of bread.

description:

You all love my sauce-with-rice recipes, and this one is truly something special. I’ve shared the full written recipe below, do give it a try. Happy Cooking! ❤️

Full written recipe - Chicken in Creamy Sauce with Lemon Coriander Rice
Prep Time: 20-25 minutes 
Cooking Time: 40-45 minutes 
Serving: 3-4 people

Creamy Chicken
Ingredients
For Velveting
CHICKEN BREAST | चिकन ब्रेस्ट 300–350 GRAMS
SALT | नमक A LARGE PINCH
BLACK PEPPER | काली मिर्च A LARGE PINCH (FRESHLY CRUSHED)
WATER | पानी 6–8 TBSP (ADDED GRADUALLY)
OIL | तेल 1 TBSP
CORN FLOUR | कॉर्न फ्लोर 2 TBSP
Cooking the Chicken
OIL | तेल 1–2 TBSP
GARLIC | लहसुन 1 TBSP (CHOPPED)
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS. (CHOPPED)
Cream Sauce & Final Cooking
BUTTER | मक्खन 2 TBSP
REFINED FLOUR | मैदा 2 TBSP
MILK | दूध 500 ML (ADDED GRADUALLY)
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
OREGANO | ऑरेगैनो A PINCH
RED CHILLI FLAKES | रेड चिली फ्लेक्स A PINCH
BLACK PEPPER | काली मिर्च A LARGE PINCH (FRESHLY CRUSHED)
Method:
Start by cutting the chicken. Slice the breast into thin pieces, then cut against the grain into strips. Transfer them into a bowl.
Now we will velvet the chicken for that, add salt, freshly crushed black pepper, and water gradually about 6–8 tablespoons added in stages. 
Using the back of a spoon, stir vigorously after each addition. The chicken will slowly absorb the water and begin to feel slightly slimy as if you've added egg whites. 
Repeat this process 3–4 times until the chicken looks hydrated and has a light, almost egg white-like coating on it. 
Add oil and corn flour and mix well until a light coating forms on all the strips.
Heat a pan on high flame and add oil. 
Once hot, add chopped garlic and green chillies and stir briefly, do not cook them too long. 
Add the velveted chicken and cook on high flame, stirring in between, until just cooked through and still juicy. Transfer to a bowl immediately.
To make the cream sauce, heat a sauce pan, add butter and let it melt. 
Once melted, reduce the flame to low and add refined flour. Cook the flour slowly over low flame, stirring continuously, until it reaches a sandy texture. 
Now add the milk gradually in 2–3 batches, whisking well after each addition and scraping the edges of the pan to ensure no lumps form. 
Once the first few batches are smooth, add the remaining milk all at once and whisk into a completely smooth, lump-free sauce.
Add the cooked chicken back into the sauce along with salt, oregano, red chilli flakes, and freshly crushed black pepper. Stir well and cook for 1–2 minutes. Taste and adjust salt.
Your delicious Creamy Chicken is ready.

Lemon Coriander Rice
Ingredients:
BUTTER | मक्खन 2 TBSP
OIL | तेल 1 TSP
GARLIC | लहसुन 1 TBSP (CHOPPED)
ONION | प्याज़ 1 NO. (SLICED)
OREGANO | ऑरेगैनो A PINCH
RED CHILLI FLAKES | रेड चिली फ्लेक्स A PINCH
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A LARGE HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
COOKED RICE | पके हुए चावल 3–4 CUPS
SALT | नमक A PINCH
LEMON ZEST | नींबू का छिलका OF 1 LEMON
LEMON JUICE | नींबू का रस OF 1/2 LEMON
SALT | नमक TO TASTE

Method:
Heat a pan on high flame and add butter and oil. 
Once the butter melts, reduce the flame to low and add chopped garlic, sliced onion, oregano, and red chilli flakes. Increase the flame and cook until the garlic is just done and the onions start to turn light golden brown. 
Add the fresh coriander and stir briefly. 
Add the cooked rice and a pinch of salt and mix well. 
Switch off the flame. Add lemon zest, only the yellow part, avoid the white part, then squeeze in the lemon juice. 
Add salt to taste and mix everything well so the rice is evenly coated. Cook for 3–4 minutes and the Lemon 
Fresh and delicious Lemon Coriander Rice is ready.

TO SERVE:
Serve the Creamy Chicken over the Lemon Coriander Rice or you can even plate it half and half like I do.
And enjoy this recipe as a complete meal.

title: Ragi Ambali - INDIAN PROBIOTIC HEALTHY SUMMER DRINK Recipe| रागी अंबली | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
Ragi Ambali, a traditional Indian probiotic summer drink, primarily made in Karnataka and loved across South India. It’s cooling, nourishing, great for gut health, and gives you sustained energy through the day.

Perfect for hot days when you don’t feel like eating much, yet want something wholesome and refreshing. I’ve shared the full written recipe below, do give it a try. Happy Cooking! heart❤️

Smart. Compact. Efficient - CHOPLAB LITE
Meet the kitchen essential designed to keep up with your everyday cooking.
Chop faster, prep easier, and save time, without the mess 🙌

🛒 Buy on Amazon: https://amzn.in/d/iUkb3Ve

Full written recipe - Ragi Ambali

Prep Time: 5-10 minutes
Fermentation Time: 8-12 hours
Cooking Time: 20-25 minutes 
Serving: 10-12 medium sized glasses

Ingredients:
RAGI FLOUR | रागी का आटा 1 CUP
WATER | पानी 1 CUP
HOT WATER | गरम पानी 3 CUPS
WATER | पानी 5 CUPS
CURD | दही 1 CUP
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS. (ROUGHLY CUT)
GINGER | अदरक 1 INCH (ROUGHLY CUT)
CURRY LEAVES | कड़ी पत्ता 10–15 NOS.
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL
SALT | नमक TO TASTE

Method:
Start by making the ragi slurry. Ragi, also known as finger millet or Naachni in Maharashtra, is used here in flour form. Add ragi flour to a bowl along with 1 cup water and whisk well until you have a completely lump-free slurry.
In a kadhai, bring 3 cups of hot water to a boil. Once it comes to a rolling boil, reduce the flame to low and gradually pour in the slurry, stirring continuously. Cook on medium flame, stirring throughout, as the mixture will begin to thicken. Once it has thickened well, cook for 6–7 minutes, then reduce the flame to the lowest setting, cover, and let it steam for 3–4 minutes. After steaming it will thicken further. Switch off the flame and let it cool completely.
To ferment, take a clay pot if available, traditionally that is what is used and add 5 cups of water to it. Wet your hands slightly and take portions of the cooled ragi mixture, drop them into the water one by one. The shape does not need to be perfect just drop them in roughly. Once all the mixture is in, cover and leave to ferment.

Fermentation will take around 8–10 hours depending on the season and your climate. In warmer months it will happen faster, in cooler months it may take a little longer. This fermentation step is the most important part of making Ragi Ambali. In a warm environment, good bacteria grows naturally during fermentation and this is exactly what makes ambali so special, that same bacteria is incredibly beneficial for your gut health. Look for bubbles on the surface and a mild, tangy fermented aroma that is when you know it is ready.

After fermentation, you will see bubbles and smell a pleasant fermented aroma. With clean hands, mash the ragi pieces well into the water until everything breaks down. Add the curd and mix thoroughly using a whisk or mathani until the ambali is smooth and well combined.
In a chopper, add roughly cut green chillies, ginger, curry leaves, and fresh coriander. Chop everything finely and add to the ambali along with salt. Mix well, taste, and adjust salt if needed. Let it rest for a few minutes after adding everything, the flavours will come together even better.
Our refreshing, nourishing Ragi Ambali is ready.

description:

Full written recipe - Anda Rice
Prep Time: 10 minutes 
Cooking Time: 10-15 minutes 
Serving: 3-4 people

Ingredients:
Prep
ONION | प्याज़ 2 MEDIUM SIZED (FINELY CHOPPED)
TOMATO | टमाटर 2 MEDIUM SIZED (FINELY CHOPPED)
EGGS | अंडे 4 NOS.
SALT | नमक A PINCH (to season eggs)
BLACK PEPPER | काली मिर्च A PINCH (FRESHLY CRUSHED) (to season eggs)
Cooking
BUTTER | मक्खन 2 TBSP
OIL | तेल 1 TSP
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1/2 TSP
CURRY LEAVES | कड़ी पत्ता 10–15 NOS.
GINGER GARLIC GREEN CHILLI PASTE | अदरक लहसुन हरी मिर्च पेस्ट 1 TBSP
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1 TSP
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/4 TSP
BIRYANI MASALA | बिरयानी मसाला 1 TSP
HOT WATER | गरम पानी A SPLASH
COOKED RICE | पके हुए चावल 4 CUPS
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
FRESH CORIANDER STEMS | हरे धनिये की डंठल A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
LEMON JUICE | नींबू का रस OF 1/2 LEMON
Method:
Start by roughly cutting the onions and tomatoes and adding them to a string chopper. Chop them finely and set them aside. Using a string chopper makes the process a lot faster and easier.
In a bowl, crack in the eggs, add a pinch of salt and freshly crushed black pepper, and whisk well.
You will also need 4 cups of cooked rice for this recipe. Perfect if you have left-over rice, but if you don’t then just wash 1 cup of rice, preferably basmati, allow it to soak for 30 minutes and then just boil them until cooked and strain.
Now heat a kadhai on high flame. Once hot, add butter and oil. Once they heat up, add cumin seeds and curry leaves and let them crackle. 
Next, add the chopped onions and tomatoes along with salt and cook on high flame, stirring, for 3–4 minutes just until the moisture dries out. You don't need to brown them, just allow the moisture to evaporate.
Add ginger garlic green chilli paste, stir, and cook for 1–2 minutes. Once the masala comes together, reduce the flame and add kashmiri red chilli powder, turmeric powder, biryani masala, and a splash of hot water. Stir well and increase the flame to high to roast the masala through.
Once the masala is well cooked, add the beaten eggs. Stir continuously and cook for 2–3 minutes, keep them soft, do not overcook it.
Once the eggs are just done, add the cooked rice along with salt, chopped coriander stems, fresh coriander, and lemon juice. Mix everything well so the rice is evenly coated in the masala. Taste and adjust salt if required.
Finish with fresh coriander and give it a final mix.
Our fresh, flavourful Street Style Anda Rice is ready, you can have it as is or even have some schezwan sauce along with it.

title: THE QUICKEST RAJMA Recipe in PRESSURE COOKER | कुकर में राजमा बनाने का सबसे आसान तरीका | Sajyot Keer
description:
The easiest Rajma Recipe you’ll ever make, everything goes straight into the pressure cooker, and in just a few whistles, you’ll have a rich, flavourful rajma curry ready. 🫘✨

Minimal prep, no fuss, yet it tastes just as good as a traditional bhuna hua rajma. I’ve shared the full written recipe below in the description, do give it a try. Happy Cooking! ❤️🍛

Pressure Cooker Rajma

Soaking Time: 6-8 hours
Prep Time: 5 minutes
Cooking Time: 30–35 minutes 
Serving: 3-4 people

Ingredients:
CHITRA RAJMA | चित्रा राजमा 250 GMS (SOAKED FOR 6–8 HOURS)
ONION | प्याज़ 3 MEDIUM SIZED (ROUGHLY CUT)
TOMATO | टमाटर 4 MEDIUM SIZED (CROSS SLIT)
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS. (SLIT)
GARLIC | लहसुन 8–10 CLOVES
GINGER | अदरक 2 INCH (GRATED)
CINNAMON | दालचीनी 2 INCH
BLACK CARDAMOM | बड़ी इलायची 1 NO.
BAY LEAF | तेज पत्ता 2 SMALL SIZED
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1 TSP
SALT | नमक 1 TSP
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/2 TSP
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 2 TBSP
CORIANDER POWDER | धनिया पाउडर 2 TBSP
GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला 1/2 TSP
GHEE | घी 1 TBSP
WATER | पानी 3 CUPS
HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED
GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला A PINCH
ROASTED KASURI METHI POWDER | कसूरी मेथी पाउडर A LARGE PINCH
GHEE | घी 1 TBSP
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
Method:
Start by washing and soaking the chitra rajma for 6–8 hours. You can also use regular red rajma if you prefer. Once soaked, discard the soaking water.
Add the soaked rajma to a pressure cooker. Add the roughly cut onions and the tomatoes, just make a cross slit on top of each tomato, no need to cut them further. 
Add the slit green chillies, garlic cloves, grated ginger, cinnamon, black cardamom, bay leaves, cumin seeds, salt, turmeric powder, kashmiri red chilli powder, coriander powder, and garam masala. Add ghee and water and give everything a good stir.
Cover the cooker and cook on high flame for 1 whistle, then reduce to medium low and cook for 15 minutes. Once done, switch off the flame and let the cooker depressurise naturally.
Once the cooker has depressurised, open the lid and check, the tomatoes will be completely soft. Peel off their skins and remove the whole spices from the top. The garlic will have turned completely soft as well and the rajma will be beautifully cooked through.
Switch the flame back on and bring everything to a boil, stirring well so the tomatoes and onions mash into the gravy completely. Within a minute or two you will see everything coming together beautifully.
Cook for 10–12 minutes, stirring in between. If the gravy thickens too much, add a little hot water. If it is too thin, lightly mash a few rajma pieces to bring it together. Adjust the consistency to your liking.
Taste and adjust salt if required. Finish with a pinch of garam masala, a large pinch of roasted kasuri methi powder, ghee, and fresh coriander. Mix well and your amazingly delicious yet easiest Pressure Cooker Rajma is ready.
You can have this Rajma with rice and top it with a squeeze of lemon juice, ghee, sliced onions, and green chutney on the side or you can also enjoy it with some roti or chapati.
