title: Besan Pyaaz Ka Paratha | Easy Breakfast Recipe | स्वादिष्ट बेसन प्याज़ का पराठा | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
Besan Pyaaz Paratha, filled with a super delicious besan and onion stuffing and cooked till nicely crisp with that comforting, perfect paraha texture. 🫓✨ It’s flavourful, filling, and makes for a quick, satisfying breakfast or even an evening snack.
Made with simple ingredients, and something you’ll truly enjoy making at home. I hope you like this recipe, the full written recipe is mentioned below in the description. Happy Cooking! ❤️

A sleek stainless steel masala dani that keeps your everyday spices fresh, organized, and within easy reach ❤️
Shop now: 
Amazon - https://amzn.in/d/0eL0ESDW
Website - https://curaahome.com/products/curaa-steel-masala-dani

Besan Pyaaz ka Paratha

Prep time: 10-15 minutes
Cooking time: Cooking time 15-20 minutes
Serves: 3-4 people


Ingredients:
Dough
WHEAT FLOUR | गेहूं का आटा 1 CUP
SALT | नमक A PINCH
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED
GHEE | घी 1 TSP
GHEE | घी ½ TSP (for greasing)

Besan Pyaaz filling
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 1 NO. (ROUGHLY CUT)
GINGER | अदरक 1/2 INCH (ROUGHLY CUT)
ONION | प्याज़ 2 SMALL SIZED (ROUGHLY CUT)
CORIANDER SEEDS | धनिया 1/2 TSP
FENNEL SEEDS | सौंफ 1/4 TSP
GHEE | घी 2 TSP
AJWAIN | अजवाइन 1/4 TSP (CRUSHED)
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1/2 TSP
BESAN | बेसन 1/4 CUP
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1/2 TSP
CORIANDER POWDER | धनिया पाउडर 1 TSP
GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला A PINCH
DRY MANGO POWDER | अमचूर पाउडर 1/2 TSP
ROASTED KASURI METHI POWDER | कसूरी मेथी पाउडर 1/4 TSP
BLACK SALT | काला नमक A PINCH
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
GHEE | घी AS REQUIRED (for cooking paratha)

Method:
Start by making the dough. Take a large bowl and add wheat flour and salt. Gradually add water and bring everything together into a soft dough, then knead well for 4–5 minutes until well combined, and smooth. Add ghee and knead once more until the dough is soft and smooth. Shape into a ball, apply a little ghee on top so the surface doesn't dry out, cover with a damp cloth, and let it rest for 15–20 minutes.
By the time the dough rests, prepare the filling. Add roughly cut green chilli, ginger, and onions to a chopper and chop them, keep it slightly coarse, not too fine, otherwise the onion will release too much water.
In a mortar and pestle, lightly crush the coriander seeds and fennel seeds, you want them coarse, not powdered.
Heat ghee in a pan and once warm, add the crushed coriander and fennel seeds, crushed ajwain, and cumin seeds. Let them roast briefly. Switch off the flame and sieve the besan directly into the pan. Turn the flame back on to low and roast the besan slowly, stirring continuously, until it deepens slightly in colour and turns fragrant.
Transfer the roasted besan to a large bowl and let it come to room temperature. Once cooled, add the chopped onion mixture, salt, Kashmiri red chilli powder, coriander powder, garam masala, dry mango powder, roasted kasuri methi powder, black salt, and fresh coriander. Mix gently, the salt will draw out moisture from the onion which the besan will absorb beautifully. The filling is ready.
Now for shaping, there are two ways to do this. For the first method, take a 67 gram dough ball and shape it into a tight round. Then cup it from the underside to create a small bowl shape and add about 50 grams of filling into it. Pinch and seal the sides carefully, shape it gently, dust with dry wheat flour and roll it out into a paratha. If the sides start to open while rolling, seal them and continue.
For the second method, divide the same sized dough ball into two equal portions. Shape both into round balls and roll each one out slightly thick, not as thin as a chapati. Place the filling on one rolled out disc, then place the second disc on top and press the edges together to seal. Dust with wheat flour on both sides and roll out into a paratha.
To cook, heat a tawa on high flame and once hot, apply ghee and wipe it across the surface. Sprinkle a little dry wheat flour and wipe that too. Carefully place the paratha on the tawa and cook on medium high flame, pressing gently as it cooks, including along the sides, until light brown spots appear. Flip and cook the other side the same way. Once both sides have cooked through, apply ghee on both sides and cook again until evenly golden and done.
Our crispy, flavourful Besan ka Paratha is ready.

Full written recipe - Paneer Pepper Fry 
Prep Time: 10 minutes 
Cooking Time: 20–25 minutes 
Serving: 3–4 people 

Ingredients:
Pepper Fry Masala
BLACK PEPPERCORNS | साबुत काली मिर्च 2 TBSP
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1 TSP
FENNEL SEEDS | सौंफ 1 TSP
Paneer Prep
PANEER | पनीर 300–350 GRAMS
OIL | तेल 1–2 TSP
Prep
GARLIC | लहसुन A HANDFUL
ONION | प्याज़ 3 MEDIUM SIZED
Tempering & Cooking
OIL | तेल 2 TBSP
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1/4 TSP
FENNEL SEEDS | सौंफ 1/4 TSP
CURRY LEAVES | कड़ी पत्ता 15–20 NOS.
GARLIC | लहसुन 1 TBSP (CHOPPED) AS PREPARED
GARLIC | लहसुन 15 CLOVES
ONIONS | प्याज़ (FINELY CHOPPED) AS PREPARED
SALT | नमक A PINCH
GINGER GARLIC GREEN CHILLI PASTE | अदरक लहसुन हरी मिर्च पेस्ट 2 TBSP
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/2 TSP
GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला 1/2 TSP
HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 4–5 NOS. (SLIT)
CURRY LEAVES | कड़ी पत्ता 15–20 NOS.
LEMON JUICE | नींबू का रस OF 1/2 LEMON
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)

Method:
Start by preparing the pepper fry masala. Take a pan, keep the flame low, and add black peppercorns, cumin seeds, and fennel seeds. Dry roast slowly until they turn fragrant. 
Once done, transfer to a bowl and let them cool completely. Then grind into a fine powder.
Now for the paneer, cut it into thick slabs. Heat a non-stick pan, add a little oil, and once hot, place the paneer slabs in. 
Cook until one side turns light golden brown, then flip and cook the other side the same way. 
Once both sides are done, transfer to a board and let them cool slightly, then cut into pieces.
For the prep, take a chopper and coarsely chop the garlic. Transfer it to a bowl. 
Roughly cut the onions, add them to the same chopper, and chop them finely.
Now for cooking, heat oil in a pan on high flame. Add cumin seeds, fennel seeds, curry leaves, and a little chopped garlic. Sauté briefly. 
Then add the whole garlic cloves, chopped onions, and a pinch of salt. Cook on high flame, stirring continuously, until the onions turn light golden brown.
Add the ginger garlic green chilli paste and cook further. The onions will deepen slightly in colour and develop more flavour.
Now reduce the flame and add turmeric powder, garam masala, half the prepared pepper fry masala, and a splash of hot water. Mix well and cook until the masala is properly roasted and the oil starts to separate.
At this stage the masala will look slightly dry. Add a little hot water to loosen it so it can coat the paneer well. 
Add the paneer pieces along with the remaining pepper fry masala, slit green chillies, curry leaves, lemon juice, and salt. Mix gently so all the paneer gets evenly coated without breaking.
Taste and adjust salt if required. Finish with fresh coriander and give it a final mix.
Our spicy, delicious Paneer Pepper Fry is ready. You can have it as a starter by itself or also enjoy it with some malabar parotta.

title: EASY PANEER TOAST Sandwich Recipe | HOW TO MAKE Paneer at HOME | ताज़े पनीर का सैंडविच | Sanjyot Keer
description:
Breakfast Paneer Toast Sandwich made using quickly prepared fresh paneer, a super filling and incredibly delicious start to your day. 🥪✨ The entire recipe comes together in just 15–20 minutes, including making the paneer from scratch.
It’s simple, wholesome, and perfect for busy mornings. I’ve shared the full written recipe below, do give it a try, I’m sure it’ll become a breakfast staple. Happy Cooking! ❤️

Smart. Compact. Efficient - CHOPLAB LITE
Meet the kitchen essential designed to keep up with your everyday cooking.
Chop faster, prep easier, and save time, without the mess 🙌

🛒 Buy on Amazon: https://amzn.in/d/iUkb3Ve

Fresh Paneer Sandwich
Prep Time: 15–20 minutes 
Cooking Time: 15–20 minutes 
Serving: 2–3
Ingredients:
FULL FAT MILK | फुल फैट दूध 1 LITRE
VINEGAR | सिरका 2 TBSP
WATER | पानी 2 TBSP
BELL PEPPER | शिमला मिर्च 1 MEDIUM SIZED
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS. (ROUGHLY CUT)
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL
CURD | दही 2 TBSP
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
BLACK PEPPER | काली मिर्च 1/4 TSP (FRESHLY CRUSHED)
OREGANO | ओरेगैनो A LARGE PINCH
RED CHILLI FLAKES | रेड चिल्ली फ्लेक्स A LARGE PINCH
SANDWICH BREAD | सैंडविच ब्रेड AS REQUIRED
SOFT BUTTER | मक्खन AS REQUIRED 

Method:
Add full fat milk to a pan and bring it to a gentle boil, stirring as it heats. Once it reaches a gentle boil, switch off the flame and let it rest for 1–2 minutes. 
In a bowl, mix vinegar and water together. Slowly add this mixture to the milk and stir lightly. You will see the paneer begin to separate from the whey almost immediately. 
Strain it through a fine strainer to separate the paneer. Gently press out the excess moisture(Do not squeeze the paneer too hard, you want it soft and slightly moist for the filling, not dry and crumbly.) Transfer the paneer to a large bowl and let it cool completely. 
The whey collected can be saved and used for making kadhi, dough, dal, or any sabzi. 
By the time the paneer cools, prep the vegetables. Take the bell peppers or capsicum and cut just a plank from each side or take a full capsicum if you are using just the capsicum. 
Roughly cut it along with the green chillies and fresh coriander and add everything to a chopper. Chop finely. 
Once the paneer has cooled, add the chopped vegetables to it along with curd, salt, freshly crushed black pepper, oregano, and red chilli flakes. Mix everything well. Taste and adjust salt. The filling is ready.
To assemble, take your choice of bread and apply butter evenly to the bread and then spread the filling generously on a slice of bread and place another slice on top.
 Heat a pan and once hot, apply butter on the surface. Place the sandwich on the pan and press it down with a plate so it toasts evenly from below. 
Check occasionally by lifting it slightly, once the bottom is golden and toasted, apply a little butter on the top side and carefully flip. Press again with the plate and toast the other side the same way until both sides are perfectly golden and crisp. 
Transfer to a chopping board, cut, and serve. 
Our crispy, creamy and healthy Fresh Paneer Sandwich is ready.

title: EASY DRUMSTICK DAL | HEALTHY MORINGA Recipe | सहजन की स्वादिष्ट दाल | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
Drumstick Dal, a super healthy, nutritious, and comforting everyday recipe. 🥣✨ I’ve also shared a simple trick that makes it easy for even kids to enjoy this dal without any fuss. 

Power meets precision in every blend!
Say hello to the BLP 2.0, your new kitchen superstar that makes cooking easier, faster, and way more fun 🥰🚀

Happy shopping! 🛍️ 
🛒 Buy on Amazon: https://amzn.in/d/00oVb9g
🌐 Curaa Website: https://curaahome.com/products/blendlabpro
Swiggy: https://www.swiggy.com/stores/instamart/item/5I2JAHGXDG 

Drumstick Dal
Prep Time: 15–20 minutes 
Cooking Time: 25–30 minutes 
Serving: 3–4
Ingredients:
DRUMSTICKS | सहजन की फलियां3 NOS.
TOOR DAL | तूर दाल 1 CUP (SOAKED FOR 30 MINUTES)
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 1 NO. (SLIT)
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/2 TSP
SALT | नमक 1/2 TSP
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS. (ROUGHLY CUT)
GINGER | अदरक 2 INCH (ROUGHLY CUT)
TOMATO | टमाटर 2 SMALL SIZED (DICED)
GHEE | घी 2 TBSP
MUSTARD SEEDS | राई 1/2 TSP
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1/2 TSP
CINNAMON STICK | दालचीनी 1/2 INCH
GUNDU RED CHILLI | गुंडू लाल मिर्च 2 NOS.
FENUGREEK SEEDS | मेथी के बीज A PINCH
ASAFOETIDA | हींग 1/4 TSP
CURRY LEAVES | कड़ी पत्ता 10–15 NOS.
GINGER CHILLI PASTE | अदरक मिर्च की पेस्ट 1 TBSP (AS PREPARED)
TOMATO | टमाटर 1 MEDIUM SIZED (CHOPPED)
SALT | नमक A PINCH
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/2 TSP
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1/2 TSP
HOT WATER | गरम पानी A SPLASH
SALT | नमक IF REQUIRED
JAGGERY | गुड़ 1 TBSP (OPTIONAL)
LEMON JUICE | नींबू का रस OF 1/2 LEMON
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)

Method:
Start by prepping the drumsticks. Take thin drumsticks avoid the very thick ones. Cut them in the middle this will make peeling easier, then peel them lightly using a peeler or a small knife. Once peeled, cut them into medium sized pieces.
Wash the soaked toor dal and discard the soaking water. Add the dal to a pressure cooker along with the cut drumsticks, slit green chilli, turmeric powder, salt, and enough water so it sits about an inch above the dal. Cover and cook on high flame for 1 whistle, then reduce to medium low and cook for 7–8 minutes or 2–3 more whistles.
By the time, roughly cut the green chillies and ginger and add them to a mortar and pestle. Crush into a coarse paste. Dice the tomatoes and set everything aside.
Once the cooker has naturally depressurised, open the lid and check the drumsticks and dal should both be perfectly cooked. Transfer the drumsticks to a mixer jar, add a little water, and grind finely. Strain this back into the dal, all the woody fibrous parts will stay behind in the strainer while all the goodness of the drumstick goes into the dal. Give the dal a good whisk to bring everything together. If you prefer a slightly textured dal, whisk it less.
For the tempering, heat a frying pan on high flame and add ghee. Once the ghee is hot, add mustard seeds, cumin seeds, cinnamon stick, gundu red chillies, fenugreek seeds, asafoetida, curry leaves, and ginger chilli paste. Stir well. Add the chopped tomato and a pinch of salt. Cook on high flame until the tomatoes soften slightly. Then reduce the flame and add turmeric powder, Kashmiri red chilli powder, and a splash of hot water. Stir and let the masala cook for a minute.
Pour this tempering into the dal and mix well. Place the dal back on the flame and bring it to a boil. Taste and adjust salt. Add jaggery if using and stir well, cooking for 1–2 minutes so it melts through completely.
Finish with lemon juice and fresh coriander, give it a final mix, and our comforting, flavourful Drumstick Dal is ready.

title: PINEAPPLE PANNA RECIPE | इस गर्मी में बनाइये ठंडा और चटपटा अनानास /पाइनएप्पल का पन्ना | Sanjyot Keer
description:
Pineapple Panna, a super refreshing and delicious drink with the perfect balance of sweet, tangy, and slightly spicy flavours. 🍍✨ 

I’ve shared the easiest way to make a pineapple panna syrup that you can store and enjoy anytime. With all the right tips, this one turns out beautifully every single time. Try it once and I’m sure you’ll keep coming back to it. Happy Cooking! ❤️

Power meets precision in every blend!
Say hello to the BLP 2.0, your new kitchen superstar that makes cooking easier, faster, and way more fun 🥰🚀

🛒 Buy on Amazon: https://amzn.in/d/00oVb9g
🌐 Curaa Website: https://curaahome.com/products/blendlabpro

Pineapple Panna

Prep Time: 10–15 minutes 
Cooking Time: 20–25 minutes 
Serving: 15 medium sized glasses

Ingredients:
PINEAPPLE | अनानास 500–600 GRAMS (CLEANED)
SALT | नमक 1 TSP
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED
GINGER | अदरक 1/2 INCH
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 1 NO.
BLACK SALT | काला नमक 1 TSP
CUMIN POWDER | जीरा पाउडर 1/2 TSP
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1/2 TSP
AMCHUR POWDER | अमचूर पाउडर 1/2 TSP
SUGAR | चीनी 125 GRAMS
LEMON JUICE | नींबू का रस OF 1 LEMON

Method:
Start by cutting the pineapple. You can easily get cleaned pineapple from the market which will save a lot of your time to make the syrup.
Trim the top, cut it in half and then into sizable chunks. Add them to a pressure cooker along with salt and water to reach about below half the level of the pineapple, not more than that. Do not add a lot of water, the pineapple will release its own moisture as well.
Cover and cook on high flame for 1 whistle, then reduce the flame and cook for another 5–6 minutes. Switch off the flame and let the pressure release naturally.
Once the cooker has depressurised, open the lid and the pineapple should be completely soft, the core part of the pineapple will still remain a bit stiff. Let it cool down completely before blending.
Once cool, transfer the pineapple along with the cooking water to a mixer jar. Add ginger, green chilli, black salt, cumin powder, kashmiri red chilli powder, and amchur powder. Blend into a fine, smooth puree.
Place a strainer over the cooker and strain the puree through it to remove any fibrous bits. You will see the strainer catches very little, what you are left with is a beautifully smooth pineapple puree.
Add sugar to the strained puree and stir well. Place on high flame and let the sugar dissolve completely and let the syrup come to a boil. Then reduce to medium flame and simmer for 5–6 minutes until the consistency thickens slightly, it should still flow but have some body to it.
Switch off the flame and add lemon juice. Mix well and let it cool completely. As it cools, it will thicken further and come together into a beautiful syrup consistency.
Once completely cool, transfer into a bottle and store. You can store the syrup in the fridge for 1.5 to 2 months.
To serve, you can optionally rim the glass for an additional flavour experience. Just cut a slice of a lemon and rim the slice on the glass then gently tap the rim in a plate with a mixture of kashmiri red chilli powder and salt.
To serve, add loads of ice cubes into a glass, add the syrup as per your taste preference, add a few mint leaves after smacking them gently between your palms to activate the flavour and then just add chilled water and mix well.
Our refreshing, tangy Pineapple Panna syrup is ready.

title: BUSTING INDIAN FOOD MYTHS with @krishashok WHILE COOKING BISIBELEBATH | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
In today’s Cook-Along, we’re not just cooking… we’re questioning everything we’ve been told about food. We are joined by Krish Ashok, author of Masala Lab, and someone you’ve probably seen on social media busting food myths we’ve all grown up believing.

While cooking a comforting Bisi Bele Bath together, what starts as a simple recipe turns into a deeper conversation around fear-mongering, diet fads, and the many food myths we often follow without questioning.

Are eggs vegetarian?
Is jaggery actually healthier than sugar?
Do soaked nuts really make a difference?
Why does food taste better the next day?

We break down everyday kitchen beliefs and look at them through a more practical, easy to understand science-backed lens.

This is a slightly different episode but the conversations go deeper into Indian food habits, beliefs, and how social media is shaping the way we think about what we eat.

Today, we’re constantly exposed to new food advice online. Some of it useful, some of it misleading. This episode is about not following things blindly, and learning how to think for yourself in the kitchen.

If you’ve ever paused mid-cooking or mid-eating and wondered why you’re doing something a certain way, this one’s for you.

title: Butter Garlic Pepper Mushroom Recipe | 10-MINUTE VEG Starter | बटर गार्लिक पेपर मशरूम | Sanjyot Keer
description:
Butter Garlic Pepper Mushrooms, ready in just 10 minutes and packed with bold, peppery flavours. 🍄✨ 

Rich, buttery, garlicky, and loaded with that strong pepper kick, it’s simple yet incredibly satisfying.
Perfect for mushroom lovers and a great quick fix any time of the day. Do try this recipe… Happy Cooking! ❤️

Grind fresh, taste the difference ❤️
With Cüraa Pepper Grinder, every meal gets the perfect finishing touch 🫶🏻

Shop now -
Amazon: https://amzn.in/d/0dgVvpr5
Website: https://yflhome.com/products/curaa-automatic-pepper-grinder
Swiggy: https://surl.lu/kpgecb

Butter Garlic Mushroom

Prep Time: 5-10 minutes 
Cooking Time: 10 minutes 
Serving: 2–3
Ingredients:
Prep for the Recipe
MUSHROOMS | मशरूम 300–350 GMS
GARLIC | लहसुन A HANDFUL
FRESH CORIANDER STEMS | हरे धनिये की डंठल A HANDFUL
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS.
Cooking
BUTTER | मक्खन 2–3 TBSP
OIL | तेल 1 TSP
MUSHROOMS | मशरूम AS PREPARED
CHILLI FLAKES | चिल्ली फ्लेक्स A PINCH
BLACK PEPPER | काली मिर्च 1 TSP (FRESHLY CRUSHED)
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
LEMON ZEST | नींबू का छिलका OF 1 LEMON
LEMON JUICE | नींबू का रस OF 1/2 LEMON
Finish
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
Method:
Start by prepping the mushrooms. Take good quality, medium to small sized mushrooms, wash and clean them well, then pat dry with a clean napkin. Do this right before you are ready to cook. Trim just the stems slightly and they are ready.
Now take a chopper and add garlic cloves, fresh coriander stems, and green chillies. Chop everything finely.
Heat a tawa or pan on high flame. 
Once hot, add butter and oil. Once it heats up, add the chopped garlic, coriander stems, and green chillies. Stir and cook on medium flame for about 30 seconds until the rawness of the garlic is gone.
Now add the mushrooms along with chilli flakes, freshly crushed black pepper, and salt. Stir everything together and cook on medium high flame. 
The mushrooms will first release their water and let that cook off completely. 
Once the moisture is gone, the mushrooms will start picking up a beautiful brown colour.
In about 7–8 minutes you will see the mushrooms are nicely cooked and golden brown. At this stage, add lemon zest and lemon juice and stir well.
Finish with fresh coriander, give it a final mix
Our delicious, garlicky Butter Garlic Mushroom is ready.

title: QUICK & EASY EGG POHA Recipe | नाश्ते में बनाइये प्रोटीन वाला अंडा पोहा | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
Quick and Easy Egg Poha, a delicious breakfast and one of the easiest ways to add protein to your everyday poha. 🍳✨ With all the right tips to keep it moist and fluffy, this one comes together beautifully every time.
Try it once and it might just become your go-to breakfast staple. Do give it a try. Happy Cooking! ❤️

Meet the kitchen essential designed to keep up with your everyday cooking.
Chop faster, prep easier, and save time, without the mess 🙌

🛒 Buy on Amazon: https://amzn.in/d/iUkb3Ve
🌐 Curaa Website: https://yflhome.com/products/curaa-choplab-lite

Full written recipe - Egg Poha

Prep Time: 10 minutes 
Cooking Time: 10 minutes 
Serving: 2–3 people

Ingredients:
Poha
POHA | पोहा 1.5 CUPS
Soaking the Poha
SALT | नमक A PINCH
SUGAR | शक्कर 1/2 TSP
LEMON JUICE | नींबू का रस OF 1/2 LEMON
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/4 TSP
Eggs
EGGS | अंडे 3 NOS.
SALT | नमक A PINCH
BLACK PEPPER | काली मिर्च A PINCH (FRESHLY CRUSHED)
Cooking
GHEE | घी 1 TBSP
MUSTARD SEEDS | राई 1/4 TSP
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1/4 TSP
ONION | प्याज़ 1 MEDIUM SIZED (FINELY CHOPPED)
CURRY LEAVES | कड़ी पत्ता 8–10 NOS.
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS. (SLICED)
TOMATO | टमाटर 1 MEDIUM SIZED (FINELY CHOPPED)
SALT | नमक A PINCH
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/4 TSP
GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला 1/4 TSP
EGGS | अंडे AS PREPARED
POHA | पोहा AS PREPARED
WATER | पानी A SPLASH
Finish
ROASTED PEANUTS | भुनी मूंगफली A HANDFUL (CRUSHED)
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
Method:
Start by soaking the poha. Place a colander over a bowl and add the poha into it. Pour enough water so the poha is submerged, then quickly and gently wash it with your hands so all the loose starch comes off, you will see the water turn cloudy. Once washed, drain the water out and let the colander sit over the bowl so only as much water as the poha needs stays, and the rest drips away below.
Then, finely chop the onion in a chopper and transfer it to a bowl. Roughly cut the tomato, add it to the same chopper, and chop it as well.
Check the poha at this stage, each grain should be separate, soft, and well soaked with none of them sticking together. Add salt, sugar, lemon juice, and turmeric powder to the soaked poha and mix gently.
In a bowl, crack in the eggs and add salt and freshly crushed black pepper. Beat well.
Now heat a kadhai on high flame and add ghee. Once the ghee is hot, add mustard seeds and cumin seeds and let them crackle. Add the chopped onions and cook, stirring well, until they turn light golden brown. That slight caramelised sweetness from the onions makes all the difference.
Once the onions are light golden brown, add curry leaves and sliced green chillies. Stir well, then add the chopped tomatoes along with a pinch of salt. Cook for about a minute. Add turmeric powder and garam masala and mix everything together.
Now pour in the beaten eggs and stir continuously. Cook for just 1–2 minutes — keep the eggs soft and do not overcook them.
Add the seasoned poha and mix everything together gently. The eggs will continue to cook along with the poha. Add a splash of water, cover, and switch off the flame. This keeps the poha from drying out and makes it fluffy and moist. Let it rest for 2–3 minutes.
Open the lid, give it a stir, you will see the steam rising from within. Finish with crushed roasted peanuts and fresh coriander and mix well.
Our fluffy, flavourful Anda Poha is ready.

title: INSTANT NYLON KHAMAN in 5 MINUTES | Easy Microwave Recipe | इंस्टेंट नायलॉन खमण | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
Instant Nylon Khaman that cooks in just 5 minutes in the microwave, super soft, spongy, and beautifully juicy every time. ✨ It’s quick, fuss-free, and perfect when you’re craving something light and delicious.

I’ve also shared the chutney that pairs perfectly with it. Do give this recipe a try… Happy Cooking! ❤️

Say hello to the BLP 2.0, your new kitchen superstar that makes cooking easier, faster, and way more fun 🥰🚀
Happy shopping! 🛍️ 

🛒 Buy on Amazon: https://amzn.in/d/00oVb9g

🌐 Curaa Website: https://curaahome.com/products/blendlabpro

Instant Nylon Khaman

Prep Time: 10 minutes
Cooking Time: 10-15 minutes
Serving: 3–4
Ingredients:
WATER | पानी 1 CUP / 240 ML
CITRIC ACID | नींबू का सत 1 TSP
SALT | नमक 1/2 TBSP
SUGAR | शक्कर 3 TBSP
GROUNDNUT OIL | मूंगफली का तेल 1.5 TBSP
GRAM FLOUR | बेसन 2 CUPS + 2 TBSP / 220 GRAMS
BAKING SODA | बेकिंग सोडा 1 TSP
WATER | पानी 1 TSP
OIL | तेल A FEW DROPS
Tempering
GROUNDNUT OIL | मूंगफली का तेल 2 TBSP
MUSTARD SEEDS | राई 2 TSP
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 3 NOS. (SLIT)
CURRY LEAVES | कड़ी पत्ता 10–12 NOS.
ASAFOETIDA | हींग 1/4 TSP
WATER | पानी 1 CUP
SUGAR | शक्कर 2 TBSP
SALT | नमक 1/4 TSP
Garnish
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया 
FRESH COCONUT | ताज़ा नारियल
Method:
Start by preparing the base mixture. For that, take a large bowl and add water to it. Add citric acid, salt, sugar, and groundnut oil. Whisk properly until the citric acid dissolves completely. This step is important, so make sure everything is fully dissolved.
Now place a strainer over the same bowl and add gram flour to it. Sieve it directly into the liquid. Once added, whisk it well to form a smooth batter without lumps. The consistency should be flowing but slightly thick.
Cover and let this batter rest for 10 minutes. While the batter rests, prepare the chutney.
Now check the batter. You’ll notice it has settled slightly. At this stage, grease a microwave-safe tray with a few drops of oil, covering both the base and sides properly.
Add baking soda to the batter along with a little water and mix immediately. You will see the batter becoming light and airy. Do not overmix; just combine it well.
Pour this batter into the greased tray and lightly tap it to level it out. Place it in the microwave and cook for 5 minutes.
After 5 minutes, check using a toothpick. If it comes out clean, it is done.
Let it cool slightly, then loosen the sides and demould by flipping it out. Cut into desired pieces. You’ll see a soft, airy, spongy texture.
Now prepare the tempering. Heat groundnut oil in a pan. Once hot, add mustard seeds and let them crackle. Add green chillies, curry leaves, and hing. Switch off the flame and let it cool slightly.
Now carefully add water, sugar, and salt. Mix well and cook for 2–3 minutes on high flame. Then switch off and let it cool slightly. The tempering should be warm, not very hot.
Pour this evenly over the khaman pieces so everything soaks properly. You’ll see it absorb nicely and become even softer.
Your delicious, soft, spongy and juicy Nylon Khaman is ready.

Green Chutney:
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया 1 CUP
GINGER | अदरक 1/2 INCH
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 3 NOS.
GATHIYA | गाठिया 3 TBSP
SUGAR | शक्कर 1 TBSP
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1 TSP
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
ICE CUBE | बर्फ 1 NO.
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED
LEMON JUICE | नींबू का रस OF 1/2 LEMON
Method:
In a mixer jar, add fresh coriander, ginger, green chillies, gathiya, sugar, cumin seeds, salt, ice cubes, and water. Grind into a smooth chutney. 
Keep it slightly thin. Add lemon juice later, just before serving.
Finally, add lemon juice to the chutney and mix. Taste and adjust if needed.
Serve the soft, spongy Nylon Khaman with chutney.

title: AMRITSARI ALOO PURI | Khatte Meethe Aloo & PERFECT PURI with TIPS | अमृतसरी आलू पूरी | Sanjyot Keer
description:
Amritsari Special Aloo Ki Launji with perfect puris, khatte-meethe aloo paired with beautifully fried, puffed puris, just like the ones I had on my recent trip to Amritsar. ✨ That trip inspired me to share this recipe exactly the way I enjoyed it there.
It turns out incredibly delicious, comforting, and full of flavour. You guys should definitely try this one… Happy Cooking! ❤️

Our Wooden Masala Dani blends timeless craftsmanship with everyday functionality, keeping your spices fresh and your kitchen elegant.
Add a touch of heritage to your cooking ❤️

🛒 Available now:
Website: https://curaahome.com/...
Amazon: https://amzn.in/d/8ixtD2f

Aloo Ki Launji with Puri

Prep Time: 20–25 minutes
Cooking Time: 25–30 minutes
Serving: 3–4

Ingredients

Puri Dough

WHEAT FLOUR | गेहूं का आटा 2 CUPS
SALT | नमक 1/2 TSP
OIL | तेल 1 TBSP
AJWAIN | अजवाइन A LARGE PINCH
WATER | पानी 3/4 CUP
OIL | तेल 1 TSP

Aloo Prep

POTATO | आलू 4 MEDIUM SIZED

Paste

GINGER | अदरक 1 INCH
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS.
CORIANDER SEEDS | साबुत धनिया 1 TSP

Launji

MUSTARD OIL | सरसों का तेल 2 TBSP
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1/2 TSP
FENNEL SEEDS | सौंफ 1/2 TSP
KALONJI | कलौंजी 1/4 TSP
METHI SEEDS | मेथी दाना 1/4 TSP
BLACK PEPPERCORNS | साबुत काली मिर्च 2 NOS.
HING | हींग 1/2 TSP
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/2 TSP
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1/2 TSP
CORIANDER POWDER | धनिया पाउडर 2 TSP
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED

Sweet & Sour

TAMARIND | इमली AS REQUIRED
JAGGERY | गुड़ 2 TBSP

Finishing

GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला A PINCH
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)

Frying

OIL | तेल AS REQUIRED

Method

Start by preparing the dough for puri. Take a large bowl and add wheat flour, salt, oil, and ajwain. Mix it lightly.
Now gradually add water and bring everything together into a dough. Once it comes together, knead it properly for 3–4 minutes until smooth and semi-stiff.
At this stage, add a little oil and knead once again. Shape it into a round, apply a light layer of oil on top so it doesn’t dry out, cover with a damp cloth, and let it rest.
Now move to the potatoes. Cut them into medium dice and keep them in water so they don’t discolour.
Next, prepare a coarse paste. In a mortar and pestle, add ginger, green chillies, and coriander seeds. Crush it roughly.
Now for the launji, place a pressure cooker on high flame and let it heat. Add mustard oil and heat it well until it starts smoking. Switch off the flame and let it cool slightly.
Once slightly cooled, switch the flame back on and add cumin seeds, fennel seeds, kalonji, methi seeds, black peppercorns, and hing. Let them crackle.
Add the prepared paste and sauté briefly until the raw aroma goes away.
Now add the diced potatoes and mix well. Add turmeric powder, Kashmiri red chilli powder, coriander powder, and salt. Stir properly so the masalas coat the potatoes well.
Add water just enough to cover the potatoes. Cover with the lid and cook on medium flame for 2 whistles.
Meanwhile, soak tamarind in hot water, mash it, and strain to get a smooth pulp. Keep jaggery ready.
Once the cooker depressurises naturally, open the lid and check the potatoes. They should be nicely cooked.
Switch on the flame again and lightly mash a few potato pieces. This helps thicken the launji naturally.
Now add jaggery and tamarind pulp. Mix well and cook for 2–3 minutes so the jaggery melts and the rawness of the tamarind goes away.
You will see the consistency turning slightly thick and glossy. Taste and adjust salt, sweetness, and tanginess as needed.
Finish with garam masala and fresh coriander. Mix gently. Aloo ki launji is ready.
Now for the puri, heat oil in a pan.
Take small portions of the dough and shape them into smooth balls. Lightly press and coat with a little oil, then roll into puris.
Once the oil is hot, add the puri carefully. As it comes up, gently press it so it puffs. Flip and fry until light golden.
Remove and fry the remaining puris in the same way.
Serve hot puri with aloo ki launji and grated mooli with chutney and some pickle.

description:

Many people say their halwa doesn’t turn out right, so I’ve shared all the exact tips and measurements to help you get it perfect every time.

Rawa Sheera (Suji Halwa)
Prep Time: 5–10 minutes
Cooking Time: 20–25 minutes
Serving: 4
Ingredients:
•⁠  ⁠RAWA | सूजी 1 CUP
•⁠  ⁠SUGAR | चीनी 1 CUP + 2 TBSP
•⁠  ⁠GHEE | घी 1/2 CUP + 2 TBSP
•⁠  ⁠WATER | पानी 3 CUPS
•⁠  ⁠GREEN CARDAMOM | हरी इलायची 2 NOS.
•⁠  ⁠RAISINS | किशमिश 15–20 NOS.
•⁠  ⁠ALMONDS | बादाम 10–12 NOS. (SLICED)
Method:
•⁠  ⁠Start by setting your ratios. Take rawa, sugar, and water in the given proportions. You can use any cup or katori at home; just use the same one for measuring all of it. If you are making this for prasad, you can slightly increase the sugar and ghee by 2 TBSP each, which is already mentioned in the recipe. Otherwise, you can use 1 cup sugar and 1/2 cup ghee.
•⁠  ⁠Now begin with the sugar syrup. Take a vessel and add sugar and water. Lightly crush the green cardamom and add it in. Place it on the gas and let the sugar dissolve.
•⁠  ⁠Once the sugar melts, let it simmer for 3–4 minutes. You don’t need any string consistency here, just a lightly cooked syrup.
•⁠  ⁠Now move to making the sheera. Take a kadhai and add ghee to it. Once hot, add the rawa and start roasting it on low flame. Keep stirring continuously so it roasts evenly.
•⁠  ⁠As it cooks, the rawa will start releasing aroma and the ghee will begin separating slightly. Do not rush this step and do not let the colour change too much. This process will take around 15 minutes.
•⁠  ⁠Once the rawa is properly roasted and you see the ghee separating slightly, add raisins and sliced almonds. Stir and roast for about a minute.
•⁠  ⁠Now carefully add the prepared sugar syrup. You can strain it while adding so the cardamom doesn’t come into the mouth later.
•⁠  ⁠Mix everything well. At this stage, it will look quite liquid, but that is exactly what you want. The rawa needs this moisture to cook and swell properly.
•⁠  ⁠Increase the flame to medium and cook until the rawa absorbs all the water. Keep stirring occasionally.
•⁠  ⁠Within a few minutes, you will see most of the liquid get absorbed. Continue cooking until all the moisture is gone and the ghee starts separating again. The halwa will look glossy.
•⁠  ⁠Once done, switch off the flame and cover it. Let it rest for 10–15 minutes.
•⁠  ⁠After resting, open the lid and give it a good mix. Fluff it up gently.
•⁠  ⁠You will see a soft, fluffy, perfectly cooked Suji Halwa.

title: THECHA PANEER RICE | BEST & QUICK Spicy Meal Recipe | झणझणीत ठेचा पनीर राइस | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
Thecha Paneer Rice, a zanzanit, Maharashtrian-style way to use leftover rice. Fresh thecha tossed with rice, paneer, and veggies makes this a simple yet super comforting and satisfying meal.
Full of bold flavours and that perfect thecha kick, this is the kind of homely food you’ll want to make again and again. Do try this recipe. Happy Cooking! ❤️🍚🌶️✨

Full written recipe - Thecha Paneer Rice

Prep Time: 15-20 minutes
Cooking Time: 15–20 minutes
Serving: 2-3 people

Ingredients:
Roasting Peanuts
PEANUTS | मूंगफली 2 TBSP

Thecha (Spicy Green Paste)
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 5 NOS.
FRESH CORIANDER STEMS | हरा धनिया की डंठल A HANDFUL
GARLIC | लहसुन 10–12 CLOVES
PEANUTS | मूंगफली 2 TBSP
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा A PINCH
GROUNDNUT OIL | मूंगफली का तेल 1 TSP
FRESH CORIANDER | हरे धनिये की डंठल A HANDFUL
SALT | नमक A PINCH
WATER | पानी A SPLASH

Veggies & Paneer Prep
CABBAGE | पत्ता गोभी 1/3 SMALL SIZED
TOMATO | टमाटर 1 MEDIUM SIZED
CAPSICUM | शिमला मिर्च 1 SMALL SIZED
ONION | प्याज़ 1 MEDIUM SIZED
PANEER | पनीर 200–250 GRAMS

Thecha Paneer Rice
GROUNDNUT OIL | मूंगफली का तेल 1 TBSP
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1/2 TSP
CURRY LEAVES | कड़ी पत्ता A HANDFUL
SALT | नमक A PINCH
SUGAR | शक्कर A PINCH
COOKED RICE | पाके हुए चावल 4 CUPS
LEMON JUICE | नींबू का रस OF 1/2 LEMON
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)

Method:
Start by roasting the peanuts. Place a pan on high flame and let it heat slightly. Add the peanuts and roast them until nicely toasted. Once done, transfer them to a bowl and let them cool completely. These need to be crushed later and added on top.
In the same pan, add green chillies, coriander stems, garlic, and peanuts. Dry roast everything properly until you see dark brown spots and light charring.
Switch off the flame. Add cumin seeds and groundnut oil, toss quickly, and transfer everything to a bowl to cool.
Once cooled, transfer this mixture to a mixer jar. Add fresh coriander, salt, and a little water. Grind into a fine paste. The thecha is ready.
Now prepare the vegetables. Thinly slice the cabbage into juliennes. Cut the tomato in half, remove the eye, and slice it lengthwise. Cut the capsicum by removing the core and slicing it into thin strips, keeping the skin side down. This helps you cut the capsicum more safely and effectively. Slice the onions thinly as well. Keep all the vegetables ready.
Cut the paneer into strips and then into bite-sized cubes.
Take the roasted peanuts and crush them coarsely using a mortar and pestle.
Now for cooking, take a kadhai and place it on a high flame. Let it heat well. Add groundnut oil and allow it to heat. 
Add cumin seeds and curry leaves, let them crackle.
Add sliced onions and sauté lightly. Once they start softening, add all the remaining vegetables and cook on high flame for 2–3 minutes while stirring. Keep the vegetables slightly crunchy.
Add the prepared thecha (paste) along with paneer, salt, and a pinch of sugar. Mix everything well so the paneer gets coated with the masala.
Add the cooked rice and gently mix everything together so the rice is evenly coated. I have not used basmati rice here, I have used Sona Masuri. You can use any short- to medium-grain rice like Sona Masuri, Ambemohar, or Kolam.
Taste and adjust salt if required.
Finish by adding crushed peanuts, lemon juice, and fresh coriander. Mix lightly.
Zanzanit Thecha Paneer Rice is ready, you can serve curd alongside it or also some chilled Chaas (buttermilk).

title: Kadhi Chawal in 15-MINUTES | PRESSURE COOKER RECIPE | कुकर वाले इंस्टेंट कड़ी चावल | Sanjyot Keer
description:
Kadhi Chawal made using just one pressure cooker, no need to cook the rice and kadhi separately. I’ve shared the perfect way to pressure cook them together, saving time, effort, and even fuel, which matters to all of us now.

The recipe comes together in minutes and tastes incredibly wholesome and comforting. Do give it a try, I’m sure you’ll love it. Happy Cooking! ❤️🍚✨

Full written recipe - Pressure Cooker Kadhi Chawal

Prep Time: 10 minutes
Cooking Time: 15-20 minutes
Serves: 3-4 people

Ingredients:
Rice
RICE | चावल 1/2 CUP
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED (for washing and soaking the rice)

Kadhi
CURD | दही 1 CUP
BESAN | बेसन 1/4 CUP
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/2 TSP
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1/2 TSP
WATER | पानी 4 CUPS

Ginger & Green chilli Paste
GINGER | अदरक 1 INCH
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS.
CORIANDER SEEDS | साबुत धनिया 1 TBSP

Tempering & Cooking
GHEE | घी 1 TBSP
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1/2 TSP
MUSTARD SEEDS | राई 1/2 TSP
FENUGREEK SEEDS | मेथी दाना 5–6 NOS.
HING | हींग 1/4 TSP
BLACK PEPPERCORNS | साबुत काली मिर्च 3–4 NOS.
RED CHILLI | सूखी लाल मिर्च 2 NOS.
CURRY LEAVES | कड़ी पत्ता 10–12 NOS.
POTATO | आलू 2 MEDIUM SIZED (DICED)
JAGGERY | गुड़ 1 TSP
HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
Method:
Take a bowl, place a strainer over it, and add the rice into it. You can use any short or medium-grain rice like Sona Masuri. Wash it 2–3 times or until the water runs clear. Once done, let it soak while we prepare the rest.
Now prepare the kadhi mixture. In a large bowl, add curd, besan, salt, turmeric powder, and Kashmiri red chilli powder. Add water in two batches and whisk it properly so there are no lumps. The mixture should be smooth.
Next, take a mortar and pestle. Add ginger, green chillies, and coriander seeds. Crush everything into a coarse mixture.
Now take a pressure cooker and place it on high flame. Add ghee and let it heat properly. 
Once hot, add cumin seeds, mustard seeds, fenugreek seeds, hing, black peppercorns, dry red chillies, and curry leaves. Let everything crackle.
Add the crushed ginger-chilli mixture and sauté briefly. Add diced potatoes and sauté for a few seconds.
Now reduce the flame to low and add the prepared curd mixture. Stir continuously and bring it to a gentle simmer.
Once it starts simmering, drain the soaked rice and add it to the cooker. Stir gently. Add jaggery and mix once.
Close the lid and cook on high flame for one whistle. Then reduce the flame to medium-low and cook for 6–8 minutes.
Switch off the flame and let the cooker depressurise naturally.
Open the lid, stir once, and taste. Adjust salt if required.
If the consistency feels thick, add a little hot water and mix.
Finish with freshly chopped coriander.
Our easy, pressure cooker Kadhi Chawal is ready. You can serve some papad and pickle along with this.

title: SHAHI TUKDA RECIPE | Special SHAHI TOAST & RABDI | मेरे घर का शाही टुकड़ा | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
This Shahi Toast is a truly special recipe in my family, crispy fried bread soaked in sugar syrup, topped with lacchedaar rabdi and finished with a fragrant shahi masala… pure heaven on a plate. ✨

I’ve shared the full recipe with all the details so you can make it at home too. Do give it a try, I’m sure it will become a favourite at your place as well. Happy Cooking! ❤️✨

The best blender + the best chopper at the best price.

Shop now: https://curaahome.com/products/curaa-blendlab 

Shahi Toast
Prep Time: 10-15 minutes
Cooking Time: 45-50 minutes
Serving: 4-5 people
Ingredients:
Sugar Syrup
SUGAR | चीनी 1 CUP
CARDAMOM POWDER | इलायची पाउडर A PINCH
SAFFRON | केसर A PINCH
WATER | पानी 1 CUP
LEMON JUICE | नींबू का रस A FEW DROPS
Method:
Start off by making the sugar syrup. Take a vessel and add sugar, cardamom powder, saffron, and water. Place it on the gas and let the sugar dissolve completely.
You don’t need to cook it too much. There is no need for a one-string consistency — the syrup should just be slightly sticky. Once the sugar dissolves, let it simmer for about a minute or two.
To check, take a little syrup on a plate and allow it to cool slightly. It should feel lightly sticky. At this stage, switch off the flame and add a few drops of lemon juice. Mix well so the syrup doesn’t crystallise later.
Transfer the syrup to a bowl and let it cool slightly.

Shahi Tukda Masala
FENNEL SEEDS | सौंफ 1 TBSP
GREEN CARDAMOM | हरी इलायची 3–4 NOS.
BLACK CARDAMOM | बड़ी इलायची 1 NO.
BLACK PEPPERCORNS | साबुत काली मिर्च 3–4 NOS.
ALMOND | बादाम 1/4 CUP
Method:
In a spice grinder jar, add almonds first. Then add fennel seeds and green cardamom. Lightly crush the black cardamom and add only the seeds inside. Finally, add black peppercorns and grind everything into a fine powder.
The special Shahi Masala is ready, this masala will take this recipe to the next level.

Rabdi
MILK | दूध 1.5 LITRES
SUGAR | चीनी 3 TBSP / 50 GRAMS
SHAHI TUKDA MASALA | तैयार मसाला 2 TBSP
Method:
Take a wide kadhai and add milk. Place it on the gas and bring it to a gentle boil. Keep stirring occasionally so the milk doesn’t stick to the bottom.
Once the milk begins simmering, reduce the flame to medium-low and allow a layer of malai to form on top. When this layer forms, gently push it to the sides of the pan using a fork or spatula.
Keep repeating this process while the milk slowly reduces. Let the milk reduce until it becomes roughly one-third of its original quantity.
If the malai isn’t forming quickly, you can fan the surface lightly using a plate or tray to speed up the process.
This reduction process takes about 25 minutes. By then the milk will have reduced significantly and you will see thick layers of malai collected along the sides.
At this stage, add sugar and mix it nicely. Cook for a couple of minutes.
Now carefully scrape the malai from the sides and fold it back into the milk. Do not dissolve the malai bits, completely the rabdi should have visible lacche.
Add the prepared masala and mix gently. Cook for another 2–4 minutes.
At this stage the rabdi may look slightly thin, but it will thicken naturally as it cools, so avoid over-reducing it.
Once done, switch off the flame and transfer the rabdi into a bowl. Allow it to cool completely to room temperature and then refrigerate it until chilled.

Frying Bread
SANDWICH BREAD | ब्रेड स्लाइस AS REQUIRED
GHEE | घी AS REQUIRED
Method:
Take sandwich bread and stack the slices. Trim off the edges. Once trimmed, cut each slice diagonally and then again into triangles so each slice becomes four pieces.
Repeat the same for the remaining bread depending on how many servings you want.
Take a frying pan and add ghee. We will shallow-fry the bread. Once the ghee is hot, add the bread pieces and fry them on very low flame.
Cook until both sides turn light golden brown and crisp. Once fried, remove them and transfer onto a sieve to drain excess ghee.
Your bread pieces are ready for assembling the Shahi Toast.
Assembly
For assembling, dip the crisp bread into the sugar syrup and place it on a plate. Add the rabdi over it. Sprinkle some Shahi Masala that we prepared earlier and garnish with dry rose petals and chopped pistachios.
Our delicious Shahi Toast is ready.

title: INSTANT BESAN KA CHILLA | 15-MINUTE Healthy Vegetable Paneer BREAKFAST | बेसन का चीला | Sanjyot Keer
description:
Instant Besan Dosa, or you can call it a crispy cheela, a super quick, healthy and easy recipe. It turns out beautifully crisp and is loaded with veggies and paneer, making it a wholesome and satisfying meal.
The batter comes together in seconds and the texture is just amazing. Do give this recipe a try! I’ve shared the full written recipe below in the description. Happy Cooking! ❤️✨

Instant Besan Dosa / Cheela
Prep Time: 5-10 minutes
Cooking Time: 5-10 minutes
Serving: 3-4 Dosas/Cheela
Ingredients:
Batter
BESAN | बेसन 1/2 CUP
RAWA | रवा 4 TBSP
AJWAIN | अजवाइन 1/4 TSP
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 1 NO.
GINGER | अदरक 1/2 INCH
CURD | दही 1 TBSP
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/4 TSP
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1/4 TSP
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा A PINCH
WATER | पानी 1/4 CUP + 2 TBSP
Vegetable & Paneer Filling
CABBAGE | पत्ता गोभी 1/3 SMALL SIZED
CARROT | गाजर 1/2 MEDIUM SIZED
CAPSICUM | शिमला मिर्च 1/2 MEDIUM SIZED
PANEER | पनीर 150 GRAMS (GRATED)
Cooking
GHEE | घी AS REQUIRED
SALT | नमक A PINCH
BLACK PEPPER | काली मिर्च TO TASTE (FRESHLY CRUSHED)
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A SMALL HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
Method:
To make the instant batter, add besan, rawa, green chilli, ginger, curd, turmeric powder, kashmiri red chilli powder, salt, cumin seeds, and water into a mixer jar. 
Blend everything into a smooth batter. It shouldn’t take more than a few seconds to make the batter smooth. Cover it and let it rest just until you prepare the filling.
Roughly cut the cabbage. Peel the carrot and cut it roughly. Remove the core of the capsicum and cut it roughly as well.
Add all the vegetables to a chopper and chop them finely. It takes just a few seconds and the vegetables get nicely chopped. Transfer them to a bowl.
Add grated paneer and mix everything together. The filling is now ready.
Now place a dosa tawa on the gas and heat it on high flame. Here is how you can check the perfect temperature: sprinkle a little water on the tawa. The water should sizzle and evaporate immediately. Once the tawa is hot, reduce the flame to low.
By now, the batter would have rested. The consistency should be similar to dosa batter.
You can make this in two ways. To make the plain version, pour a ladle of batter on the tawa and spread it evenly just like dosa. If making a plain cheela, spread it slightly bigger so it becomes more filling.
Drizzle some ghee and cook it on low flame for 2–3 minutes. Once the base becomes crisp, gently loosen it with a spatula and fold it. The plain besan dosa is ready.
For the vegetable version, pour a ladle of batter and spread it like dosa again. Immediately add the prepared vegetable paneer filling over it and press it lightly.
Drizzle melted ghee over it. Sprinkle a pinch of salt, freshly crushed black pepper, and chopped fresh coriander. 
Using the spatula, press the topping gently so it sticks to the cheela. Cook it on low flame for 2–3 minutes so the base becomes crisp.
Carefully flip the cheela and press it again. Let the vegetables cook on low flame for another 2–3 minutes.
Once everything is nicely cooked and you see even browning, fold the cheela.
Our healthy, instant and tasty Besan Vegetable Paneer Cheela is ready. You can serve it with curd and some chutney or even ketchup.

title: CHILLI GARLIC PANEER Tawa Fry | Spicy Starter Recipe | चिली गार्लिक पनीर तवा फ्राई | Sanjyot Keer
description:
Chilli Garlic Paneer Tawa Fry recipe, a super easy paneer starter that comes together in no time and tastes absolutely incredible. It’s delicious on its own or even paired with soft rumali roti. If you’re a paneer lover, you should definitely try this recipe.😋✨
I’ve shared the full written recipe below in the description. Happy Cooking!❤️

🌐 Curaa Website: https://curaahome.com/products/curaa-blendlab


Full written recipe - Chilli Garlic Tawa Paneer Fry

Prep time: 5-10 mins (excluding soaking time of chillies)
Cooking time: 20-25 mins
Serves: 4-5 people


Ingredients:
Prep
GARLIC | लहसुन A LARGE HANDFUL
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च 15 NOS. (DESEEDED & SOAKED)
GINGER | अदरक 1 INCH
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 3 NOS.
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED

Paneer
OIL | तेल 1–2 TSP
PANEER | पनीर 350 GRAMS

Masala Base
OIL | तेल 2–3 TBSP
GARLIC | लहसुन 4 TBSP (CHOPPED)
RED CHILLI PASTE | लाल मिर्च पेस्ट AS PREPARED
SALT | नमक A PINCH
CURD | दही 1/3 CUP
GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला 1/2 TSP
SPICY RED CHILLI POWDER | तीखी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1 TSP
HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED
SUGAR | शक्कर A PINCH
SALT | नमक TO TASTE

Finish
FRESH CORIANDER STEMS | हरा धनिया डंठल A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)

Plating
LEMON | नींबू AS REQUIRED
CHAAT MASALA | चाट मसाला AS REQUIRED
CHEESE | चीज़ AS REQUIRED
BUTTER | मक्खन AS REQUIRED
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया AS REQUIRED

Method:
Start by preparing the chilli paste. In a mixer jar, add the soaked red chillies along with ginger, green chillies, and a little water. Grind everything into a fine, smooth paste. Transfer the paste to a bowl and keep it aside.
Now take the garlic, add it to a chopper and roughly chop it. Keep it slightly coarse so you get small bits of garlic in the masala.
Place a non-stick pan on high flame and let it heat properly. Add oil and allow it to warm up. Place the paneer slabs in the pan and fry them until both sides turn lightly golden brown. Once done, transfer them to a chopping board or a plate and let them rest for a bit.
Now place a tawa on high flame and heat it well. Add oil and let it heat properly. Once hot, add the chopped garlic and sauté briefly. Add the prepared red chilli paste along with a pinch of salt. Stir well and cook it until the raw flavour of the chilli paste reduces.
Meanwhile whisk the curd properly so there are no lumps. Add the whisked curd to the pan. Immediately add garam masala and spicy red chilli powder. Stir continuously and cook until the masala starts releasing a little oil from the sides.
Add a little hot water to adjust the consistency. Add sugar and salt to taste and mix well. Let it cook briefly and taste once to adjust the seasoning if required.
Cut the rested paneer slabs into pieces and add them to the tawa. Add chopped fresh coriander stems as well and gently toss everything so the paneer gets nicely coated with the masala. Do not cook it for too long at this stage.
Finish with freshly chopped coriander and give it a light mix.

title: MUST TRY Mawa Jalebi Recipe | बुरहानपुर की मशहूर मावा जलेबी | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
If you love indulgent Indian mithai, this Mawa Jalebi from Burhanpur is something truly special. A stunning blend of jalebi, gulab jamun and kala jamun, rich, juicy and beautifully caramelised in every bite.
I’ve shared a foolproof recipe with all the right tips and measurements so you can recreate it perfectly at home. Do make and enjoy this incredible sweet.
Happy Cooking! ❤️

Full written recipe - Mawa Jalebi

Prep Time: 5-10 minutes
Cooking Time: 25–30 minutes
Serves: 500 grams

Ingredients:
SUGAR | शक्कर 500 GRAMS
WATER | पानी 250 ML
LEMON JUICE | नींबू का रस 1 TSP
MAWA | मावा 250 GRAMS
MILK | दूध 60 ML
CORN FLOUR | कॉर्न फ्लोर 50 GRAMS
OIL | तेल AS REQUIRED (FOR FRYING)
Method:
Take a saucepan and add sugar and water to it. Place it on high flame and let the sugar dissolve completely.
Once the sugar melts and the syrup comes to a boil, cook it for just 1 minute. The syrup should remain thin, no string consistency is required.
Add lemon juice and stir well to prevent crystallisation. Switch off the flame, transfer the syrup to a bowl, and let it cool. It should remain slightly warm when using.
A quick tip to clean the sugar-coated pan or spatula, simply add hot water to the vessel and rinse. Everything will clean up easily.
Take a kadhai or a pan and add mawa to it. Keep it on low flame and mash it gently with a spatula until it softens and melts slightly. It will loosen up within seconds.
Once soft, gradually add milk while keeping the flame low. Mix continuously. The mixture should become smooth and slightly loose. Make sure to add the milk in batches, you want the batter to be semi-thick and not completely runny while it's warming.
Once the consistency is achieved, switch off the flame.
Add corn flour in three batches, mixing after each addition so no lumps form. Keep mixing gently, by this time the batter will also cool down.
The consistency at this stage should be smooth and slightly thick. Once fully cooled, strain the batter through a sieve to make it extra smooth. The batter should be in a thick dropping consistency at this stage.
Transfer the batter into a piping bag. Keep the opening ready but cut it only when you are about to fry.
Take a shallow frying pan, preferably non-stick or a well-seasoned iron pan, and add oil to about 1 inch depth. Heat the oil to 155°C.
Ensure the sugar syrup is slightly warm before frying. Keep tongs ready for flipping and a strainer tray to place the soaked jalebis later.
Cut the piping bag to create an opening of about 1–1.2 cm.
Once the oil reaches 155°C, pipe the batter directly into the oil in spiral shapes. Don’t stress too much about the perfect shape, for mawa jalebi only 1.5 circles are made per jalebi.
Keep the flame low and fry patiently. If the jalebis don’t come floating on the surface, just use a spatula to release them from the surface. 
Let the jalebi turn dark brown slowly. Check the underside before flipping. Once flipped, the second side will cook and colour faster, so maintain a low flame.
Fry until you achieve a deep dark colour, almost like kala jamun. Do not let it blacken completely otherwise the jalebis will turn out to be bitter.
Remove carefully from the oil and immediately transfer into the warm sugar syrup. Dip properly using a ladle. Let the jalebi soak in the syrup for at least 10 minutes.
After soaking, remove and place on a sieve to drain excess syrup. You can enjoy the Mawa Jalebis while they are hot or even once they cool down according to your preference.
Our rich, indulgent Mawa Jalebi is ready. You can enjoy it as it is or along with rabdi.

title: Doodh Sev Bhaji | Punjabi Dhaba Style Easy Recipe | ढाबे जैसी दूध वाली सेव भाजी | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
Sharing the recipe of Doodh Sev Bhaji, a unique and indulgent way of preparing Sev Bhaji using milk, inspired by the famous Punjabi dhabas of Maharashtra.
This version is rich, creamy, and packed with flavour, yet super easy to make with minimal prep. If you love comforting dhaba-style food, this one is definitely worth trying.
Do give this delicious creamy Sev Bhaji a try… Happy Cooking! ❤️

Meet the kitchen essential designed to keep up with your everyday cooking.
Chop faster, prep easier, and save time, without the mess 🙌

🛒 Buy on Amazon: https://amzn.in/d/iUkb3Ve
🌐 Curaa Website: https://yflhome.com/products/curaa-choplab-lite

Full written recipe - Doodh Sev Bhaji (Punjabi Dhaba Style)

Prep Time: 15–20 minutes
Cooking Time: 20–25 minutes
Serving: 3–4

Ingredients:
 
Cashew Paste
CASHEW | काजू 12–15 NOS.
HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED

Vegetable Prep
ONION | प्याज़ 1 MEDIUM SIZED
TOMATO | टमाटर 1 MEDIUM SIZED
GINGER | अदरक 2 INCH
GARLIC | लहसुन 15 CLOVES
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 3 NOS.

Gravy

OIL | तेल 2–3 TBSP
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1 TSP
MUSTARD SEEDS | राई 1/2 TSP
HING | हींग 1/4 TSP
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1 TSP 
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 2 TSP
CORIANDER POWDER | धनिया पाउडर 1 TBSP
GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला A PINCH
HOT WATER | गरम पानी A SPLASH
MILK | दूध 2 CUPS
SALT | नमक IF REQUIRED

To Finish
THICK SEV / GATHIYA | मोटी सेव / गठिया 2 CUPS / 120 GRAMS
ROASTED KASURI METHI | भुनी कसूरी मेथी A HANDFUL (CRUSHED)
GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला A PINCH
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)

Plating
FRESH CREAM | फ्रेश क्रीम AS REQUIRED
BUTTER | मक्खन AS REQUIRED
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया AS REQUIRED

Method:
Start by soaking the cashews. Add the cashews to a bowl and pour hot water over them. Cover with a lid or plate and let them soften while you prepare the rest.
For preparing the vegetables, roughly cut the onion and add it to a string chopper. Finely chop it and transfer it to a bowl. 
Next, roughly cut the tomato as well, put it into the same string chopper, chop finely, and transfer it to a separate bowl.
Roughly cut the ginger and green chillies. Add ginger, garlic, and green chillies to the chopper and finely chop them together.
Take a kadhai and heat it on high flame. Add oil and let it heat properly. 
Once hot, add cumin seeds and mustard seeds and let them crackle. 
Add the chopped ginger-garlic-chilli mixture and sauté nicely until the raw aroma disappears. 
Add hing and immediately add the chopped onions and cook the onions until they soften and just start to change colour. 
Once softened, add the chopped tomatoes. Add salt, turmeric powder, Kashmiri red chilli powder, coriander powder, and garam masala. Mix well. Add a splash of hot water and cook the masala properly until the oil separates.
Once the oil separates , add a splash of hot water and lower the flame. 
Drain the soaked cashews and grind them into a fine paste with a splash of water. Add this paste to the kadhai and mix well. 
Cook over high flame for a few minutes until the masala starts leaving the sides of the pan.
Add a little hot water and stir. Keeping the flame low, add milk and mix properly. Increase the flame and bring it to a gentle boil. The gravy will start looking creamy and slightly thick because of the cashew paste. Taste and adjust the salt if required.
Add the thick sev or gathiya and mix gently. Let it simmer briefly so the sev absorbs the flavour but doesn’t become overly soft.
Finish with the crushed roasted kasuri methi, a pinch of garam masala, and fresh coriander. Mix gently.
Our creamy, delicious Doodh Sev Bhaji is ready.
While serving, you can optionally drizzle fresh cream and add a small knob of butter, and some fresh coriander.
Serve it with tandoori roti or any bread of your choice.

title: AGLIO E OLIO PASTA IN 10-MINUTES | Quick & Easy Italian Pasta Recipe | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
The best Spaghetti Aglio e Olio recipe that comes together in just 10 minutes✨
Made with only a handful of simple ingredients, but when done the right way, with all the important tips and techniques, it turns out absolutely incredible. Light, flavourful, and beautifully balanced, this is comfort food at its finest.
Do give it a try, and as always… Happy Cooking! ❤️

Full written recipe - Spaghetti Aglio e Olio

Prep Time: 5 minutes
Cooking Time: 10–12 minutes
Serving: 2

Ingredients
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED (FOR BOILING PASTA)
SALT | नमक 2 LARGE PINCHES
GARLIC | लहसुन 4 CLOVES
PARSLEY | पार्सले A HANDFUL
EXTRA VIRGIN OLIVE OIL | एक्स्ट्रा वर्जिन ऑलिव ऑयल 2–3 TBSP
RED CHILLI FLAKES | रेड चिली फ्लेक्स 1.5 TSP
PASTA | पास्ता 200 GMS
PASTA WATER | पास्ता का पानी 2 LADLES (OR AS REQUIRED)

Method:
Add hot water as required, then add a large pinch of salt. Cover with a lid and let it come to a proper boil on high flame.
Once the water is boiling well, add the spaghetti. Let it go in as it is, do not break it. It will slowly soften and settle into the water on its own.
While the pasta is cooking, slice the garlic thinly. Chop the parsley finely and keep it ready.
After about 3–4 minutes, place a pan on a high flame and let it heat up. 
Once hot, add extra virgin olive oil. As soon as the oil warms slightly, reduce the flame to low.
Add the sliced garlic and cook it gently on a low flame until it softens. 
This is where the garlic slowly releases its flavour into the oil, so don’t rush this step. After a couple of minutes, when the garlic has turned slightly soft and just begun to change colour, add red chilli flakes and stir well.
Once the garlic turns light golden, add two ladles of pasta water into the pan and stir vigorously. The oil and water will start emulsify (coming together) and form a light sauce. The water will also stop the cooking process of the garlic and prevent it from turning brown and bitter.
By now, the pasta should be done. Using tongs, transfer it directly into the sauce. Add the chopped parsley and salt to taste. Stir everything vigorously so the pasta gets nicely coated.
If necessary, add a little more pasta water to adjust the consistency and continue stirring until the sauce appears glossy and properly emulsified.
And that's it, your delicious Spaghetti Aglio e Olio is ready in just 10 minutes, serve hot.

description:
Sharing the perfect way to make Karnataka-style Tamarind Rice, just like temple-style Puliyogare from Karnataka 🙏✨

Full writtem recipe - Tamarind Rice (Puliyogare)

Prep Time: 20 minutes
Cooking Time: 25–30 minutes
Serving: 3–4

Ingredients:
Rice
SONA MASURI RICE | सोना मसूरी चावल 1 CUP
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED
SALT | नमक A PINCH
Pudi (Spice Powder)
GROUNDNUT OIL | मूंगफली का तेल 1/2 TSP
BLACK PEPPERCORNS | साबुत काली मिर्च  1/2 TSP
MUSTARD SEEDS | राई 1/2 TSP
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 2 TSP
CORIANDER SEEDS | साबुत धनिया बीज 4 TSP
METHI SEEDS | मेथी दाना 1/2 TSP
BYADGI RED CHILLI | ब्याडगी लाल मिर्च 8–9 NOS.
GUNTUR RED CHILLI | गुंटूर लाल मिर्च 2 NOS.
CURRY LEAVES | कड़ी पत्ता A HANDFUL
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/4 TSP
HING | हींग 1/4 TSP
Gojju
TAMARIND | इमली 1/3 CUP
HOT WATER | गरम पानी 1 CUP
GROUNDNUT OIL | मूंगफली का तेल 2–3 TBSP
JAGGERY | गुड़ 1/4 CUP
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
Tempering
GROUNDNUT OIL | मूंगफली का तेल 1 TBSP
CHANA DAL | चना दाल 2 TSP
URAD DAL | उड़द दाल 2 TSP
CASHEW | काजू 8 NOS. (HALVED)
PEANUTS | मूंगफली 3 TBSP
MUSTARD SEEDS | राई 1 TSP
GUNTUR RED CHILLI | गुंटूर लाल मिर्च 1 NO.
HING | हींग 1/4 TSP
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/4 TSP
DRY COCONUT | सूखा नारियल 2 TBSP (GRATED)
CURRY LEAVES | कड़ी पत्ता 10–12 NOS.

Method:
Begin by soaking the rice. Set a colander over a bowl and rinse the rice a few times until the water runs completely clear. Once clean, let it soak while you prepare the other components.
To make the spice powder, heat groundnut oil in a pan on low flame. Add the spices and roast them gently, stirring continuously, until they release a warm aroma. Add the dried red chillies, turmeric powder, and hing. Mix once and transfer everything to a bowl. Let it cool completely, then grind into a fine powder and keep aside.
Soak the tamarind in hot water until soft. Once it loosens up, squeeze it well and strain to extract a thick pulp. Discard the fibres.
For the gojju, heat groundnut oil in a pan and add the tamarind pulp. Cook on a gentle flame until the raw sour smell fades. Add jaggery and salt, and continue cooking until the mixture reduces slightly and turns glossy. Switch off the flame and allow it to cool a little. Once warm, add the prepared spice powder and mix well.
To cook the rice, bring water to a rolling boil. Add the soaked rice and salt, cover, and cook on low flame. Check using the back of a spoon to see if any water remains at the bottom. If there is, cook for a few more minutes until fully absorbed. Once done, switch off the flame and let the rice rest. After resting, gently fluff the rice.
For the tempering, heat groundnut oil in a pan. Add chana dal and urad dal and fry until lightly golden. Add cashews and peanuts and fry until crisp. Add mustard seeds and let them crackle. Then add dried red chilli, hing, turmeric powder, dry coconut, and curry leaves. Cook briefly until aromatic.
Pour the hot tempering over the cooked rice. Add the prepared gojju and mix thoroughly. Traditionally, this is mixed by hand so the rice remains intact and evenly coated.
Our delicious Tamarind Rice is ready.

Full written recipe - Home-Style Egg Curry
Prep time: 15–20 minutes
Cooking time: 35–40 minutes
Serves: 3–4

Ingredients:
Vegetable Prep
ONION | प्याज़ 3 MEDIUM SIZED
TOMATO | टमाटर 2 MEDIUM SIZED
First Cooking (Base Masala)
OIL | तेल 1–2 TBSP
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1 TSP
HOT WATER | गरम पानी A SPLASH
GARLIC | लहसुन A HANDFUL (20 CLOVES)
GINGER | अदरक 3–4 INCH
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS.
FRESH CORIANDER STEMS | हरा धनिया डंठल A HANDFUL
SALT | नमक A PINCH
HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED
Final Cooking of Gravy
GHEE | घी 1 TBSP
OIL | तेल 1 TBSP
BAY LEAF | तेज पत्ता 2 NOS.
GREEN CARDAMOM | हरी इलायची 3 NOS.
CINNAMON | दालचीनी 1 INCH
BLACK CARDAMOM | बड़ी इलायची 1 NO.
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा A LARGE PINCH
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/2 TSP
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1 TBSP
CORIANDER POWDER | धनिया पाउडर 2 TBSP
SPICY RED CHILLI POWDER | तीखी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1 TSP
HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED

Eggs
OIL | तेल 1 TSP
BOILED EGGS | उबले अंडे 6 NOS.
SALT | नमक A PINCH
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर A PINCH
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर A PINCH
Final Seasoning
ROASTED KASURI METHI POWDER | भुनी कसूरी मेथी पाउडर A PINCH
GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला A PINCH
SALT | नमक IF REQUIRED
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED
Method:
Take onions and roughly slice them. You can also use a slicer or chop them using a chopper.
Remove the eye from the tomatoes and slice them into thick slices.
Heat a vessel on high flame and add oil. Once the oil is hot, add cumin seeds and let them crackle.
Add the sliced onions and roast on high flame, stirring continuously, until they turn golden brown and caramelized. In between, add splashes of hot water to help the onions caramelize faster without burning.
Once the onions are well browned, add garlic, ginger, green chillies, fresh coriander stems, sliced tomatoes, and salt. Mix well and cook for 2–3 minutes on high flame.
Add hot water as required, cover, and cook on low flame for 10–15 minutes until the tomatoes turn soft and mushy.
After 10–15 minutes, uncover and switch off the flame. Let the mixture cool down completely.
Once cooled, transfer the mixture to a mixer jar and grind into a fine puree using water as required. Keep the puree aside.
Heat a kadhai on high flame and add ghee and oil. Once hot, add bay leaf, green cardamom, cinnamon, black cardamom, and cumin seeds. Let the whole spices crackle well.
Add the prepared puree to the kadhai. Immediately add turmeric powder, Kashmiri red chilli powder, coriander powder, and spicy red chilli powder. Mix well.
Cook the masala on medium-high flame for 7–8 minutes, stirring continuously, until the oil separates and the gravy starts leaving the sides of the kadhai.
Adjust the consistency by adding hot water as required. The gravy should be smooth, velvety, and medium-thick, not too thin and not too thick.
Cover the kadhai, reduce the flame to low, and let the gravy simmer.
For tempering the boiled eggs, heat a non-stick pan and add oil. Once hot, add the boiled eggs along with salt, turmeric powder, and Kashmiri red chilli powder. Toss the eggs gently so they get evenly coated and lightly roasted for better texture.
Remove the eggs into a bowl and keep aside.
Check the gravy consistency and ensure the oil has released on top.
Add the tempered eggs to the gravy. Add roasted kasuri methi powder and garam masala. Mix gently.
Taste and adjust salt if required. Add chopped fresh coriander and mix well.
Your delicious, smooth and velvety Egg Curry is ready. You can enjoy it with some tandoori rotis, chapati and rice.

title: Spinach Rice with Paneer & Vegetables | Tomato Salsa Recipe | वेज पनीर पालक राइस | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
Spinach Rice with veggies & Paneer, one-pot, simple, wholesome and packed with flavour 💚 

Shop our Kettle now 😍
Amazon: https://amzn.in/d/dp8WzaL
Website: https://curaahome.com/products/beige-curaa-kettle

Spinach Rice with Veggies & Paneer
Prep time: 25–30 minutes
Cooking time: 25–30 minutes
Serves: 4
Ingredients:
Washing & Soaking
BASMATI RICE | बासमती चावल 1.5 CUPS
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED
Spinach Puree
SPINACH | पालक 150 GRAMS
SALT | नमक A LARGE PINCH
HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED
MINT | पुदीना 10–12 LEAVES
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS.
GINGER | अदरक 1 INCH
GARLIC | लहसुन 4–5 CLOVES
WATER | पानी A SPLASH
ICE CUBE | बर्फ AS REQUIRED
Veggies Prep
ONION | प्याज़ 1 MEDIUM SIZED
CAPSICUM | शिमला मिर्च 1 MEDIUM SIZED
CARROT | गाजर 1 MEDIUM SIZED
SWEET CORN | स्वीट कॉर्न 1/3 CUP
Spinach Rice
OIL | तेल 1 TBSP
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1 TSP
HOT WATER | गरम पानी A SPLASH
RED CHILLI FLAKES | रेड चिली फ्लेक्स A PINCH
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
SUGAR | शक्कर A PINCH
HOT WATER | गरम पानी 3 CUPS
SALT | नमक IF REQUIRED
Paneer (Optional)
OLIVE OIL | ऑलिव ऑयल 2 TSP
PANEER | पनीर 200 GRAMS
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
BLACK PEPPER | काली मिर्च TO TASTE
Method:
Place a strainer over a bowl, add the rice, and wash it thoroughly until the water runs clear. Once washed, add fresh water and let the rice soak while you prepare the remaining components.
Next, prepare the spinach purée. Keep two bowls ready, one with ice-cold water and the other for blanching the spinach. Add the spinach to the bowl, sprinkle salt, and pour hot water over it. Blanch the spinach for 15–20 seconds only.
Immediately transfer the spinach to the ice-cold water to stop further cooking and preserve the colour. 
Once cooled, squeeze lightly to remove excess water and transfer it to a mixer jar. 
Add remind ingredients of the puree, a splash of water, and an ice cube. Grind into a fine, smooth purée. Transfer the purée to a bowl and keep it refrigerated so the colour stays vibrant.
Now prepare the vegetables. 
Next, prep all the veggies & keep them aside. 
Heat a flat pan or wide vessel on medium–high flame. Add oil and let it heat well.
Add cumin seeds and allow them to crackle. Add the sliced onions and cook until they turn light golden brown.
Add the carrots and stir well. Cook for 2–3 minutes, then add the capsicum and sweet corn. Add red chilli flakes, salt, and sugar, and cook for another minute.
Add the spinach purée and stir well. Cook for a couple of minutes, then add hot water and mix again. Taste and adjust salt if required.
Drain the soaked rice and add it to the pan. Stir gently to distribute the rice evenly.
Tie a clean cloth around the lid, cover the pan, and cook on the lowest flame for 12 minutes.
After 12 minutes, check by inserting a spoon. Once the rice is cooked and no water remains at the bottom, switch off the flame and let it rest for at least 15 minutes.
For the optional paneer, heat a non-stick pan and add olive oil. Place the paneer slab on the plate, season with salt and black pepper, and pan grill until golden brown on both sides. Transfer to a plate, let it rest for a couple of minutes, then cut as required.
Serve the spinach rice hot with roasted tomato salsa and grilled paneer.
Spinach Rice with Veggies and Paneer is ready, serve hot and pair it with the Roasted Tomato Salsa. 

Roasted Tomato Salsa
Ingredients:
TOMATO | टमाटर 3 NOS.
ONION | प्याज़ 1 SMALL SIZED
GARLIC | लहसुन 4 CLOVES
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS.
FRESH CORIANDER STEMS | हरा धनिया डंठल A HANDFUL
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL
LEMON JUICE | नींबू का रस OF 1/2 LEMON
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
CUMIN POWDER | जीरा पाउडर 1/4 TSP
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1/2 TSP
OLIVE OIL | ऑलिव ऑयल 1 TBSP
Method:
For the roasted tomato salsa, place a roasting rack directly on high flame and roast the tomatoes until they are well charred from all sides. Transfer them to a bowl, cover, and allow them to steam for 3–4 minutes.
Once done, rinse the tomatoes under ice-cold water to remove the burnt skin. Trim the eyes and cut the tomatoes into quarters. Transfer them to an electric chopper. Roughly cut the onion into quarters and add it along with all the remaining ingredients and olive oil. Coarsely chop to make a chunky salsa.
Your Roasted Tomato Salsa is ready.

description:
Kolkata-style Chicken Bharta, rich, creamy and absolutely indulgent 😍 I’ve shared my way of making this iconic dish with all the little tips that make it truly incredible and full of flavour. Do give it a try and I'm sure you'll love it. Happy cooking!✨


Small enough to fit any kitchen, powerful enough to handle your daily prep ❤️

🌐 Curaa Website: https://curaahome.com/products/curaa-choplab-lite
🛒 Buy on Amazon: https://amzn.in/d/0dd3IQSA


Full written recipe - Chicken Bharta

Prep time: 20–25 minutes
Cooking time: 35–40 minutes
Serves: 4–5 people

Ingredients:

Cashew Paste
CASHEW | काजू 20 NOS.
HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED

Chicken Marinade
CHICKEN BREAST | चिकन ब्रेस्ट 250 GRAMS
CHICKEN THIGHS | चिकन थाइज़ 250 GRAMS
GINGER GARLIC GREEN CHILLI PASTE | अदरक लहसुन हरी मिर्च पेस्ट 1 TBSP
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/4 TSP
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1 TSP
LEMON JUICE | नींबू का रस OF 1/2 LEMON
MUSTARD OIL | सरसों का तेल 2 TSP

Cooking Chicken
OIL | तेल 1 TBSP

Masala Base
ONION | प्याज़ 4–5 MEDIUM SIZED
OIL | तेल 2 TBSP
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा A LARGE PINCH
HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED
GARLIC | लहसुन 15 CLOVES
GINGER | अदरक 2 INCH
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS.
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/2 TSP
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 2 TSP
CORIANDER POWDER | धनिया पाउडर 1 TBSP
GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला 1/2 TSP
HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED

Finishing
FRESH CREAM | फ्रेश क्रीम 2–3 TBSP
BUTTER | मक्खन 1 TBSP
FRESH CORIANDER STEMS | हरा धनिया डंठल A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
LEMON JUICE | नींबू का रस OF 1/2 LEMON
ROASTED KASURI METHI POWDER | भुना कसूरी मेथी पाउडर A LARGE PINCH
GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला A PINCH
HOT WATER | गरम पानी A SPLASH (IF REQUIRED)
HONEY | शहद 1 TSP (OPTIONAL)
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS. (SLICED)

Method:
Start with soaking the cashews. For that, take cashews in a bowl, add hot water, and let them soak for a while until you prepare the rest of the ingredients.
For the chicken, slice it slightly thin so it cooks faster and stays juicy. Add it to a bowl and mix with ginger-garlic-green chilli paste, salt, turmeric powder, Kashmiri red chilli powder, lemon juice, and mustard oil. Mix it well and let it marinate.
Heat a tawa on high flame and add oil to it. Once hot, place the chicken pieces and cook on medium flame. Let it cook on one side for a couple minutes or until it is nicely cooked, then flip and cook the other side for another couple of minutes. Once done, transfer it to a bowl and let it rest for a few minutes so that it retains its juices.
Now, start with chopping the onions. Heat a pan, add oil, and once it’s hot, add cumin seeds. As soon as they crackle, add the chopped onions and cook on medium-high flame until golden brown. If the onions start to stick to the pan, add a small splash of hot water and continue cooking.
Chop the garlic, ginger, and green chillies slightly coarse and add them to the pan. Cook for a couple of minutes until the raw flavour goes away.
Now add turmeric powder, Kashmiri red chilli powder, coriander powder, garam masala, and a splash of hot water. Mix well and cook until the masala starts to release oil. 
Add another splash of hot water, cover, reduce the flame, and let it cook for a few minutes. By the time you can make the cashew paste for that, drain the soaked cashews and grind them with water into a smooth paste and add it to the pan.
Now shred the cooked chicken using your hands. Keep it slightly chunky for that proper bharta texture.
Add the shredded chicken to the pan and mix it well. Along with fresh cream, butter, coriander stems, lemon juice, roasted Kasuri methi powder, and garam masala. Mix everything well.
Check the consistency. If the bharta feels a bit dry, add a small splash of hot water and adjust the consistency. Taste and adjust the salt if required. If you like a slight balance in flavour, add honey (optional) and mix well.
Finish with fresh coriander and sliced green chillies.
Our delicious chicken bharta is ready. You can enjoy this with tandoori roti or any bread of your choice or even rice.
