title: Maharastrian Masale Bhat Recipe | Easy Veg Pulao | महाराष्ट्रियन शादीओ वाला मसाले भात | Sanjyot Keer
description:
Maharashtrian Masale Bhat, just like the one famously served at weddings 💛 A super easy recipe with perfect measurements so it turns out flavourful, aromatic, and absolutely delicious every single time. Do give it a try and I'm sure you will love this recipe. Happy cooking! ✨

Our Wooden Masala Dani blends timeless craftsmanship with everyday functionality, keeping your spices fresh and your kitchen elegant.❤️

🛒 Available now:
Website: https://curaahome.com/products/curaa-wooden-masala-dani
Amazon: https://amzn.in/d/8ixtD2f


Full written recipe - Maharashtrian Masale Bhat
Prep time: 15–20 minutes
Cooking time: 25–30 minutes
Serves: 4–5

Ingredients:
Washing & Soaking
KOLUM RICE | कोलम चावल 1.5 CUPS
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED
Vegetables Prep
CAULIFLOWER | फूलगोभी 1/2 SMALL SIZED
IVY GOURD | टिंडली 8–10 NOS.
POTATO | आलू 1 MEDIUM SIZED
GREEN PEAS | हरे मटर 1/3 CUP
Spice Mix
CINNAMON | दालचीनी 1 INCH
BLACK PEPPERCORNS | काली मिर्च 5–6 NOS.
CLOVES | लौंग 3 NOS.
Ginger–Chilli Paste
GINGER | अदरक 1 INCH
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS.
Cooking
OIL | तेल 2 TBSP
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1 TSP
MUSTARD SEEDS | राई 1/2 TSP
HING | हींग 1/4 TSP
BAY LEAF | तेज पत्ता 2 SMALL NOS.
CURRY LEAVES | कड़ी पत्ता 8–10 NOS.
CASHEWS | काजू 7–8 NOS. (HALVED)
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/4 TSP
GODA MASALA | गोडा मसाला 1.5 TBSP
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
HOT WATER | गरम पानी 3 CUPS
JAGGERY | गुड़ 1 TSP
Garnish while serving
FRESH COCONUT | नारियल
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया
Method:
Start by washing the rice. Place a strainer over a bowl, add the kolum rice, and wash it 2–3 times until the water runs clear. Once washed, soak it in fresh water and keep it aside. I would recommend not using basmati for this preparation, use any short or medium grain rice for best results.
Meanwhile, prepare all the vegetables. Break the cauliflower into small florets. Wash the ivy gourd, trim the top and bottom, and cut it into quarters. Cut the potato into dices. Rinse all the chopped vegetables lightly in water and keep them ready.
Now make the spice mix. Add cinnamon, black peppercorns, and cloves to a mixer grinder and grind into a coarse powder.
Next, in a mortar and pestle, crush ginger and green chilli into a coarse paste.
Heat a wide vessel on medium-high flame. Add oil and let it heat up. Add cumin seeds, mustard seeds, hing, and bay leaves and let them crackle.
Add all the prepared vegetables along with green peas. Add the ginger-green chilli paste and the coarse spice powder. Mix everything well and cook for a minute.
Now add curry leaves, cashews, turmeric powder, goda masala, and salt. Mix well and cook on medium-high to high flame for 2–3 minutes.
Cover the vessel and cook on low flame for 2–3 minutes so the vegetables steam slightly. After 2 minutes, open the lid and give it a quick stir.
Add hot water and jaggery, and stir well until the jaggery dissolves completely. Taste the water at this stage and adjust salt if required.
Increase the flame to high and bring everything to a proper boil. Tie a clean cloth around the lid and keep it ready.
Now drain the soaked rice and add it to the boiling water. Stir gently once and bring it back to a boil.
Cover and cook on low flame for 15 minutes.
After 15 minutes, open the lid and check the bottom using the back of a spoon. If there is still water left, cover again and cook for a couple more minutes until it gets absorbed.
Once done, switch off the flame and let the masale bhat rest for 15 minutes before serving.
Maharashtrian Masale Bhat is ready to be served. While serving, fluff up the rice gently using a spatula and add fresh coriander, grated fresh coconut and some more ghee.

title: THE BEST Chicken Tikka Roll | Rumali Roti on Tawa | चिकन टिक्का रूमाली रोल | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
Power meets precision in every blend!
Say hello to the BLP 2.0, now available on Amazon, your new kitchen superstar that makes cooking easier, faster, and way more fun 🥰🚀

Happy shopping! 🛍️ 
🛒 Buy on Amazon: https://amzn.in/d/00oVb9g

Chicken Tikka Roll (Homemade Tawa Rumali Roti)

Prep time: 30 minutes (1 hour of resting time for the dough)
Cooking time: 1 hour 
Serves: 4-5 people

Ingredients:
Dough (Rumali Roti)
REFINED FLOUR | मैदा 1.5 CUPS
WHEAT FLOUR | गेहूं का आटा 1/2 CUP
SALT | नमक A PINCH
SUGAR | शक्कर A PINCH
OIL | तेल 1 TBSP
MILK | दूध 200 ML (LUKEWARM, ADD GRADUALLY)

Method:
Dough Preparation
In a large bowl, add refined flour, wheat flour, salt, sugar, and oil. Mix lightly. Gradually add lukewarm milk and bring everything together to form a soft dough.
Transfer the dough to a work surface and knead using a push-and-stretch motion until smooth and elastic. Shape into a round dough ball, place it in a bowl, coat lightly with oil, cover with a damp cloth, and rest for at least 1 hour.
While the dough is resting, prepare the remaining components of the roll.

Rumali Roti Cooking
Divide the rested dough into 30–35 g pedas (approximately 33 g). Apply a little oil to each peda and rest for 10–15 minutes.
Heat a tawa on medium flame until hot but not smoking. Flatten a peda and roll it very thin using dry flour.
Lightly oil the tawa, dust it with refined flour, and wipe off any excess. Place the roti on high flame. Once bubbles appear, flip quickly. Cook both sides briefly until just cooked and soft.
Stack three pedas. The oil and flour is applied on first 2 pedhas and then the 3rd stacked on the other two stack them together and gently roll into a single roti. Cook on a hot tawa only until it is partially cooked and the layers begin to show—do not cook it fully at this stage. Remove from the tawa, carefully separate the three layers, then place each roti back on the tawa and cook fully on both sides.


Chicken Tikka
Ingredients:
CHICKEN | चिकन 500 GRAMS (BREAST)
SALT | नमक A PINCH
GINGER GARLIC GREEN CHILLI PASTE | अदरक लहसुन हरी मिर्च पेस्ट 1 TBSP
LEMON JUICE | नींबू का रस OF 1/2 LEMON
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर A PINCH
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1/2 TSP
Second Marinade
MUSTARD OIL | सरसों का तेल 1 TSP
OIL | तेल 1 TSP
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 2 TBSP
GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला 1/2 TSP
SALT | नमक A PINCH
BLACK SALT | काला नमक 1/4 TSP
GINGER GARLIC GREEN CHILLI PASTE | अदरक लहसुन हरी मिर्च पेस्ट 2 TSP
CURD | दही 2 TBSP
GHEE | घी 1 TSP( FOR COOKING)

Method:
Slice the chicken breast diagonally into thin, flat strips and transfer them to a bowl. Add all the ingredients of the first marinade, mix well, and set aside.
In another bowl, whisk together all the ingredients of the second marinade. Add this to the chicken and mix thoroughly.
For dhungar, heat a charcoal piece until red hot. Place it in a small bowl over the chicken, add ghee, cover immediately, and allow it to smoke for 3–4 minutes. Remove the charcoal and mix well.
Soak satay sticks in water. Fold each chicken strip and skewer it through the folds. Keep aside.
Heat the same tawa until moderately hot. Add ghee and place the chicken skewers on it.
Cook on medium-high flame, turning continuously, until evenly charred and cooked through. The chicken will get cooked perfectly, firm but juicy from the inside. I always prefer to cook tikkas at home like this, if you directly cut cubes then they won't be as juicy

Green Chutney
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया 1 CUP (PACKED)
MINT | पुदीना 1/2 CUP (PACKED)
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 7–8 NOS.
GARLIC | लहसुन 2–3 CLOVES
GINGER | अदरक 1/2 INCH
TAMARIND | इमली 1 SMALL PIECE
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
BLACK SALT | काला नमक A PINCH

Full written recipe - Healthy Chilli Garlic Paneer Sandwich

Prep time: 25–30 minutes
Cooking time: 10–12 minutes

Chilli Garlic Paneer Sauce
PANEER | पनीर 150 GRAMS
THICK CURD | गाढ़ा दही 150 GRAMS
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च 5 NOS. (DESEEDED & SOAKED)
GARLIC | लहसुन 7–8 CLOVES
HONEY | शहद 1 TBSP
LEMON ZEST | नींबू का छिलका OF 1 LEMON
LEMON JUICE | नींबू का रस OF 1/2 LEMON
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
BLACK PEPPER | काली मिर्च TO TASTE (FRESHLY CRUSHED)
OREGANO | ऑरेगैनो 1/2 TSP
Method:
Add paneer, thick curd, soaked Kashmiri red chillies, garlic, honey, lemon zest, lemon juice, salt, black pepper, and oregano to a mixer jar.
Grind into a smooth, fine sauce.
This is the healthy sandwich/burger sauce that I made which tastes amazing and is super healthy. It's a very versatile sauce and you can use in sandwiches, wraps, burgers and even add it over rice.


Sandwich Chutney
Ingredients:
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया 1 BUNCH
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 10 NOS.
GARLIC | लहसुन 10 CLOVES
ROASTED CHANA DAL | भुनी हुई चना दाल 2 TBSP
CHAAT MASALA | चाट मसाला A LARGE PINCH
SALT | नमक A PINCH
ICE CUBE | बर्फ 1–2 NOS.
WATER | पानी A SPLASH
Ingredients:
Veggies Prep
CABBAGE | पत्ता गोभी 1/4 NO.
CAPSICUM | शिमला मिर्च 1 MEDIUM SIZED
CARROT | गाजर 1 MEDIUM SIZED
ONION | प्याज़ 1 MEDIUM SIZED
Method:
Add fresh coriander, green chillies, garlic, roasted chana dal, chaat masala, salt, ice cubes, and water to a mixer jar.
Grind into a thick, smooth chutney. Taste and adjust salt if required.
Sandwich chutney is ready.

Chilli Garlic Paneer Sandwich 
Ingredient 

Veggies Prep
CABBAGE | पत्ता गोभी 1/4 NO.
CAPSICUM | शिमला मिर्च 1 MEDIUM SIZED
CARROT | गाजर 1 MEDIUM SIZED
ONION | प्याज़ 1 MEDIUM SIZED

Paneer (For Filling)
PANEER | पनीर 250 GRAMS
LEMON JUICE | नींबू का रस 1 TSP
SALT | नमक A PINCH
BLACK PEPPER | काली मिर्च TO TASTE (FRESHLY CRUSHED)
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1/2 TSP

Assembly & Toasting
ZERO MAIDA BREAD SLICES | ज़ीरो मैदा ब्रेड AS REQUIRED
SOFT BUTTER | मक्खन AS REQUIRED
GREEN CHUTNEY | हरी चटनी AS REQUIRED
PANEER | पनीर AS REQUIRED
Method:
Thinly shred the cabbage and transfer it to a large bowl.
Cut the capsicum by slicing off the sides and removing the core. Place the skin side down and cut into thin juliennes. Add to the same bowl.
Peel the carrot, slice it diagonally into thin pieces, stack them, and cut into juliennes. Transfer to the bowl.
Slice the onion into thin slices and add it to the bowl with the other vegetables.
Cut the paneer into small dice and transfer to a bowl.
Add lemon juice, salt, freshly crushed black pepper, and Kashmiri red chilli powder. Mix gently so the paneer does not break. Keep aside.
Once you have prepared the chilli garlic sauce and the sandwich chutney, you can prepare the sandwich.
Add the prepared chilli garlic sauce to the bowl of mixed vegetables gradually and mix well until evenly coated. This mixture can also be used as a salad or burger filling.

Assembly:
Take zero-maida bread slices and apply soft butter evenly on one side of each slice.
Apply green chutney on the inner side of both slices.
On one slice, spread a layer of the prepared coleslaw. Add the marinated paneer evenly over it. Cover with the second bread slice.
Toasting:
Heat a tawa on medium flame. Apply a little soft butter on the tawa and place the sandwich on it.
Place a plate on top and gently press to ensure even toasting. Check once in between.
Once the bottom side is golden and crisp, apply soft butter on the top, flip carefully, place the plate again, and toast until crisp on the second side.
Remove the sandwich once both sides are evenly toasted and golden.
Healthy Chilli Garlic Paneer Grilled Sandwich is ready.

title: Vegetable Bajra Khichdi | Easy & Healthy Millet Recipe | मसाला बाजरा खिचड़ी | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
This Vegetable Bajra Khichdi is an easy, tasty, and healthy meal made with loads of veggies and nutrient-packed bajra.✨
It’s a comforting and delicious way to incorporate millets into your daily diet, without any fuss.
The full written recipe is mentioned below, do give it a try. Happy cooking! ❤️

Crafted with care. Rooted in tradition. Designed to last 🤎 Our Wooden Masala Dani blends timeless craftsmanship with everyday functionality, keeping your spices fresh and your kitchen elegant.
Add a touch of heritage to your cooking ❤️

🛒 Available now:
Website: https://curaahome.com/products/curaa-wooden-masala-dani
Amazon: https://amzn.in/d/8ixtD2f

Full written recipe - Vegetable Bajra Khichdi

Prep time: 10-15 minutes (plus soaking time)
Cooking time: 35–40 minutes
Serves: 4–5 people

Ingredients
Pressure Cooking
BAJRA | बाजरा 1 CUP
YELLOW MOONG DAL | पीली मूंग दाल 1/2 CUP
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED
WATER | पानी 5 CUPS
SALT | नमक A PINCH
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/2 TSP
GHEE | घी 1/2 TBSP

Vegetable Prep
ONION | प्याज़ 2 MEDIUM SIZED
TOMATO | टमाटर 1 LARGE
GINGER | अदरक 2 INCH
GARLIC | लहसुन 7–8 CLOVES
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 3 NOS.
FRENCH BEANS | फ्रेंच बीन्स 8–10 NOS.
CARROT | गाजर 1 MEDIUM SIZED
POTATO | आलू 1 MEDIUM SIZED
GREEN PEAS | हरे मटर 1/2 CUP

Tadka & Final Cooking
GHEE | घी 2 TBSP
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1/2 TSP
DRIED RED CHILLI | सूखी लाल मिर्च 1 NO.
HING | हींग 1/4 TSP
CURRY LEAVES | कड़ी पत्ता 8–10 NOS.
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1 TSP
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1 TSP
CORIANDER POWDER | धनिया पाउडर 2 TSP
HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED
GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला A PINCH
ROASTED KASURI METHI POWDER | भुनी कसूरी मेथी पाउडर A PINCH
LEMON JUICE | नींबू का रस OF 1/2 LEMON
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)

Method:
Wash and soak the bajra for at least 6–8 hours or overnight. After soaking, discard the water and keep the bajra aside.
Wash the yellow moong dal thoroughly in a sieve using water as required.
Transfer the soaked bajra to a pressure cooker. Optionally, pulse the soaked bajra in a mixer using pulse mode to lightly break the grains, then add it back to the cooker. Doing this step allows the bajra to cook quicker and also aids in digestion.
Add washed yellow moong dal to the cooker along with water, salt, turmeric powder, and ghee. Mix well, cover with the lid, and pressure cook on high flame for 1 whistle, then on medium flame for 6–7 whistles. 
While the khichdi is cooking, prepare the vegetables. Slice the onions and cut the tomato. Roughly chop ginger, garlic, and green chillies and crush them coarsely. Cut the french beans into large dice. Peel and cut the carrot. Peel and dice the potato. Keep green peas ready.
Once the whistles are done, switch off the flame and let the pressure release naturally.
Once the pressure releases, open the lid and stir well. Keep the cooked bajra and dal mixture aside. If the bajra and dal is still undercooked, you can cook it for 2-3 more whistles.
For the tadka, heat a kadhai on high flame and add ghee. Once hot, add cumin seeds, dried red chilli, and hing, and let them crackle.
Add the sliced onions and cook until they start picking up light colour. Add curry leaves, then add the potato and stir well. Cover and cook on low flame for 5 minutes, stirring once in between, until the potatoes are cooked through.
Uncover and add green peas, carrot, french beans, and tomato. Add salt, turmeric powder, Kashmiri red chilli powder, and coriander powder. Stir well and add a splash of hot water so the spices do not burn. Cover and cook on low flame for 4–5 minutes until the vegetables are cooked.
Once the vegetables are cooked, add the cooked bajra khichdi mixture to the kadhai and mix well.
Adjust the consistency by adding hot water as required and mix again. Taste and adjust salt if required.
Add garam masala, roasted kasuri methi powder, lemon juice, and fresh coriander. Stir well and cook briefly until everything is well combined.
Vegetable Bajra Khichdi is ready, serve hot with dahi, pickle and papad.

title: Paneer in Peri Peri Sauce | Butter Coriander Rice | पेरी पेरी पनीर और कोरिएंडर राइस | Sanjyot Keer
description:
This Paneer in Peri Peri Sauce with Butter Coriander Rice is an easy, flavour-packed meal combo. 🍽️✨
I've shared how to make peri peri sauce is made from scratch with simple ingredients for a beautiful smoky flavour and I have paired it with sautéed paneer, veggies, and fragrant butter coriander rice.
Do give this delicious meal a try. Happy cooking! ❤️

Our BlendLab - Designed to make everyday kitchen tasks faster, easier, and more enjoyable
🛒 Buy on Amazon: https://amzn.in/d/eAjiCdi
🌐 Curaa Website: https://curaahome.com/products/curaa-blendlab

Prep time: 25–30 minutes
Cooking time: 25–30 minutes
Serves: 3–4
Ingredients:
Peri Peri Sauce Base 
TOMATO | टमाटर 3 NOS.
RED BELL PEPPER | लाल शिमला मिर्च 1 MEDIUM SIZED
ONION | प्याज़ 1 MEDIUM SIZED
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS.
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च 5–6 NOS. (DESEEDED & SOAKED)
GARLIC | लहसुन 3 CLOVES
CASHEW | काजू 3–4 NOS. (SOAKED)
Paneer
PANEER | पनीर 300 GRAMS
OIL | तेल 2 TSP
Vegetables (Tossing)
CAPSICUM | शिमला मिर्च 1 MEDIUM SIZED
ONION | प्याज़ 1 MEDIUM SIZED
Final Cooking
OLIVE OIL | ऑलिव ऑयल 1 TBSP
GINGER GARLIC PASTE | अदरक लहसुन पेस्ट 1 TBSP
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
BLACK PEPPER | काली मिर्च A PINCH (FRESHLY CRUSHED)
HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED
HONEY | शहद 1 TSP
LEMON JUICE | नींबू का रस OF 1/2 LEMON
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)

Method:
Place a grill rack on high flame. Add tomatoes, red bell pepper, onion, and green chillies. Roast them directly on flame until well charred on all sides.
Remove the green chillies first once they are charred. Continue roasting the remaining vegetables until done.
Transfer all roasted vegetables to a bowl, cover, and allow them to steam for 5 minutes.
After 5 minutes, uncover and transfer the tomatoes and bell pepper to ice-cold water. Peel off the skins easily.
Trim the sides of the bell pepper and remove the core. Roughly chop the tomatoes. Cut the onion into quarters and remove the outer charred layers.
Add all the roasted vegetables along with soaked Kashmiri red chillies, garlic, and soaked cashews to a mixer jar. Grind into a smooth, fine purée. Keep aside.
Cut the paneer into two large slabs.
Heat a non-stick pan and add oil. Place the paneer slabs in the pan and cook on medium flame until golden brown on one side.
Flip and cook the other side until golden brown.
Remove the paneer slabs onto a chopping board and allow them to cool slightly. Cut into cubes and transfer to a plate.
Cut the capsicum into triangular pieces by removing the core. Cut the onion into quarters and separate into petals.
Heat the same pan used for paneer on high flame. Add the vegetables and toss briefly until lightly charred but still crunchy.
Transfer the tossed vegetables to a bowl and keep aside.
Heat a kadhai on high flame and add olive oil. Once hot, add ginger garlic paste and sauté for 1–2 minutes.
Add the prepared peri peri sauce purée, salt, and freshly crushed black pepper. Mix well, cover, and cook on low flame for 3–4 minutes.
After 5 minutes, uncover and stir. Adjust the consistency using hot water as required. The gravy should be thick, not runny.
Taste and adjust salt if required. Add honey and lemon juice, mix well, and taste again.
Add the paneer cubes, tossed vegetables, and chopped fresh coriander. Mix gently until everything is well coated in the sauce.
Paneer in peri peri sauce is ready.

Butter Coriander Rice
BUTTER | मक्खन 1 TBSP
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A LARGE HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
COOKED RICE | पके हुए चावल 4 CUPS OR AS REQUIRED
Method:
Heat a frying pan and add butter. Allow it to melt.
Add chopped fresh coriander and stir briefly.
Add cooked rice and toss gently until the rice is evenly coated with butter and coriander.
Butter coriander rice is ready.
Serve Paneer in Peri Peri Sauce hot with Butter Coriander Rice.

title: Kathiyawadi Kadhi Recipe | Dhaba Style Spicy Kadhi | कथियवादी कढ़ी बनाने का तरीका | Chef Sanjyot keer
description:
This is a simple, homestyle Kathiyawadi Kadhi, spicy, garlicky, tangy and perfect for chilly winter days. ✨
Enjoy it hot with bajra rotla, white butter, pickle, garlic chutney and rice for a truly comforting meal.
Do give this recipe a try. Happy cooking! ❤️🍽️

Crafted with care. Rooted in tradition. Designed to last 🤎 Our Wooden Masala Dani blends timeless craftsmanship with everyday functionality, keeping your spices fresh and your kitchen elegant.

🛒 Available now:
Website: https://curaahome.com/products/curaa-wooden-masala-dani
Amazon: https://amzn.in/d/8OKpGBO


Full written recipe - Kathiyawadi Kadhi

Prep time: 10–15 minutes
Cooking time: 25–30 minutes
Serves: 3–4
Ingredients
Kadhi Base:
SOUR CURD | खट्टा दही 1 CUP
BESAN | बेसन 1/4 CUP
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/2 TSP
CORIANDER POWDER | धनिया पाउडर 1/2 TSP
SPICY RED CHILLI POWDER | तीखी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1/2 TSP
CUMIN POWDER | जीरा पाउडर 1/2 TSP
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
WATER | पानी 3 CUPS
Garlic–Chilli Paste:
GARLIC | लहसुन 10–12 CLOVES
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS.
Cooking (Main Tempering):
GROUNDNUT OIL | मूंगफली का तेल 1–2 TBSP
MUSTARD SEEDS | राई 1/2 TSP
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1/2 TSP
HING | हींग 1/4 TSP
FENUGREEK SEEDS | मेथी दाना A PINCH
BAY LEAF | तेज पत्ता 2 NOS.
CINNAMON STICK | दालचीनी 1 INCH
CURRY LEAVES | कड़ी पत्ता 10 NOS.
Simmering:
CURRY LEAVES | कड़ी पत्ता 2 SPRIGS
Final Tadka:
GHEE | घी 1 TBSP
DRIED RED CHILLI | सूखी लाल मिर्च 2 NOS.
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1/2 TSP
Finishing:
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
Method:
Place a strainer over a bowl. Add sour curd and besan to the strainer and sieve them together to remove any lumps.
Add turmeric powder, coriander powder, spicy red chilli powder, cumin powder, and salt. Whisk well. Add water and mix thoroughly to form a smooth kadhi mixture. Keep aside.
In a mortar and pestle, add garlic and green chillies and crush them coarsely to make a rough paste. Keep aside.
Heat a kadhai on medium flame and add groundnut oil. Once the oil is hot, add mustard seeds and cumin seeds. Let them crackle, then add hing, fenugreek seeds, bay leaves, cinnamon stick, and curry leaves. Sauté briefly until aromatic.
Stir the kadhi mixture once again and slowly add it to the kadhai, stirring continuously to prevent curdling. Mix well.
Bring the kadhi to a gentle boil. Once it starts boiling, reduce the flame to medium and add curry leaf sprigs. Let the kadhi simmer for 5–7 minutes.
After simmering, cover the kadhai and cook the kadhi for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally. The kadhi should develop a rich colour and aroma. Adjust the thickness as per preference.
Once cooked, discard the curry leaf sprigs and bay leaves for easier serving. Taste and adjust salt if required.
For the final tadka, heat ghee in a small pan. Add dried red chillies and Kashmiri red chilli powder, and immediately pour this tempering over the kadhi.
Finish by adding chopped fresh coriander.
Kathiyawadi Kadhi is ready. Serve hot with bajra rotla, rice, or pulao.

title: Winter Special Amritsari Fish Fry Recipe | Chaat Masala & Chutney | अमृतसरी फिश फ्राई | Sanjyot Keer
description:
Sharing the authentic Amritsari Fish Fry recipe, made the perfect way with its signature chaat masala sprinkled on top and the special green chutney served alongside. 🐟✨
Do give this recipe a try. Happy cooking! ❤️🍽️

Our BlendLab - Designed to make everyday kitchen tasks faster, easier, and more enjoyable
🛒 Buy on Amazon: https://amzn.in/d/eAjiCdi
🌐 Curaa Website: https://curaahome.com/products/curaa-blendlab

Amritsari Fish Fry
Prep time: 20 minutes
Cooking time: 15–20 minutes
Serves: 3–4

Ingredients
SINGHARA FISH | सिंघाड़ा मछली 500 GRAMS
GARLIC CLOVES | लहसुन की कलियाँ A HANDFUL
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED
First Marinade
SALT | नमक A PINCH
HING | हींग A PINCH
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/4 TSP
Besan Batter
BESAN | बेसन 3 TBSP
CORN FLOUR | कॉर्न फ्लोर 1 TBSP
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/4 TSP
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर A PINCH
SALT | नमक A PINCH
AJWAIN | अजवाइन 1/2 TSP
MUSTARD OIL | सरसों का तेल AS REQUIRED (FOR FRYING)

Method
Use the tail section of the singhara fish with skin intact. Do not remove the skin, as traditionally the skin is kept on for Amritsari Fish Fry.
Cut the fish just behind the tail section to separate it. You will see a natural centre line, cut along this line to divide the piece into three sections. Further cut these into chunky pieces. Repeat until all the fish is cut into similar-sized pieces.
Add garlic cloves to a mixer jar with a splash of water and grind into a fine paste.
Remove 1 TBSP of this paste into a bowl for the first marinade. To the remaining paste in the jar, add water as required and blend to make a thin garlic water. Keep aside.
To the reserved garlic paste, add salt, hing, and turmeric powder. Mix well to form the first marinade.
Coat the fish pieces lightly and evenly with this marinade using gentle hands. Set aside for 10 minutes.
In a bowl, add besan, corn flour, turmeric powder, Kashmiri red chilli powder, and salt.
Gradually add the prepared garlic water little by little to make a thick batter. The batter should be yellow in colour and very thick so it coats lightly and does not absorb excess oil.
Crush ajwain between your palms and add it to the batter. Mix well. Allow the batter to rest briefly.
Transfer the marinated fish to a large bowl. Ensure all pieces are evenly coated with the first marinade.
Heat mustard oil in a kadhai until it starts smoking. Switch off the flame and allow the oil to cool slightly.
Once the oil has cooled, coat the fish lightly with the besan batter. The coating should be thin and even.
Heat the oil again to medium-high heat (approximately 170°C). Fry the fish in batches without overcrowding. Fry for 3–4 minutes until the fish is cooked through.
Remove the fish and let it rest for 10–15 minutes. Increase the oil temperature to approximately 185°C.
Flash-fry the fish again for 30 seconds until extremely crisp. Remove and drain excess oil.
The Amritsari Fish Fry is ready, crispy on the outside and juicy on the inside.
While serving, add a generous sprinkle of the chaat masala

Chaat Masala
Ingredients:
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1 TBSP
CORIANDER SEEDS | धनिया के बीज 1/2 TBSP
BLACK PEPPERCORNS | काली मिर्च 1 TSP
CAROM SEEDS | अजवाइन 1/2 TSP
CINNAMON | दालचीनी 1/2 INCH
CLOVES | लौंग 2 NOS.
SALT | नमक 1/2 TBSP
BLACK SALT | काला नमक 1 TBSP
GINGER POWDER | सोंठ पाउडर 1 TSP
ASAFOETIDA | हींग 1/2 TSP
DRY MANGO POWDER | अमचूर पाउडर 1 TBSP
YELLOW CHILLI POWDER | पीली मिर्च पाउडर 1/2 TSP
Method:
Heat a pan on low flame. Add cumin seeds, coriander seeds, black peppercorns, carom seeds, cinnamon, and cloves. Dry roast gently on low flame until aromatic.
Switch off the flame and allow the spices to cool completely. Transfer to a spice grinder, add salt, black salt, ginger powder, asafoetida, dry mango powder, and yellow chilli powder. Grind into a fine powder. Set aside.

Green Chutney
Ingredients:
MINT | पुदीना A LARGE HANDFUL
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL
ONION | प्याज़ 1 SMALL SIZED
GARLIC | लहसुन 7–8 CLOVES
GINGER | अदरक 1 INCH
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 5–6 NOS.
TAMARIND | इमली A SMALL PIECE
BLACK SALT | काला नमक 1/2 TSP
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
ICE CUBE | बर्फ का टुकड़ा 1 NO.
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED
Method:
Add mint, fresh coriander, onion, garlic, ginger, green chillies, tamarind, black salt, salt, ice cube, and water to a mixer jar. Grind into a coarse chutney. Keep chilled until serving.

title: Nolen Gurer Rosogolla | Jaggery Rasgulla Recipe with Easy Tips | गुड़ का रसगुल्ला | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
This Nolen Gurer Rosogolla is one of my favourite winter desserts, soft, delicate, and made with a beautiful ingredient called Nolen Gur which means New or Fresh Jaggery. ✨
I’ve shared the perfect recipe with all the measurements and tips to help you achieve the softest, spongiest rosogollas.
Do give it a try. Happy cooking! ❤️🍽️

Precision that makes every recipe perfect.
Accurate measurements, effortless use, and a sleek design made for everyday cooking ❤️

Full written recipe - Nolen Gurer Rosogolla
Prep time: 10 minutes
Cooking time: 1 hour
Serves: 12–14 rosogolla

Ingredients:
Nolen Gur Syrup (For Soaking)
NOLEN GUR | नोलन गुर 1 CUP
WATER | पानी 2 CUPS
Chenna
MILK | दूध 1 LITRE (FULL FAT)
VINEGAR | सिरका 3 TBSP
WATER | पानी 3 TBSP
ICE COLD WATER | बर्फ़ वाला ठंडा पानी AS REQUIRED
Sugar Syrup (For Boiling)
SUGAR | शक्कर 1.5 CUPS
WATER | पानी 7 CUPS

Method:

Add the nolen gur and water to a saucepan. Place it on low flame and gently heat, stirring occasionally, just until the gur melts completely. Do not boil or overcook. Transfer the melted syrup to a bowl and keep aside; this will be used later for soaking the rosogollas.
Pour the milk into a heavy-bottomed vessel and place it on medium-high flame. Stir continuously and bring it to a gentle simmer.
While the milk heats, mix vinegar and water in a bowl. Set up a strainer lined with a double-layered muslin cloth over a bowl, and keep ice-cold water ready.
Once the milk reaches a simmer, turn off the flame and stir for 2–3 minutes to slightly cool it. Gradually add the vinegar-water mixture while gently stirring, adding little by little until the milk curdles completely and the whey separates.
Immediately strain the chenna and rinse thoroughly with ice-cold water to remove the vinegar taste and stop further cooking.
Once cooled, squeeze the muslin cloth to remove as much excess moisture as possible. If needed, loosen the chenna slightly and squeeze again. The chenna should be moist but not wet or dripping. Transfer it to a clean plate or paraat placed over a damp cloth so it doesn’t slip.
Using the base of your palm, rub the chenna against the plate. Initially it will appear grainy. Continue rubbing, gathering it back together, and repeating the process until it becomes smooth and creamy, comes together easily, and does not stick to the plate or your hand.
Test by rolling a small portion into a smooth, crack free ball. Once achieved, gather all the chenna into a dough like ball and cover with a damp cloth.
Divide the chenna into equal portions of 20 grams each. Roll each portion gently between your palms with light pressure to form smooth, crack-free round balls. Cover the shaped rosogolla with a damp muslin cloth.
In a wide kadhai, add sugar and water. Place on high flame and allow the sugar to dissolve completely. Cover and bring the syrup to a rolling boil.
Once boiling vigorously, gently add the rosogolla one by one. Let them boil uncovered for 2–3 minutes until they puff slightly and set their structure. Cover and cook on high flame for 12–15 minutes, ensuring the syrup maintains a rolling boil throughout.
After 12 minutes, uncover and check the rosogolla should be well puffed and spongy.
Using a ladle, remove the rosogolla and immediately transfer them into cold water. Let them cool completely; once they sink, they are fully cooked.
Once cooled, gently squeeze each rosogolla once to remove excess water without compressing them. Transfer them to the prepared nolen gur syrup and allow to soak for at least 5–6 hours or overnight in the fridge.
Your soft, spongy Nolen Gurer Roshogulla is ready.

title: My Secret Mutton Curry Recipe with Gulshan Devaiah | Cook Along with Chef Sanjyot Keer EP 11
description:
A never tried before Mutton recipe, cooked for the first time on channel with the amazing Gulshan Devaiah 🍴✨
This week on Your Food Lab, I had the absolute joy of spending the day in the kitchen with this brilliant and charming actor. I’ve always loved the characters he’s played, and cooking with him was such a delight!

We explored a mix of flavours, from the rich comfort of Bengali cuisine, to the fresh zest of Greek dishes, a touch of Coorg magic, and even Kantara inspiration 😍.
In this episode, we created a Mutton recipe inspired by three different Mutton recipes of mine, and I can’t wait to see if you can guess the name in the comments 👀😅. 

The whole experience was full of laughter, stories, and seriously delicious food. Plus, he shared some amazing food insights too ❤️

Watch the video till the end, tell me what you think, and don’t forget to like, share, and subscribe for more episodes where food, stories, and wonderful people come together at Your Food Lab 🥰

SK Style Mutton Curry
Prep time: 30 minutes
Cooking time: 2 hours
Serves: 4-5 people 

Ingredients:

Mutton Marination
MUTTON | मटन 1 KG
GINGER–GARLIC–GREEN CHILLI PASTE | अदरक–लहसुन–हरी मिर्च पेस्ट 2 TBSP
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी ½ TSP

For Vaatan (Paste)
ONION | प्याज़ 2 MEDIUM
DRY COCONUT | सूखा नारियल ¼ NO.
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 3 NOS.
GINGER | अदरक 2 INCH
GARLIC | लहसुन 1 FISTFUL (30 CLOVES)
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A LARGE HANDFUL

Final Cooking
GHEE | घी 2–3 TBSP
OIL | तेल 1 TBSP
BAY LEAF | तेज पत्ता 2 NOS.
CINNAMON | दालचीनी 1 INCH
JAVITRI | जावित्री 1 NO.
GREEN CARDAMOM | हरी इलायची 3 NOS.
BLACK CARDAMOM | बड़ी इलायची 1
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1 TSP
ONION | प्याज़ 300 GRAMS (CHOPPED)
AGRI MASALA | आगरी मसाला 1 TBSP
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी ½ TSP
CURD | दही 2–3 TBSP (WHISKED) 

Finishing
GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला A PINCH
ROASTED KASURI METHI POWDER | भुना कसूरी मेथी पाउडर
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)

For Serving:
Cooked Rice 
Rice Bhakri
Solkadhi 

Method:
In a large bowl, add the mutton along with ginger-garlic-green chilli paste and turmeric. Mix well so that every piece is evenly coated. If you want to add a smoky restaurant-style flavour, place a small piece of live charcoal in a steel bowl inside the mutton, pour a little ghee over it, cover the bowl immediately and let the smoke infuse for a few minutes. Remove the charcoal and keep the mutton aside.
To prepare the vaatan, place a papad jali directly on a high flame. Put the onions, dry coconut and green chillies on it and roast them until they are well charred. Be careful while roasting the dry coconut as it can catch fire quickly. Once nicely roasted, transfer everything onto a chopping board and let it cool slightly. Peel off and discard the burnt skin of the onions. Add the roasted onions, coconut and green chillies to a mixer jar along with ginger, garlic and a large handful of fresh coriander with stems. Add a little water and grind to a fine, smooth paste. This aromatic paste is the flavour base of the curry.
For the curry, heat ghee and oil together in a heavy-bottomed kadhai over medium-high flame. Add bay leaves, cinnamon, javitri, green cardamom, black cardamom and cumin seeds and let the spices crackle and release their aroma. 
Add the chopped onions and cook them, stirring regularly, until they turn deep golden brown.
Lower the flame and add Agri masala and turmeric powder along with a splash of hot water. 
Stir well and cook the spices until the rawness goes away and the oil starts to separate. Now add the prepared vaatan and the whisked curd. Stir continuously and cook this masala until it becomes thick, crumbly and glossy with oil separating from the sides.
Add the marinated mutton to the kadhai and mix well so the meat is coated with the masala. Cook on medium-high flame for 5–7 minutes, allowing the mutton to sear and start releasing its moisture. Cover and cook on low flame until the mutton releases more juices and begins to soften.
Add hot water as required to form the gravy, cover again and cook on low flame until the mutton becomes soft and tender. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking and allow the flavours to develop. 
Once the mutton is fully cooked, check the consistency of the gravy and adjust it if needed by adding a little hot water or reducing it further.
Finish the curry with garam masala, crushed kasuri methi, fresh coriander and a squeeze of lemon juice. Stir well and switch off the flame.
Serve this rich, smoky SK Style Mutton Curry hot with rice, rice bhakri and a chilled glass of solkadhi for a truly authentic experience.

title: Winter Spl Strawberry Yoghurt Recipe | Tasty Shrikhand | आसान स्ट्रॉबेरी श्रीखंड | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
This is an easy 3-ingredient Strawberry Shrikhand that turns out super delicious and beautifully creamy. 🍓✨
I’ve also shared my special way of plating it with biscuit crumble and chopped strawberries, perfect to make this strawberry season.
Do give it a try. Happy cooking! ❤️🍽️

Power meets precision in every blend!

Full written recipe - Strawberry Shrikhand

Prep time: 10 minutes (plus hanging time)
Resting time: 12–15 hours
Cooking time: 15 minutes
Serves: 4–5

Ingredients:
Hung Curd Base
DAHI | दही 1 KG

Strawberry Compote
STRAWBERRY | स्ट्रॉबेरी 250 GRAMS
SUGAR | शक्कर 7 TBSP


For Garnish
BISCUIT CRUMBLE | बिस्किट का चूरा 
CHOPPED STRAWBERRIES | कटी हुई स्ट्रॉबेरी 
MINT LEAVES | पुदीना 

Method:
Place a strainer over a bowl and line it with a double-layered muslin cloth. Add the dahi to the cloth, gather the edges together, twist gently, and lightly squeeze to remove as much free whey as possible.
Place the tied curd back into the strainer, cover with a plate, and place a weight on top. Transfer this setup to the refrigerator and allow it to hang for 12–15 hours or overnight.
After resting, remove the weight and plate. You will notice the curd has reduced significantly and thickened to a paneer-like consistency, with whey collected below.
Transfer the hung curd to a fine sieve. Using a spatula, press it through to remove any remaining graininess.
Once sieved, whisk the curd vigorously for 3–4 minutes until it becomes completely smooth, light, glossy, and airy. Scrape down the whisk to ensure even texture. The shrikhand base is now ready.
Clean and wash the strawberries. Trim the tops and transfer them to a mixer jar. Add sugar and blend into a smooth purée.
Transfer the purée to a saucepan and place it on medium flame. Bring it to a boil, then continue cooking while stirring continuously until it thickens to a jam-like consistency that coats the back of a spoon. This will take about 10–12 minutes.
Remove from heat and allow the compote to cool completely. It will thicken further as it cools. Do not mix while warm.
Once the compote is fully cooled, add it to the shrikhand base in small batches, mixing gently after each addition.
For a uniform pink shrikhand, mix fully until evenly coloured. For a marbled or streaked effect, fold lightly and stop early.
Your Strawberry Shrikhand is ready smooth, light, balanced, and naturally flavoured. Chill before serving for best texture and flavour.
Garnish with biscuit crumble, chopped strawberries, and mint leaves before serving.

title: Tomato Saar Recipe | Maharashtrian Style Tasty Veg Soup | महाराष्ट्र का टोमेटो सूप | Sanjyot Keer
description:
This Tomato Saar from Maharashtra is a regional soup that, for me, has so much more depth and comfort than regular tomato soups. 🍅✨
I learnt this recipe from our cinematographer Manas’s parents, Praful Kaka and Priyanka Kaku and like every Maharashtrian home, this one has its own beautiful character.
Do give it a try, it’s incredibly delicious. Happy cooking! ❤️

Written recipe - Tomato Saar
Prep time: 10-15 minutes
Cooking time: 15-20 minutes
Serves: 4-5 people

Ingredients:
TOMATO | टमाटर 7-8 NOS.
BEETROOT | बीटरूट 1/4 NO.
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED
FRESH COCONUT | नारियल 1/2 NO. (GRATED)
GINGER | अदरक 1 INCH
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS. 
WATER | पानी 5 CUPS (ADDED IN 2 BATCHES)
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
SUGAR | शक्कर 2-3 TBSP
RICE FLOUR | चावल का आटा 1 TBSP
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED (for slurry)
GHEE | घी 1 TBSP
URAD DAL | उड़द दाल 1/4 TSP
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1/4 TSP
CURRY LEAVES | कड़ी पत्ता 5-6 NOS.
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A SMALL HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
Method:
Make crosscuts on the tomatoes & boil them for 5-6 minutes or until soft.
Dip them into ice cold water for 1-2 minutes once boiled & then peel their skins off.
Add the boiled tomatoes into a mixer grinder jar along with beetroot & water, grind it into a fine puree.
Further pass the puree through a sieve & directly add it into a stock pot.
To prepare the flavored coconut milk, add the grated coconut into a mixer grinder jar along with ginger, green chilli & 2.5 cups of water & grind it.
Set a sieve over bowl & place a muslin cloth on the sieve, pour the coconut milk into the muslin cloth & squeeze out all the coconut milk into the bowl.
Then add the coconut leftovers from the muslin cloth back into the mixer grinder jar & add the remaining 2.5 cups of water & repeat the process of grinding & squeezing out the coconut milk using the muslin cloth, flavoured coconut milk is ready.
Add the coconut milk into the same stock pot as the tomato puree, switch on the flame & stir well.
Further add salt & sugar, then mix the rice flour & some water in a separate bowl & add that slurry into the stock pot, stir well & bring the saar to a gentle simmer.
Once the saar comes to a simmer, cook it for 5 minutes.
To make the tempering, set a small pan over high flame, once it gets hot add the ghee & let it heat up.
Add the urad dal & cook it until it turns light golden brown, then add cumin seeds & curry leaves & add the tadka to the saar & stir well.
Lastly add fresh coriander, the tomato saar is ready.

title: Easy & Healthy Upma with Millet Recipe | अब बनाईए स्वादिष्ट उपमा बिना सुजी के | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
Smart. Compact. Efficient.
Meet the kitchen essential designed to keep up with your everyday cooking.

Small enough to fit any kitchen, powerful enough to handle your daily prep ❤️

Full Written Recipe - Millet Upma

Prep time: 5-10 minutes
Cooking time 35-40 minutes
Serves: 5-6 people

Ingredients:

WASHING & SOAKING:
FOXTAIL MILLET | कांगनी 1 CUP
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED

PREP:
ONION | प्याज़ 1 MEDIUM SIZED
GINGER | अदरक 1 INCH
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS.
CARROT | गाजर 1 SMALL SIZED
CAPSICUM | शिमला मिर्च 1 SMALL SIZED
FRENCH BEANS | फ्रेंच बीन्स 5–6 NOS.
FRESH GREEN PEAS | हरे मटर 1/3 CUP

COOKING:
GHEE | घी 1 TBSP
CASHEW | काजू 6–7 NOS.
PEANUTS | मूंगफली 1 TBSP
CHANA DAL | चना दाल 1 TSP
URAD DAL | उड़द दाल 1 TSP
MUSTARD SEEDS | राई 1 TSP
HING | हींग 1/4 TSP
CURRY LEAVES | कड़ी पत्ता 8–10 NOS.
HOT WATER | गरम पानी 2 CUPS + 2 TBSP
SALT | नमक TO TASTE

FINAL INGREDIENTS:
LEMON JUICE
FRESH CORIANDER
GRATED FRESH COCONUT
FRIED NUTS

Method:
Add the foxtail millet to a large sieve and place it over a bowl. Wash it thoroughly under running water, gently rubbing the grains to remove any dust or bitterness. Discard the water, add fresh water, and let the millet soak for 15–20 minutes. This step helps the millet cook evenly and improves its texture.
Meanwhile, prepare the vegetables. Using a string chopper, finely chop the onion, ginger, and green chilli together. Transfer this mixture to a bowl and keep it aside. Using the same chopper, coarsely chop the carrot, capsicum, and french beans and keep them ready.
Set a kadhai over medium flame and add ghee. Once the ghee is hot, add the halved cashews and peanuts and fry them until lightly golden and crisp. Remove them onto a bowl and keep aside to be added later.
In the same kadhai, add chana dal and urad dal and sauté until they turn light golden. Add mustard seeds and let them crackle, then add hing and curry leaves and stir briefly until aromatic. Add the chopped onion, ginger, and green chilli mixture and sauté until the onions turn translucent and lose their raw smell.
Next, add the chopped vegetables and sauté for a minute or two, just until they are lightly cooked but still retain a bite. Pour in the measured water, stir well, and bring it to a simmer. Taste the water at this stage and adjust the salt, this is important, as the seasoning should be perfect before adding the millet.
Once the water starts simmering, strain the soaked foxtail millet and add it to the kadhai. Stir gently to distribute the millet evenly. Tie the lid with a clean kitchen cloth, cover the kadhai, and cook the upma on the lowest flame for about 15 minutes, allowing the millet to absorb all the water and cook through slowly.
After 15 minutes, remove the lid and check by inserting the back of a spoon, there should be no water left at the bottom. Cover the upma again, switch off the flame, and let it rest for 5–7 minutes so the steam settles.
Once rested, add lemon juice, freshly grated coconut, chopped fresh coriander, and the fried cashews and peanuts. Gently fluff the upma using a spatula, lifting it from the bottom to keep the grains separate.
Serve the hot, fluffy Foxtail Millet Upma with coconut chutney for a wholesome and comforting meal.

Coconut Chutney
Ingredients:
COCONUT | नारियल 1/2 CUP (PACKED)
ROASTED CHANA DAL | भुनी हुई चना दाल 1/2 CUP 
TAMARIND | इमली A MARBLE SIZED BALL
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2  NOS. 
BLACK PEPPER POWDER | काली मिर्च का पाउडर A PINCH
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1/4 TSP
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED

Method:
For coconut chutney add coconut, roasted chana dal & all the ingredients into a mixer grinder jar, grind it into a fine chutney & transfer it into a bowl. 
To make the tempering set a pan over high heat, add oil once the pan gets hot & let the oil heat as well. 
Further add chana dal & urad dal, stir well & cook them briefly then add mustard seeds, curry leaves & red chillies, stir once & immediately pour the tadka over the chutney & mix well. 
Your coconut chutney is ready.

title: Gajar Ka Halwa Recipe IN 30-MINUTES NO GRATING | आधे घंटे में बनाइये गाजर का हलवा | Sanjyot Keer
description:
Starting the year with a comforting classic, Gajar Ka Halwa made in just 30 minutes, without any grating and cooked easily in a pressure cooker. 🥕✨
This is the quickest way to enjoy halwa this season, and you have to try it paired with vanilla ice cream, just like I do!
Wishing you all a very Happy New Year ❤️✨ 

The full written recipe is shared below. Happy cooking! ❤️🍨

Our ChopLab Pro is now available at the best price 😍

✨ Upgrade your kitchen today. Shop now ✨ 
Amazon - https://amzn.in/d/ctEpGtr
Website - https://curaahome.com/products/curaa-choplab-pro

Full written recipe - Gajar Ka Halwa (Pressure Cooker without grating)
Prep time: 10 minutes
Cooking time: 35-40 minutes
Serves: 5-6 people

Ingredients:
RED CARROT | लाल गाजर 1/2 KG
GHEE | घी 1 TBSP
MILK | दूध 500 ML (FULL FAT)
SUGAR | शक्कर 1/2 CUP / 100 GRAMS
GREEN CARDAMOM POWDER | इलायची पाउडर A PINCH 
GHEE | घी 1 TBSP (for frying nuts)
CASHEW | काजू 2 TBSP (ROUGHLY CHOPPED)
ALMOND | बादाम 2 TBSP (ROUGHLY CHOPPED)
RAISINS | किशमिश 1 TBSP
Method:
Wash the carrots & then trim their tops & bottoms, further peel the carrots & cut them roughly, then using an electric chopper chop them up coarsely. This step will save a lot of time. 
Set a pressure cooker over high heat & add the ghee, let it get slightly hot & then add all the chopped carrot, stir well & cook for 3-4 minutes until the moisture from the carrot gets evaporated.
Further add the milk, stir once then pressure cook over medium flame only for 1 whistle then switch off the flame & let the cooker depressurize naturally.
Next, check the whistle & open the lid, stir well & then switch on the flame to medium high & cook until the milk reduces, this process will take 10-12 minutes, make sure you keep stirring the halwa.
Once almost all the milk has reduced, add the sugar & cardamom powder, stir well & cook it again for 4-5 minutes, the sugar will release its moisture & the halwa will get slightly runny again so cook until it thickens up again.
Do not overcook the halwa or it will become very tight, almost into barfi texture, only until the water from sugar evaporates & the halwa looks a bit shiny then switch off the flame.
Heat a pan, add the ghee into it & let it get slightly hot, then add the almonds & cashews & fry them until they turn light golden brown, keep stirring them.
Once the nuts are almost about to turn light golden brown add the raisins & stir briefly.
Pour the ghee & nuts mixture over the halwa & stir well.
Your gajar ka halwa is ready, let it rest for a couple of minutes, it will soak up all the ghee, heat it up before serving or have as it is, I personally like to have hot halwa with a scoop of vanilla ice cream.

title: Mushroom Do Pyaza Dhaba Style Recipe | ढाबे जैसा स्वादिष्ट मशरुम दो प्याज़ा | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
This Mushroom Do Pyaza is a true dhaba-style favourite super delicious, flavourful, and surprisingly easy to make at home. ✨
It tastes absolutely delicious with rumali roti, and I’ve shared all the tips to get the flavours just right.
The full written recipe is mentioned below. Happy cooking! ❤️🍽️

Check out our Retro Electric Kettle here 👉 https://amzn.in/d/dp8WzaL

Full written recipe - Mushroom Do Pyaza
Prep time: 20-25 minutes
Cooking time: 45 minutes
Serves: 4-5 people

Ingredients:
Vegetable Prep
ONION | प्याज़ 3 MEDIUM SIZED (FOR PETALS)
ONION | प्याज़ 4 MEDIUM SIZED (FOR GRAVY)
TOMATO | टमाटर 2 MEDIUM SIZED
FRESH CORIANDER STEMS | हरा धनिया डंठल A HANDFUL
MUSHROOM | मशरूम 400 GRAMS
Gravy
MUSTARD OIL | सरसों का तेल 3–4 TBSP
BAY LEAF | तेज पत्ता 3 NOS.
CINNAMON | दालचीनी 1 NO.
GREEN CARDAMOM | हरी इलायची 3 NOS.
BLACK CARDAMOM | काली इलायची 1 NO.
JAVITRI | जावित्री 1 SMALL PIECE
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1 TSP
HOT WATER | गरम पानी A SPLASH
HOT WATER | गरम पानी A SPLASH
GINGER GARLIC GREEN CHILLI PASTE | अदरक लहसुन हरी मिर्च पेस्ट 2 TBSP
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/4 TSP
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 2 TSP
CORIANDER POWDER | धनिया पाउडर 1 TBSP
SPICY RED CHILLI POWDER | तीखी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1 TSP
HOT WATER | गरम पानी A SPLASH
CURD | दही 1/2 CUP
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED
For Tossing
OIL | तेल 1 TSP
CORIANDER SEEDS | धनिया बीज 1 TBSP (CORASELY CRUSHED)
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 3 NOS. (SLIT)
RED CHILLI | सूखी लाल मिर्च 2 NOS.
Final Ingredients:
GINGER JULIENNE | अदरक के लच्छे A HANDFUL
GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला A PINCH
ROASTED KASURI METHI POWDER | भुना कसूरी मेथी पाउडर A PINCH
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL
Method:
Begin with the vegetable preparation. Take three onions and cut them into quarters, then separate the layers to form onion petals and keep them aside. Reserve the centre portion of these onions. 
Thinly slice the remaining four onions along with the trimmings from the onion petals and set aside for the gravy. 
Slice the tomatoes into thick slices and finely chop the fresh coriander stems. Clean the mushrooms gently, pat them completely dry, and keep them aside to cut later.
To prepare the gravy, set a kadhai over high flame and add mustard oil. Let the oil heat until it smokes lightly, then add the whole spices and allow them to crackle. Add the sliced onions and cook over high flame, stirring continuously, until they turn golden brown. As the onions begin to colour, add small splashes of hot water while cooking, this helps them brown evenly and faster without burning. Once the onions are well browned, add the ginger, garlic, and green chilli paste, stir well, and cook until the raw aroma disappears.
Lower the flame and add all the powdered spices along with a splash of hot water. Mix well and cook the spices until the oil begins to separate. Add the sliced tomatoes, whisked curd, and salt, stir well, and cook over high flame for a couple of minutes. Cover the kadhai and cook over medium-low flame until the tomatoes turn completely mushy and the oil separates from the masala.
While the gravy cooks, prepare the mushrooms. If the mushrooms are small, lightly trim the stem and keep them whole. If they are large, trim the stems and cut them into halves. Transfer them to a bowl. Slit a few green chillies and coarsely crush the coriander seeds using a mortar and pestle.
Once the gravy is ready, add the mushrooms, mix well, and cook over high flame for 2 minutes. Cover and cook on low flame until the mushrooms are cooked through and release their moisture. At this stage, check the consistency of the gravy. It should be semi-thick, adjust by adding hot water if needed or cooking longer to thicken.
In a separate pan set over high flame, add a little oil and let it heat well. Add the onion petals, slit green chillies, crushed coriander seeds, fresh coriander stems, and dry red chilli. Toss everything over high flame for 2 minutes until the onion petals are lightly charred yet crunchy. Add this tempering to the mushroom gravy along with ginger julienne, garam masala, roasted kasuri methi powder, and fresh coriander. Mix well, taste, and adjust salt if required. Your delicious, dhaba style Mushroom Do Pyaza is ready.
Serve the Mushroom Do Pyaza hot with rumali roti or tandoori roti.

Roasted Tomato Soup (NO OVEN)

Prep time: 5 mins
Cooking time: 15-20 mins
Serves: 3-4 people 

Ingredients:
TOMATO | टमाटर 5 MEDIUM SIZED
ONION | प्याज़ 2 SMALL SIZED
GINGER | अदरक 2 INCH
GARLIC | लहसुन 10–12 CLOVES
CORIANDER STEMS | धनिये की डंठल A HANDFUL
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च 3 NOS.
OIL | तेल 1-2 TSP
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
BLACK PEPPER | काली मिर्च 1/2 TSP (FRESHLY CRUSHED)
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED (for blending)
HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED (for adjusting consistency)
SUGAR | शक्कर A PINCH
BUTTER | मक्खन 2 TBSP (OPTIONAL)
For garnish 
FRESH CREAM | फ्रेश क्रीम
BASIL | बेसिल 

Method:
Cut the tomatoes into halves, onions into quarters, and roughly slice the ginger.
Set a pan over high flame and let it get hot. Add a little oil.
Place tomatoes, onions, ginger, garlic, fresh coriander stems, and Kashmiri red chillies into the pan.
Add a pinch of salt and black pepper. Roast on high flame without stirring for 2–3 minutes until nicely charred on one side.
Flip everything, add a splash of hot water, cover, and cook until the tomatoes turn soft.
Switch off the flame and let the mixture cool slightly.
Remove the stalks from the chillies and transfer everything to a mixer jar. Add water and blend until smooth.
Pour the soup into a saucepan, adjust consistency, and bring it to a gentle simmer.
Taste and adjust seasoning, add a pinch of sugar to balance the flavours. Add butter if using.
Simmer briefly, switch off the flame.

Your quick, comforting, and delicious Tomato Soup is ready.

title: Methi Na Gota Recipe | Gujarat's Famous Methi Pakode with Tips | मेथी के पकोड़े | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
This Methi Na Gota is a classic Gujarati favourite, loved at highway dhabas and street stalls alike and the perfect thing to make while methi is in season. 🌿✨
With all the tips to get them crispy on the outside and fluffy on the inside, this one’s truly special.
I’ve also shared the traditional besan kadhi that’s served alongside it. Happy cooking! ❤️

Written Recipe - Methi na Gota 

Prep time: 15-20 minutes 
Cooking time: 10-15 minutes
Serves: 5-6 people

Ingredients:

BATTER:
BESAN | बेसन 2 CUPS
BAKING SODA | बेकिंग सोडा 1/4 TSP 
FINE RAVA | बारीक रवा 2 TBSP 
WATER | पानी 3/4 CUP – 1 CUP (ADDED GRADUALLY) 
FENUGREEK | मेथी 1 CUP
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया 1/2 CUP (ROUGHLY CHOPPED) 
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 3–4 NOS. (FINELY CHOPPED) 
GINGER | अदरक 2 INCH (FINELY CHOPPED) CORIANDER SEEDS | धनिया के बीज 1 TBSP (ROUGHLY CRUSHED) 
AJWAIN | अजवाइन 1/2 TSP (CRUSHED) 
HING | हींग 1/4 TSP 
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/4 TSP 
BLACK PEPPER | काली मिर्च 1/4 TSP (COARSELY CRUSHED) 
SALT | नमक TO TASTE 
SUGAR | शक्कर 1 TBSP 
LEMON JUICE | नींबू का रस OF 1/2 LEMON 
HOT 
OIL | गरम तेल 2–3 TBSP

SALTED CHILLI:
PAKODA MIRCHI | पकोड़ा मिर्च AS REQUIRED SALT | नमक A PINCH

Method:
In a large bowl, sieve the besan along with baking soda. Add the fine rava and gradually add water while whisking continuously to form a thick, smooth besan batter. Whisk well to incorporate air, this helps make the pakode light. Cover the batter and let it rest for 15–20 minutes.
While the batter rests, finely chop the methi leaves and prepare the besan kadhi.
Once the methi is ready and the kadhi is done, set oil in a deep kadhai to heat for frying.
After resting, add the chopped methi to the batter along with the remaining ingredients. Mix gently using a spatula. Add a few tablespoons of hot oil from the frying kadhai into the batter and mix well, this step is important as it helps make the gota crisp on the outside while staying moist inside.
The final batter should be semi-thick, not runny. You should be able to scoop it easily with your fingers.
When the oil is hot, scoop small portions of the batter using your fingers and gently push them into the oil using your thumb. Fry the pakode on medium flame, turning occasionally, until they are evenly golden, crisp, and cooked through.
Remove the fried pakode using a spider and transfer them to a sieve so excess oil drains off. Fry the remaining batch in the same manner.
In the same hot oil, place a spider and add slit pakoda chillies. Fry them briefly, slitting the chillies is important to prevent spluttering. Once fried, transfer them to a bowl and sprinkle with salt.
Your hot, crispy Methi na Gota are ready.
Serve immediately with salted fried chillies, besan kadhi, and hot chai.

Besan Kadhi
Ingredients:
BESAN | बेसन 1/4 CUP 
WATER | पानी 2 CUPS (ADDED GRADUALLY) TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/2 TSP 
SALT | नमक TO TASTE 
SUGAR | शक्कर 1–2 TBSP 
GROUNDNUT OIL | मूंगफली का तेल 2 TBSP MUSTARD SEEDS | राई 1/2 TSP 
HING | हींग 1/4 TSP 
CURRY LEAVES | कड़ी पत्ता 8–10 NOS. 
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS. (ROUGHLY CUT) SALT | नमक IF REQUIRED 
LEMON JUICE | नींबू का रस 1/2 SMALL LEMON
Method:
In a bowl, add the besan and gradually whisk in water to form a smooth, lump-free mixture. Add turmeric powder, salt and sugar, and whisk again until everything is well combined.
Set a saucepan over high flame and add groundnut oil. Once the oil is hot, add mustard seeds and allow them to crackle. Add hing, curry leaves and green chillies, stir briefly, then immediately pour in the prepared besan & water mixture.
Stir continuously and bring the kadhi to a gentle simmer. Cook over medium flame for 3–5 minutes, stirring often. The kadhi will start to thicken, keep in mind that it thickens further as it cools, so avoid overcooking.
Taste and adjust seasoning if required. Switch off the flame, allow it to cool slightly, then add lemon juice and mix well.
Let the kadhi cool down completely.
Your cool, lightly tangy Besan Kadhi is ready to be served with Methi na Gota.

title: SUPER SOFT RAGI IDLI | Healthy Millet Recipe with Tips | रागी से बनाईए स्वादिष्ट इडली | Sanjyot Keer
description:
This Ragi Idli is made from scratch using a properly fermented batter, with all the tips and measurements to help you achieve the softest idlis every time. ✨
It’s a wholesome and delicious way to incorporate millets into your diet.
I’ve shared the full written recipe below, along with the link to my perfect hotel-style sambar. Happy cooking! ❤️🍽️

Hotel Style Sambar: https://youtu.be/peF9ha21rP4?si=cGS1fzwK_6RfnMCU

Power meets precision in every blend! ✨
Say hello to the BLP 2.0, now available on Amazon, your new kitchen superstar that makes cooking easier, faster, and way more fun. With Christmas around the corner, it’s the perfect time to treat yourself or surprise someone special with the ultimate kitchen companion. 🎄

Happy holidays & happy shopping! 🛍️ 
Link - https://amzn.in/d/0nhLQIe

Written Recipe - Ragi Idli

Soaking Time: 6-8 hours
Prep time: 10-15 minutes 
Fermentation time: 10-12 hours
Cooking time: 15 minutes

Ingredients:
RAGI | रागी 1 CUP 
IDLI RICE | इडली चावल 1 CUP 
URAD GOTA | उड़द गोटा 1/2 CUP 
FENUGREEK SEEDS | मेथी दाना 1/2 TSP 
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED
POHA | पोहा 1/4 CUP 
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED(for washing and soaking)
ICE COLD WATER | ठंडा पानी AS  REQUIRED (for blending)
SALT | नमक TO TASTE 
OIL | तेल AS REQUIRED (FOR GREASING)

Method:
For this recipe, the ratio used is 1 cup ragi : 1 cup idli rice : ½ cup urad gota.
This ratio gives soft, fluffy idlis with good structure. Using only ragi and urad will result in denser, slightly pasty idlis, while increasing ragi further from 1 cup in the ratio can make the texture softer but a bit mushy.
Add ragi and idli rice to a large bowl and wash thoroughly, changing the water until it turns clear.
In a separate bowl, wash the urad gota along with fenugreek seeds in the same way.
Cover both bowls with enough water and let them soak for 6–8 hours or overnight.
Once the ragi, rice & urad dal are soaked, add poha to a sieve, wash it under running water, drain well, and let it rest in the sieve for 10 minutes.
Discard the soaking water from the rice and ragi. Transfer them to a mixer jar, add ice-cold water just up to the level of the grains, and grind using pulse mode.
The batter should be fine but slightly coarse, when rubbed between your fingers, it should feel like fine rava, not completely smooth.
Transfer this batter to a large vessel.
Next, discard the soaking water from the urad dal. Add it to the mixer jar along with the soaked poha and less ice-cold water than used for rice. Grind until the batter is very fine, light, and fluffy.
Add the urad batter to the rice and ragi batter. Mix and whisk the batter vigorously for 2–3 minutes to incorporate air.
Clean the sides of the vessel, loosely cover with a lid, and allow the batter to ferment overnight or until it rises and becomes airy.
Fermentation time depends on temperature, 8–10 hours in summer and 12–15 hours in winter.
Once fermented, gently mix the batter using a spatula to release excess air. Add salt and mix lightly. The idli batter is now ready.
To steam the idlis, set a steamer on high heat with sufficient water, cover, and allow steam to build.
Grease the idli plates generously with oil.
Pour the batter into the moulds, filling them about ¾ full, as the idlis will rise while steaming.
Place the trays in the steamer, cover, and steam for 12–15 minutes.
To check doneness, insert a toothpick, it should come out clean.
Remove the trays carefully and allow them to cool slightly before unmoulding using the back of a spoon.
Your soft, fluffy Ragi Idlis are ready.
Serve hot with coconut chutney and sambar.

Coconut Chutney
Ingredients:
FRESH COCONUT | नारियल 1 CUP
ROASTED CHANA DAL | दालिया 1/3 CUP
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS. 
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
CUMIN POWDER | जीरा पाउडर A PINCH
BLACK PEPPER POWDER | काली मिर्च पाउडर A PINCH
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED
OIL | तेल 2 TBSP
MUSTARD SEEDS | राई 1/2 TSP
CHANA DAL | चना दाल 1/2 TSP
URAD DAL | उरद दाल 1/2 TSP
RED CHILLI | लाल मिर्च 2-3 NOS. 
CURRY LEAVES | कड़ी पत्ता 6-8 NOS.
Method:
Add the coconut, roasted chana dal, green chilli & the remaining ingredients into a mixer grinder jar & grind it into a fine paste.
Transfer the coconut chutney into a bowl & set a pan on high flame, add the oil, once the oil gets hot add in the mustar seeds, urad & chana dal, fry them in the oil for a few seconds & then add red chillies & curry leaves.
Further pour the tadka immediately over the coconut chutney & stir well.
Your coconut chutney is ready.

description:
This is a simple, homestyle Aloo Gobi made without onion, garlic or tomato, yet full of flavour.✨
With a few easy tips and very little prep, you’ll get the perfect texture and a dish that turns out truly incredible. 👌
I’ve mentioned the full written recipe below in the description. Happy cooking! ❤️🍽️

Written Recipe - Aloo Gobhi

Prep time: 10 minutes
Cooking time: 25 minutes
Serves: 3-4 people

Ingredients:
PREP:
CAULIFLOWER | फूलगोभी 1 MEDIUM SIZED. / 500 GRAMS
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED
SALT | नमक A LARGE PINCH
POTATO | आलू 3 MEDIUM SIZED.
GINGER | अदरक 3 INCH
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 3 NOS.
COOKING:
MUSTARD OIL | सरसों का तेल 2–3 TBSP
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1 TSP
AJWAIN | अजवाइन A PINCH
HING | हींग 1/4 TSP
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/4 TSP
CORIANDER POWDER | धनिया पाउडर 2 TSP
GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला A PINCH
CUMIN POWDER | जीरा पाउडर A PINCH
SALT | नमक IF REQUIRED
DRY MANGO POWDER | आमचूर पाउडर A PINCH
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
Method:
Remove the core of the cauliflower and cut it into medium, bite-sized florets. Do not discard the stem, peel off the hard outer layer and cut the tender inner part into pieces, it cooks beautifully and tastes similar to potato. You can also use some of the green leaves attached to the cauliflower.
Transfer the cauliflower florets into a bowl, add water and a large pinch of salt, wash thoroughly and keep it submerged while you prepare the remaining ingredients.
Peel the potatoes, cut them into similar-sized pieces, and keep them submerged in water to prevent oxidation.
Using a mortar and pestle, pound the ginger and green chillies into a coarse paste. Keep this aside.
Heat a kadhai over high flame and add mustard oil. Allow the oil to heat until it lightly smokes, then lower the flame. Add cumin seeds, ajwain and hing, letting them crackle.
Add the drained potato pieces and stir well and cook over medium-high flame for a minute, then cover and cook on the lowest flame for 3–4 minutes so the potatoes begin to soften.
Uncover the kadhai, stir the potatoes and add the prepared ginger–green chilli paste along with the drained cauliflower. Mix everything well, cover again and cook for 2–3 minutes to allow the vegetables to steam and release moisture.
Remove the lid and add salt along with the remaining powdered spices. Stir gently to coat the vegetables evenly. Cover once again and cook over low flame for 10–15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the potatoes and cauliflower are fully cooked and soft.
Once cooked, taste and adjust salt if required. Finish with a pinch of dry mango powder. Add fresh coriander and mix gently.
Your simple yet super delicious, dry-style Aloo Gobhi is ready. Serve hot with chapati, laccha paratha or puris.

title: Pahadi Chaunsa Dal | Uttarakhand Special Chainsoo Dal Recipe | उत्तराखंड की चौंसा दाल | Sanjyot Keer
description:
This Pahadi Chaunsa Dal is one of those dishes that feels like pure comfort in winter. Simple ingredients, deep flavour, and so soothing with hot rice. ✨
If you’re looking for a dal that’s different yet beautifully homely, I’d love for you to try this. And if you need the full written recipe, I’ve shared it down below.
Happy cooking! ❤️

From chutneys to spice mixes, BlendLab’s stainless steel jar handles it all with ease. 🙌

Use BLENDLAB200 on Amazon for a special discount for the first 100 people 😍

Shop now - https://amzn.in/d/cLkf0Rs

Written recipe - Chaunsa Dal
Prep time: 5-10 minutes
Cooking time: 1 hour
Serves: 4-5 people

Ingredients:
WHOLE URAD DAL | उड़द दाल 150 GRAMS / 3/4 CUP
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED (for washing)
GINGER | अदरक 2 INCH
GREEN GARLIC | हरा लहसुन 10–12 NOS.
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2–3
CORIANDER SEEDS | धनिया के बीज 2 TSP
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1 TSP
SPICY RED CHILLI | लाल मिर्च 2 NOS. 
SALT | नमक A PINCH
WILD MUSTARD SEEDS | जाखिया 1 TSP
HING | हींग 1/2 TSP
WHEAT FLOUR | गेहूं का आटा 1.5 TBSP WHOLE URAD DAL | उड़द दाल 150 GRAMS / 3/4 CUP
MUSTARD OIL | सरसों का तेल 3–4 TBSP
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/4 TSP
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1 TSP
CORIANDER POWDER | धनिया पाउडर 1–2 TSP
HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
Method:
Wash the whole urad dal thoroughly, then wipe it using a clean kitchen napkin to remove excess moisture.
Set a cast-iron tawa or fry pan on medium heat and dry roast the dal until it becomes fully dry, lightly toasted, and fragrant.
Transfer it to a bowl and allow it to cool completely. Once cooled, add the dal to a spice-grinding jar and grind it coarsely, do not make a fine powder. Your urad dal powder is ready.
In a mortar & pestle, add green garlic, ginger, green chillies, cumin seeds, coriander seeds, and spicy whole red chillies with some salt. Pound everything together into a coarse paste.
Set a cast-iron kadhai on high heat. Add mustard oil and let it heat until it just begins to smoke.
Add wild mustard and hing, then immediately add the prepared coarse paste. Stir and cook until the raw aroma goes away. You can use mustard seeds if wild mustard is not available.
Lower the flame and add wheat flour to the kadhai. Cook the flour on low heat, stirring continuously, until the raw smell completely disappears.
Add the prepared urad dal powder and mix well to coat it with the tempering. Cook it for a minute so the flavours absorb into the dal.
Thend add salt and powdered spices, stir well.
Now gradually add hot water while whisking continuously to avoid lumps. Once lump-free, add more hot water to bring the dal to a slightly thin consistency.
Bring the dal to a gentle simmer, cover, and cook on low flame for at least 45 minutes, stirring occasionally. Slow cooking helps the dal release its starches and develop its characteristic earthy flavour.
Once cooked, the dal will thicken beautifully. Adjust the consistency by adding hot water if needed.
Taste and adjust salt, then finish with chopped fresh coriander.
Your delicious, rustic and comforting Chaunsa Dal is ready. Serve hot with steamed rice or roti, this dal is also enjoyed thick like a sabzi with roti.

title: INSTANT Jowar Dosa Recipe | Easy Tips to make Crispy Dosa | आसान ज्वार का दोसा | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
Bringing you a quick, comforting Instant Jowar Dosa that’s crispy, tasty, needs no soaking, and is a wonderful way to add millet to your diet. ❤️✨
You’ll also find a flavourful green chutney recipe and all the tips and measurements for perfect, consistent results! 👌

Our BlendLab is a kitchen companion that makes everyday cooking easier, from chutneys and gravies to cold coffees and even spice mixes with its stainless steel grinding jar.

You can also check out the other Instant Dosa recipes I’ve shared on the channel before!
Instant Ragi Dosa: https://youtube.com/shorts/7B8zCaECYnc?si=vgk_3crwyxrqhA9O
Crispy Rava Dosa: https://youtu.be/HTSoJemOiX8?si=NNJByWL6PnYIPuS3
Instant Vrat Dosa: https://youtu.be/v9rYeV5XOmM?si=nholsK49fltoDONk
Instant Sponge Dosa: https://youtu.be/3DFWkNqSn1M?si=2r2zaztnUICtTG88

📌Written recipe - Instant Jowar Dosa

Prep time: 5-10 minutes
Cooking time: 10-15 minutes
Serves: 15 small sized dosa

Ingredients:
Batter
JOWAR FLOUR | ज्वार का आटा 1 CUP
UPMA RAWA | उपमा रवा 4 TBSP
RICE FLOUR | चावल का आटा 4 TBSP
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
BLACK PEPPER | काली मिर्च 1/2 TSP (COARSELY CRUSHED)
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS. (CHOPPED)
CURRY LEAVES | करी पत्ता A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1/2 TSP
WATER | पानी 3 CUPS
Chutney
FRESH COCONUT | ताज़ा नारियल 1 SMALL SIZED
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL
MINT | पुदीना A HANDFUL
GINGER | अदरक 1 INCH
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS.
SUGAR | शक्कर 1 TSP
BLACK PEPPER | काली मिर्च A PINCH (FRESHLY CRUSHED)
ROASTED PEANUTS | भुनी मूंगफली 1/2 CUP
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
LEMON JUICE | नींबू का रस OF HALF A LEMON
ICE CUBES | बर्फ 1-2 NOS.
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED
DOSA MAKING:
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
ONION | प्याज़ AS REQUIRED (CHOPPED)
GHEE | घी AS REQUIRED (FOR COOKING)
Method:
To make the batter, add jowar flour, rawa, rice flour, salt, black pepper, chopped green chillies, chopped curry leaves and cumin seeds into a large mixing bowl. Mix everything well, then add 2 cups of water and whisk continuously to form a lump-free batter. 
Add 1 more cup of water to make a thin, free-flowing batter. Cover and let the batter rest for 10 minutes while you prepare the chutney.
The rawa and rice flour provide structure and crispiness, since jowar has no gluten. You can make the dosa using only jowar flour, but ensure you’re using a good quality non-stick pan or a very well-seasoned dosa tawa, as pure jowar batter tends to stick. If available, add 1/2 cup urad dal powder (wash → dry roast → grind) or freshly soaked-and-ground urad dal paste to help with structure and crispiness. Avoid making very thin dosas with 100% jowar batter as they will stick, semi-thick dosas are easier to peel and cook better.
For the chutney, add all the chutney ingredients into a mixer jar and blend into a coarse, thick paste. The delicious green coconut chutney is ready.
To cook the dosa, heat a non-stick pan or a well-seasoned tawa over high flame until very hot. A fry pan works great as it naturally shapes the dosa into a neat circle. 
Once the pan is hot, sprinkle a handful of chopped onions on it. Pour the batter from some height in a circular motion starting from the edges, the batter will spread on its own and form a natural mesh-like pattern. Ensuring a very hot pan is key for this dosa.
Cook the dosa over high flame for 1–2 minutes, then reduce to the lowest flame. Add very little ghee around the edges and cook for 5–6 minutes or until the mesh holes and sides turn golden brown and crisp. 
Carefully release the dosa from the pan using a spatula, fold as preferred and serve hot with the green coconut chutney.
Your instant, super crispy Jowar Dosa is ready.

title: Aloo Chole Pulao Recipe | EASY Biryani Style Veg Pulao | बिरयानी जैसा आलू छोले पुलाओ | Sanjyot Keer
description:
Sharing a comforting biryani-style Aloo Chole Pulao that feels hearty and truly a meal in itself.
It’s super easy to make, and with my tips and measurements, you’ll get perfect results every time! ✨👌

You’ll find the link to my special biryani masala and the full written recipe right below, happy cooking! ❤️

Biryani Masala: https://youtu.be/HP2bVwNHJfM?t=98&si=cCO5cdNJRACoKfEr

Written recipe - Aloo Chole Pulao
Prep time: 10-15 minutes (excluding soaking time for Chickpeas)
Cooking time: 40-45 minutes
Serves: 4-5 people

Ingredients:
Soaking Rice
BASMATI RICE | बासमती चावल 1.5 CUPS
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED
Pressure Cooking Chickpeas
CHICKPEAS | काबुली चना 1/2 CUP
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED
BAKING SODA | बेकिंग सोडा A PINCH
BLACK CARDAMOM | बड़ी इलायची 1 NO.
BAY LEAF | तेज पत्ता 1 NO.
Pulao
GHEE | घी 1 TBSP
OIL | तेल 1 TBSP
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1 TSP
ONION | प्याज़ 3 MEDIUM SIZED (SLICED)
GINGER GARLIC PASTE | अदरक लहसुन पेस्ट 2 TBSP
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 3 NOS. (SLIT)
HOT WATER | गरम पानी A SPLASH
TOMATO | टमाटर 1 NO. (ROUGHLY SLICED)
CURD | दही 1/2 CUP
BIRYANI MASALA | बिरयानी मसाला 2 TSP
SPICY RED CHILLI POWDER | तीखा लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1/2 TSP
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
HOT WATER | गरम पानी A SPLASH
POTATO | आलू 2 MEDIUM SIZED (QUARTERS)
HOT WATER | गरम पानी 3 CUPS
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
MINT | पुदीना A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
Method:
Wash the basmati rice thoroughly until the water runs clear. Add fresh water and let it soak while you prepare everything else.
Wash ½ cup chickpeas and soak for 6–8 hours or overnight.
Once soaked, discard the water and transfer the chickpeas to a pressure cooker. Add fresh water, whole spices, a small pinch of baking soda, and pressure cook for 1 whistle on high flame + 2 whistles on medium-low flame.
In a wide, heavy-bottom vessel, heat ghee and oil over high flame.
Once hot, add cumin seeds followed by sliced onions. Sauté on medium-high flame until the onions turn golden brown.
Add ginger garlic paste and green chillies, cook briefly until the raw aroma goes away.
Add sliced tomatoes, whisked curd, salt, and the powdered spices. Mix well and cook until the oil begins to separate from the masala.
By this time, the chickpeas would have finished cooking. Switch off the cooker and allow it to depressurize naturally while you continue to cook the masala.
Once the masala has released oil, add the potato pieces and mix well.
Cover and cook for 4–5 minutes, or until the potatoes are almost cooked.
Meanwhile, open the pressure cooker and discard the whole spices. Strain and keep the chickpeas aside. They should be fully cooked but still holding shape.
Once the potatoes are almost cooked, add the boiled chickpeas and mix gently.
Pour in measured hot water (as per rice ratio), taste the liquid, and adjust salt, the seasoned water should taste accurate in saltiness.
Drain the soaked rice completely and add it along with chopped coriander and mint.
Stir gently and bring the mixture to a simmer on medium flame.
Cover the lid with a clean kitchen towel, place it on the vessel, and cook the pulao on the lowest flame for 12–15 minutes.
To check doneness, lift the lid and create a small opening using the back of a spoon, if water remains at the bottom, cook for another 2–3 minutes.
Once cooked, switch off the flame and allow the pulao to rest for 15–20 minutes without touching it. Resting helps settle the steam and prevents the rice from breaking.
After resting, gently fluff the pulao using a wide spatula, lifting the rice upwards without mashing it.
Your wholesome and flavourful Aloo Chole Pulao is ready. Serve hot with raita of your choice.

title: OIL-FREE Pumpkin Soup in 10-MINUTES | Healthy & Easy One-Pot Recipe | पम्पकिन सूप | Sanjyot Keer
description:
Get the BlendLab on Amazon: https://amzn.in/d/89fvYGe

Get the BlendLab on our website: https://curaahome.com/products/curaa-blendlab

You can order the 1000ml Jar here: https://curaahome.com/products/blendlab-spare-parts

Written recipe - Oil-Free Pumpkin Soup
Prep time: 5-10 mins
Cooking time: 15-20 mins
Serves: 4-5 people

Ingredients:
Soup Base
PUMPKIN | कद्दू 1 SMALL / 500 GRAMS
RED CARROT | लाल गाजर 2 SMALL SIZED.
ONION | प्याज़ 1 MEDIUM SIZED.
GINGER | अदरक 1 INCH
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS.
GARLIC | लहसुन 7–8 CLOVES
FRESH CORIANDER STEMS | हरे धनिये के डंठल A HANDFUL 
CELERY | सेलेरी 1 STALK (OPTIONAL)
CASHEWS | काजू 8–10 NOS
CINNAMON | दालचीनी 2 INCH
BAY LEAF | तेज पत्ता 2 NOS.
CLOVES | लौंग 2 NOS.
SALT | नमक A LARGE PINCH
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED
Final Cooking
HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED
SALT | नमक IF REQUIRED
BLACK PEPPER | काली मिर्च TO TASTE
LEMON JUICE | नींबू का रस OF 1/2 LEMON
Method:
Cut a small pumpkin into quarters and scoop out all the seeds. 
Peel the pumpkin using whichever method you prefer: either slice the quarters into large pieces and remove the skin with a knife, peel the quarters like a watermelon slice, or simply use a good-quality peeler. Once peeled, cut the pumpkin into rough dices.
Peel the red carrots and roughly chop them as well. Roughly cut the onion. Add the chopped pumpkin, carrots and onion to a pressure cooker along with roughly sliced ginger, garlic, green chilli, fresh coriander stems, celery stalk, cashews, whole spices, salt and enough water.
Pressure-cook for 3 whistles. Allow the cooker to depressurize naturally, then open the lid. Remove and discard the whole spices. Let the mixture cool down completely.
Once cooled, transfer everything into a mixer jar and blend into a smooth, fine purée. 
Transfer the purée to the cooker or a pot and bring it to a gentle simmer. Add hot water to adjust the consistency as needed. Season with additional salt and pepper. Finish with a squeeze of lemon juice. If the pumpkin isn’t naturally sweet, you can balance the flavour by adding a little honey or sugar.
