title: Lauki Kofta Curry Recipe | Tips for Soft Kofte | स्वादिष्ट लौकी कोफ्ते की करी | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
Full written recipe - Lauki Kofta Curry

Prep time: 20-25 minutes
Cooking time: 1 hour
Serves: 5-6 people

Ingredients:
Kofte 
BOTTLE GOURD | लौकी 500 GRAMS
SALT | नमक A LARGE PINCH 
GINGER GARLIC GREEN CHILLI PASTE | अदरक लहसुन हरी मिर्च की पेस्ट 1 TBSP
CORIANDER POWDER | धनिया पाउडर 2 TSP
DRY MANGO POWDER | आमचूर पाउडर 1 TSP
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/2 TSP
RED CHILLI POWDER | लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1 TSP
GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला A PINCH
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा A PINCH
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
BLACK PEPPER | काली मिर्च 1/2 TSP (FRESHLY CRUSHED)
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
MINT | पुदीना A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
GRAM FLOUR | बेसन 2 TBSP
BAKING SODA | बेकिंग सोडा A PINCH 
Curry
TOMATO | टमाटर 3 MEDIUM SIZED (ROUGHLY CHOPPED)
CASHEW | काजू 10 NOS. (BOILED)
GHEE | घी 2 TBSP
OIL | तेल 1 TSP
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1/2 TSP
CINNAMON | दालचीनी 1 INCH
BAY LEAF | तेज पत्ता 1 NO.
GREEN CARDAMOM | हरी इलायची 3 PODS
ASAFOETIDA | हींग 1/2 TSP
ONION | प्याज़ 2 MEDIUM SIZED. (CHOPPED)
GINGER GARLIC PASTE | अदरक लहसुन की पेस्ट 2 TBSP
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS. (CHOPPED)
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/2 TSP
KASHMIRI RED CHILL POWDED | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1 TBSP
SPICY RED CHILL POWDER | तीखी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1 TSP
CORIANDER POWDER | धनिया पाउडर 1 TBSP
DRY MANGO POWDER | आमचूर पाउडर 1 TSP
HOT WATER | गरम मसाला A SPLASH
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED 
SUGAR | शक्कर 1 TSP
ROASTED KASURI METHI POWDER | कसूरी मेथी पाउडर A LARGE PINCH 
GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला A PINCH 
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
Method:
Wash & peel the bottle gourd, then divide it into smaller pieces & grate it using a large hole grater. Try getting a smaller sized bottle gourd, you don't want the seeds to be very large inside otherwise you will need to cut the core out.
Add the grated lauki onto a muslin cloth, add a pinch of salt, mix & let it rest for a few minutes.
Next, squeeze out the excess moisture from the lauki, make sure you don't make it completely dry, it needs a very little amount of moisture.
Before making the kofta mixture, set a kadhai with oil over medium flame, so by the time you shape the kofta the oil will get hot.
Add the lauki into a bowl & add all the ingredients of the kofte, mix well & gently mash using your hands, the mixture should be very slightly loose but should still bind into balls.
If the mixture is very moist then you can a more besan, and if it becomes to dry then you can add splashes of water.
Once the mixture is ready, shape it into medium sized koftas.
Once the oil is moderately hot, 150°C, carefully drop the shaped koftas into the oil, let them fry for 2-3 minutes before stirring. Once they hold their structure, stir & fry them slowly over medium low flame until golden brown. If you fry them over high flame the koftas will remain raw from the inside. 
Once fried, transfer them into a sieve & set them aside until you make the gravy, fry them in batches.
Add the tomatoes & cashews into a mixer jar & blend into a fine puree. 
Set a heavy bottomes vessel over high flame, add ghee & oil, let it get hot. Then add all the whole spices & cook them for a few seconds.
Add onions, stir well & cook over medium high flame until they turn light golden brown.
Next, add the ginger garlic paste along with green chillies, stir & continue to cook further until the onions turn completely golden brown.
Then lower the flame & add all the powdered spices along with a splash of hot water, stir & cook over high flame for 2-3 minutes.
Once the oil separates, add the prepared tomato & cashew puree along with salt, stir well, cover & cook the gravy over low flame for 12-15 minutes. Open the lid & stirr the gravy at regular intervals, add hot water & continue to cook further if the gravy gets too dry.
Once the gravy is cooked, add hot water to adjust the consistency of the gravy, keep it slightly thin.
Taste & adjust salt if required & add sugar, kasuri methi powder & garam masala.
Add the prepared kofte along with some coriander & cook the koftas in the gravy briefly.
Our delicious, lauki ke kofte as ready, serve them with tandoori roti, chapati or any bread of your choice.

title: Quick & Tasty Prawn Curry | Mangalorean Style Recipe | मैंगलोरियन प्रॉन करी | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
Full written recipe - Mangalorean Prawn Curry
Prep time: 10-15 minutes
Cooking time: 20-25 minutes
Serves: 5-6 people

Ingredients:
Fresh curry paste
COCONUT OIL | नारियल का तेल 1 TSP
BYADGI RED CHILLI | ब्यादगी लाल मिर्च 10 NOS.
CORIANDER SEEDS | साबुत धनिया 2 TSP
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1 TSP
BLACK PEPPERCORNS | साबुत काली मिर्च 1/2 TSP
CAROM SEEDS | अजवाइन A PINCH (OPTIONAL)
FENUGREEK SEEDS | मेथी के बीज A PINCH
GARLIC | लहसुन 8-10 CLOVES
GINGER | अदरक 1 INCH
ONION | प्याज़ 1 NO. 
TAMARIND | इमली 2 TBSP (LEMON SIZED BALL)
FRESH COCONUT | ताज़ा नारियल 1 CUP (SCRAPED)
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/2 TSP
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED 
Tempering & final cooking
COCONUT OIL | नारियल तेल 2 TBSP
MUSTARD SEEDS | राई 1/2 TSP
CURRY LEAVES | कड़ी पत्ता 10-12 NOS. 
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 1 NO. (SLIT)
ONION | प्याज़ 1 NO. (CHOPPED)
HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED 
PRAWNS | झींगे 300 GRAMS (CLEANED & DEVIENED)
SALT | नमक A PINCH
LEMON JUICE | नींबू का रस 2 TSP
COCONUT MILK | नारियल का दूध 2-3 TBSP
Method:
Set a pan over high heat & let it get hot, then lower the flame & add the coconut oil, add the red chillies & roast the chillies over low flame while stirring continuously until they get hot & puff up slightly. Transfer the chillies into a bowl & set them aside.
In the same pan add all the whole spices of the paste, & roast them over medium flame until they turn fragrant & golden brown in colour. Transfer the spices into the same bowl as the chillies & let all the ingredients cool down completely.
Once cooled, transfer the mixture into a mixer grinding jar & add garlic, ginger, onion, tamarind, fresh coconut, turmeric powder, water & grind it into a smooth paste. Do not add a lot of water, keep the paste thick.
To make the curry, set a stock pot over high flame, add the coconut oil & let it get hot.
Add mustard seeds, let them crackle then add curry leaves, green chilli & onion, stir well & cook over medium high flame until the onions turn soft.
Next, add the prepare paste, stir well & cook over high flame for 4-5 minutes or until a slight glaze appears on the paste.
Add hot water to make a thin curry, stir well & bring it to a boil then let it simmer for 12-15 minutes over medium flame. The curry will cook nicely & thicken up, which will completely change the flavour profile of the gravy.
Meanwhile, as an optional step, you can add a pinch of salt & some lemon juice to the prawns, it will get rid of any off-smell present in the prawns.
After simmering the curry for 12-15 minutes, add the prawns, stir it gently & cook for 5-6 more minutes until the prawns get fully cooked. 
Once the prawns get cooked, you can adjust the consistency of they curry as per your preference, cook it further for a few more minutes if you want the curry to be thick or add hot water if you like the curry to be thin.
Taste & adjust salt if required & optionally you can also add some coconut milk.
Your super delicious, quick & easy Mangalorean Prawn Curry is ready. Serve piping hot with neer dosa, cooked rice or boiled matta rice (red rice).

title: Gobi Aloo Matar Masala Recipe | Dhaba Style | हलवाई जैसी गोभी आलू मटर की सब्जी़ | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
Full Written Recipe - Dhaba Style Rassewale Gobi Aloo Matar

Prep time: 30-35 minutes
Cooking time: 45-50 minutes
Serves: 5-6 people

Ingredients:
MUSTARD OIL | सरसों का तेल 1-2 LADLES
CAULIFLOWER | फूलगोभी 1 MEDIUM SIZED. (FLORETS)
POTATO | आलू 3 MEDIUM SIZED. (DICED)
WHOLE SPICES
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1 TSP
CINNAMON | दालचीनी 2 INCH
BALCK CARDAMOM | बड़ी इलायची 1 NO.
BAY LEAF | तेज पत्ता 2 NO. 
ONION | प्याज़ 4 MEDIUM SIZED (CHOPPED)
GINGER GARLIC PASTE | अदरक लहसुन की पेस्ट 2 TBSP
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS. (CHOPPED)
POWDERED SPICES
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1 TSP
CORIANDER POWDER | धनिया पाउडर 1.5 TBSP
CUMIN POWDER | जीरा पाउडर 1 TSP
SPICY RED CHILLI | तीखी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 2 TSP
BLACK SALT | काला नमक 1/2 TSP
GRAM FLOUR | बेसन 1 TBSP
HOT WATER | गरम पानी A SPLASH
TOMATO PUREE | टमाटर की पूरी OF 2 TOMATOES 
CURD | दही 1/2 CUP (WHISKED)
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
GREEN PEAS | हरे मटर 1/2 CUP
HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED
GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला A PINCH
ROASTED KASURI METHI POWDER | भुनी कसूरी मेथी का पाउडर A LARGE PINCH
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS. (SLICED)
GINGER JULIENNE | अदरक के लच्छे 1 INCH
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
Method:
Cut the cauliflower into medium sized florets & rinse them in salted water. Cut the potatoes into medium sized dice & keep them submerged into the water.
Set a kadhai over high flame, add mustard oil & heat it until the oil starts smoking. Then lower the flame & let the oil drop in temperature slightly.
Discard the water from cauliflower & potatoes, add them into the oil & cook over high flame for 5-6 minutes or until they get 90% cooked. You don’t have to cook them completely at this stage.
Once they get 90% cooked, transfer them into a bowl & set it aside.
In the same kadhai add all the whole spices & onions, stir well & cook over medium high flame until the onions turn light golden brown.
Further add ginger garlic paste & green chillies, stir well & cook for a few more minutes, the onions will also turn completely golden brown.
Next, lower the flame & add all the powdered spices, gram flour, stir well & then add a splash of hot water, cook over medium high flame for 4-5 minutes.
Then add the tomato puree, whisked curd & salt, stir well & cook over high flame for 12-15 minutes or until the oil gets separated from the gravy.
Once the oil is released, add in the green peas, stir well then cover & cook for 6-8 minutes until the peas are cooked.
Adjust the consistency by adding hot water, you can keep the consistency as per your preference, I like to keep it semi-thick.
Add the cooked cauliflower & potatoes, cook for a few minutes in the gravy.
Lastly taste & adjust salt if required & add garam masala, kasuri methi powder, green chillies, ginger & coriander, stir well.
Your delicious Dhaba/Halwai Style Gobi Aloo Matar is ready, serve it with some hot rotis, parathas or puris.

title: Eggless Mango Milk Cake Recipe in a Pan | No Oven | बिना ओवन के बनाइये मैंगो केक | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
Mango Milk Cake (No oven, No Microwave, No Cooker)
Prep time: 10-15 minutes
Cooking time: 45-50 minutes
Serves: 8 slices

Ingredients:
Vanilla Sponge
MILK | दूध 1 CUP / 240 ML (LUKEWARM)
VINEGAR | सिरका 2 TSP
BUTTER | मख्हन 1/3 CUP / 70 GRAMS
VANILLA ESSENCE | वैनिला एसेंस1 TSP
REFINED FLOUR | मैदा 1 CUP / 160 GRAMS
POWDERED SUGAR | पीसी हुई शक्कर 1/2 CUP / 70 GRAMS
BAKING POWDER | बेकिंग पाउडर 1 TSP 
BAKING SODA | बेकिंग सोडा 1/2 TSP
Mango flavoured milk mixtture
MILK | दूध 2 CUPS / 480 ML
CONDENSED MILK | कंडेंस्ड मिल्क 1/2 CUP / 160 GRAMS
FRESH CREAM | फ्रेसज क्रीम 1/2 CUP / 120 GRAMS
MANGO | आम 1 NO. 
Toppings / Garnish
WHIPPPED CREAM | व्हिप्पड क्रीम 
MANGO | आम
Method:
Add the milk to a bowl, then add the vinegar. Stir gently and let it rest for 2–3 minutes. This acts as a buttermilk replacement, the vinegar helps make the cake softer.
Next, add the melted butter and vanilla to the milk mixture, and whisk well to make a lump-free wet mixture.
Place a sieve over the bowl and add the refined flour along with all the remaining dry ingredients. Sift everything together.
Whisk until a smooth, lump-free batter is formed. Do not over-whisk.
Transfer the batter into a non-stick fry pan and tap it a few times gently to release any air bubbles.
Place the pan on the stove, cover it with a lid, and cook on the lowest possible flame for 15 minutes. If the lid has a steam vent, seal it with a piece of foil.
While the sponge is baking, prepare the mango-flavoured milk. Cut the mango and add all the pulp to a mixer jar. Add a few ladles of milk and blend. Strain the mixture and combine it with the remaining milk. Add condensed milk and fresh cream, stir well & the mango-flavoured milk is ready.
After 15 minutes of cooking, remove the lid and check the sponge. The sides and edges will be cooked, but the top may look slightly raw. Switch off the flame, cover the pan again, and let it rest for 10–15 more minutes. The residual heat will finish the cooking.
Once rested, open the lid and check doneness by inserting a toothpick, it should come out clean. Let the sponge cool completely before demoulding. Use a spatula to lift it out gently, it should release easily.
Carefully flip it onto a large plate or chopping board and cut it into 8 equal slices. Poke holes into the sponge using a fork.
Pour a few ladles of the mango milk onto the sponge, ensuring you cover it evenly, including the sides and edges. Reserve the remaining milk to serve later.
Cover with a large lid and set it in the fridge for a few minutes to allow the sponge to soak the milk. Meanwhile, whip the cream and chop mangoes for topping.
Once cooled and set, serve the delicious mango milk cake. Decorate with whipped cream and chopped mangoes, and pour some of the mango-flavoured milk around each slice while serving.

Moong Dal Bhajiya - https://youtu.be/_uP5iICpVsc?si=IY8g3KYBVdQxxJeO
Dahi Papdi Chaat - https://youtu.be/6l7MecdJjDs?si=ZuD2kyuRKToh0ly-


Written recipe - Meethi Chutney

Prep time: 10 minutes (30 minutes soaking time)
Cooking time: 45-50 minutes

Ingredients:
TAMARIND | इमली 75 GRAMS (DESEEDED)
DATES | खजूर 250 GRAMS (DESEEDED)
HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED 
JAGGERY | गुड़ 750 GRAMS (CHOPPED)
GINGER POWDER | सोंठ पाउडर 1 TSP
BLACK PEPPER POWDER | काली मिर्च पाउडर 1/4 TSP
ROASTED CUMIN POWDER | भुना जीरा पाउडर 1 TBSP
BLACK SALT | काला नमक 1 TSP
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च 1 TBSP
SALT | नमक 1 TSP
WATER | पानी 1 LITRE
Method:
Add seedless dates & seedless tamarind to a bowl. Pour enough hot water to fully submerge them, cover & let them soak for 30 minutes so they soften up.
Once soaked, transfer them to a mixer grinder & blend into a smooth, lump-free paste, you can add more water if required.
Strain the paste using a sieve into a heavy-bottomed stock pot to remove any fibrous bits from the tamarind, nothing else will be collected in the sieve.
Add all the remaining chutney ingredients to the pot, including jaggery & spices & turn the flame to high. Stir continuously until the jaggery melts completely.
Once the mixture comes to a boil, reduce the heat to medium & let is simmer. Remove the scum that will start to float on the surface.
Keep cooking while stirring at regular intervals. Let it simmer for about 30-40 minutes. The chutney will get darker & thicker as it cooks & reduces.
After about 30 minutes of cooking, it will get thick enough to coat & glaze the back of the spoon. You can stop cooking the chutney at this stage if you like it a bit runny but still slightly thick. It will also thicken up slightly once it cools down. 
I like the chutney to be slightly thicken, a proper glazing & coating consistency so I cook it for 10 more minutes, total simmering time of 40 minutes.
Turn off the heat & let the chutney cool completely. It will continue to thicken as it cools to a perfect chutney consistency that I prefer & is perfect for all types of dishes.
Your meethi chutney is ready! Store in a sterilized, airtight jar, refrigerate for a few weeks (upto 2 months) or freeze in batches which will last for 6 months to a year.

title: Gujarati Dal Recipe | Quick Khatti Meethi Toor Dal | गुजराती खट्टी मीठी दाल | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
Full written recipe - Gujarati Dal (Khatti Meethi Dal)
Prep time: 10-15 minutes
Cooking time: 20-25 minutes
Serves: 3-4 people

Ingredients:
Boiled Toor Dal
TOOR DAL | तूर दाल 1 CUP
TAMARIND | इमली 2 TBSP
HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED 
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/2 TSP
WATER | पानी 3 CUPS
PEANUTS | मूंगफली 2 TBSP
Tempering & Final Cooking
GHEE | घी 2 TBSP
MUSTARD SEEDS | राई 1/2 TSP
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1/2 TSP
CINNAMON STICK | दालचीनी 1/2 INCH
CLOVES | लौंग 3-4 NOS. 
RED CHILLI | लाल मिर्च 2 NOS. 
FENUGREEK SEEDS | मेथी के बीज A PINCH
ASAFOETIDA | हींग 1/2 TSP
CURRY LEAVES 15 NOS. 
GINGER CHILLI PASTE | अदरक मिर्च की पेस्ट 1 TBSP
TOMATO | टमाटर 1 MEDIUM SIZED. (CHOPPED)
SALT | नमक A TO TASTE
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/4 TSP
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1/4 TSP
HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED
TAMARIND PULP | इमली का पल्प 4 TBSP
JAGGERY | गुड़ 2-3 TBSP
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
Method:
Wash the toor dal 2-3 times until the added water turns clear & soak for 30 minutes to an hour.
Add a large lemon sized ball of tamarind into a bowl, add hot water & let it soak until you do the further processes.
Once soaked, discard the water & add the dal into a pressure cooker & add water, turmeric powder & salt.
Place a steel bowl over the dal in the cooker & add the peanuts into the bowl, put the cooker’s lid on & cook the dal over high flame for 4-5 whistles, then switch off the flame & let the cooker de-pressurize.
Once de-pressurized open the lid, remove the peanuts & set them aside, then blend the dal using a whisk or an emulsion blender, the dal should become smooth.
Mash the soaked tamarind to remove all of its pulp & then strain it using a sieve to get tamarind pulp.
To prepare the tempering, set a deep pan or a stock pot over high flame & once it gets hot add in the ghee & let the ghee heat as well.
Once the ghee gets hot add the cumin seeds, mustard seeds, cloves, cinnamon, red chillies, fenugreek seeds, asafoetida, curry leaves, ginger chilli paste, stir & cook over high flame briefly.
Next, add the tomatoes with salt & cook over high flame until the tomatoes turn mushy.
Add turmeric powder & kashmiri red chilli powder, stir well & add the cooked toor dal, add hot water to adjust the consistency, keep the dal a little watery at this stage as it needs to simmer for a while, it will thicken up gradually while cooking.
Add tamarind pulp, jaggery & the peanuts, let the dal come to a boil, then taste & add salt according to your preference, you can also add more tamarind pulp & jaggery as per your preference. Gujarati dal is not at all about only sweetness, it needs to have the right balance of jaggery & tamarind.
Cook until the dal reaches the right consistency, it should not be too thick nor too thin & lastly add fresh coriander.
Your super delicious & comforting Gujarati Dal is ready. For the best meal experience, have it with hot rice & serve some salad, papad, pickle & chaas on the side, do not forget to add some more ghee on the top.

title: Khatte Aloo | Chatpata Laal Batata Mumbai Style Recipe | चटपटे खट्टे आलू | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
Full written recipe - Mumbai Style Khatte Aloo (Laal Batata)

Prep time: 15-20 minutes
Cooking time: 10-15 minutes
Serves: 3-4 people

Laal Batata
POTATO | आलू 5-6 MEDIUM SIZED. (BOILED)
OIL | तेल 2 TBSP
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1/2 TSP
MUSTARD SEEDS | राई 1/2 TSP
CURRY LEAVES | कड़ी पत्ता 20 NOS. 
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS. (SLICED)
GINGER GARLIC PASTE | अदरक लहसुन की पेस्ट 2 TBSP
TAMARIND PULP | इमली का पल्प 2 TBSP
SUGAR | शक्कर A LARGE PINCH
HOT WATER | गरम पानी A SPLASH
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/4 TSP
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1 TBSP
GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला 1/4 TSP
BLACK SALT | काला नमक A PINCH
CUMIN POWDER | जीरा पाउडर A PINCH 
CHAAT MASALA | चाट मसाला A PINCH
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
HOT WATER | गरम पानी A SPLASH
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
PAV | पाव AS REQUIRED (FOR SERVING)
Method:
Peel the boiled potatoes & cut them into quarters, the pieces should be big.
Set a frypan over high flame, add oil & let it get hot.
Add cumin & mustard seeds, let them crackle & then add curry leaves, green chilli & ginger garlic paste, stir & cook over high flame for 1-2 minutes.
Next, add tamarind pulp, sugar & a splash of hot water, cook for 3-4 minutes or until the raw smell of tamarind goes away.
Lower the flame & add all the powdered spices along with salt & a splash of hot water, stir well & then cook over high flame until the oil gets separated.
Further add the cut potato pieces & coat them with the prepared masala, taste & adjust salt if required & add fresh coriander. Your delicious Mumbai style Chatpata Laal Batata is ready.
I like to serve these with some pav, directly toasted over flame, you can also serve it as a starter with the chutney.

Green Chutney
Ingredients:
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया 1 CUP 
MINT | पुदीना 1/2 CUP
GARLIC | लहसुन 2 CLOVES
GINGER | अदरक 1 INCH
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 5-6 NOS. 
JAGGERY | गुड़ 1 TSP
TAMARIND | इमली 1 TSP
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
BLACK SALT | काला नमक A LARGE PINCH
ICE CUBE | बर्फ 1 NO.
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED 
Method:
Add all the ingredients of the chutney into a mixer jar & grind it into a fine chutney. Your delicious green chutney is ready.

title: Cheesy Omelette Butter Masala Recipe | Best Egg Curry | चीज़ ऑमलेट बटर मसाला | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
Full written recipe - Cheesy Omelette Butter Masala

Prep time: 15-20 minutes
Cooking time : 1 hour 
Serves: 5-6 people

Ingredients:
Puree
TOMATO | टमाटर 4 MEDIUM SIZED. (ROUGHLY CHOPPED)
CORIANDER STEMS | धनिया के डंठल A HANDFUL 
CASHEW | काजू 15 NOS. (BOILED)
Onion mixture 
ONION | प्याज़ 3 MEDIUM SIZED. (ROUGHLY SLICED)
GARLIC | लहसुन 12 CLOVES
GINGER | अदरक 2 INCH
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS. 
Gravy
OIL | तेल 2-3 TBSP
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा A PINCH 
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1 TBSP
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/4 TSP
GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला 1/4 TSP
CORIANDER POWDER | धनिया पाउडर 1 TSP
HOT WATER | गरम पानी A SPLASH
SALT | नमक TO TASTE 
SUGAR | शक्कर A PINCH
HOT WATER | garam पानी AS REQUIRED 
BUTTER | मक्खन 2 TBSP
FRESH CREAM | फ्रेश क्रीम 2 TBSP
ROASTED KASURI METHI POWDER | भुनी कसूरी मेथी पावडर A PINCH 
GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला A PINCH
FRESH CORIANDER A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
SALT | नमक IF REQUIRED 
Omelette 
EGGS | अंडे 2 NOS. (For 1 portion) Or AS REQUIRED 
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
BLACK PEPPER | काली मिर्च A PINCH (FRESHLY CRUSHED)
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1/4 TSP
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
ONION | प्याज़ AS REQUIRED (CHOPPED)
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 1 NO. (CHOPPED)
BUTTER | मक्खन 1 TBSP
OIL | तेल 1 TSP

Method:
Add all the ingretof the puree into a mixer jar & grind it into a fine puree. 
Add onion, garlic, ginger & green chilli into a chopper & chop them finely. 
Set a kadhai over high heat, let it get hot then add oil & let the oil heat up as well. 
Once the oil gets hot, add the cumin seeds & let them crackle.
Next, add the chopped onion mixture, stir well & cook over medium high flame until the onions turn light golden brown.
Once the onions turn light golden brown, lower the flame & add all the powdered spices along with a splash of hot water, stir & cook over high flame until the oil separates.
Add the prepared tomato puree along with salt & sugar, stir well & cook over high flame until the the gravy starts to leave the pan on itself, try using as little water as possible.
Once the puree is cooked, add hot water to adjust the consistency, it should be seni-thick.
Then add the butter, cream, kasuri methi, garam masala & fresh coriander, stir well & taste to adjust salt, the gravy is ready. 
Now this gravy can be served with total 6 eggs, I like to make omelettes of 2 eggs & serve it in 3 different portions, you can adjust or make according to your preference.
Crack the eggs in a bowl add all the ingredients & whisk well. 
Add butter & oil into a hot pan, let it get hot then pour the eggs & make an omelette.
Cook & loads of grated processed cheese & fold the omelette as per your preference. 
To serve, place the omelette onto a plate & pour the hot gravy on top.
Your delicious, omelette butter masala is ready.

title: Easy Thandai Recipe | No Syrup No Powder | आसान और स्वादिष्ट ठंडाई | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
Celebrate Holi with 10% OFF sitewide
Use code COLORFUL10 at checkout: https://www.yflhome.com

Customize your BlendLab Pro 2.0 : https://yflhome.gotrackier.com/click?campaign_id=34&pub_id=12

Full written recipe - Thandai
Prep time: 5 minutes (overnight soaking)
Process time: 10-15 minutes
Serves: 6-8 people

Ingredients:
ALMOND | बादाम 2 TBSP
PISTACHIO | पिस्ता 1.5 TBSP
MELON SEEDS | मगज़ 1 TBSP
POPPY SEEDS | खस खस 1 TBSP
FENNEL SEEDS | सौंफ 1/2 TBSP
BLACK PEPPERCORNS | साबुत काली मिर्च 1 TBSP
GREEN CARDAMOM | हरी इलायची 4 NOS.
DRY ROSE PETALS | गुलाब की पंखुड़ियां 1 TBSP
SAFFRON | केसर A PINCH 
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED 
ROCK SUGAR | मिश्री 4 TBSP
GINGER POWDER | सोंठ पाउडर 1/2 TSP
NUTMEG | जयफल A PINCH (GRATED)
CHILLED MILK | ठंडा दूध 1 LITRE (ADDED GRADUALLY)
RABDI | रबड़ी 150 GRAMS
FRESH CURD | ताज़ा दही 100 GRAMS
Method:
Add almonds, pistachio, melon, poppy & fennel seeds, black peppercorns, green cardamom, dry rose petals & saffron into a bowl & add water to submerge all the ingredients completely, cover & let them soak for 5-6 hours or overnight.
Once soaked, add the mixture into a mixer jar along with the water, then add rock sugar, ginger powder, nutmeg & 300-350 ml of chilled milk, blend it into a fine mixture. Do not blend for a very long period of time, you need the mixture to remain cold.
Place a sieve over a large stock pot, keep a muslin cloth over it & add the blended mixture.
Stir & let the milk drain out through the cloth & sieve, then bring the corners together, twist & squeeze out all the remaining milk.
The thandai concentrate will be collected in the stock pot.
Collect & add the leftovers of the paste back into the mixer jar, add 300-350ml of more chilled milk & blend it again & repeat the straining process, this will help to extract all the flavours from the nuts, seeds & spices.
Add this stage your basic thandai is ready, you can taste, adjust sweetness if required, chill & serve it but, this recipe also includes the addition of rabdi & fresh curd to enhance the texture & richness of the rabdi.
In a bowl add the rabdi, fresh curd & remaining milk & whisk it well, then just using the sieve, add the mixture into the thandai, this will only take a few seconds & mix well.
You delicious, rich & creamy thandai is ready, do not add ice to chill it, instead just place it in the fridge for sometime & then serve, you can also optionally garnish it with some saffron strands & slivers nuts.

Full Written Recipe - Paneer Tawa Masala
Prep time: 5-10 minutes
Cooking time: 40-45 minutes
Serves: 4-5 people

Ingredients:
Prep
ONION | प्याज़ 3 MEDIUM SIZED.
TOMATO | टमाटर 1 MEDIUM SIZED.
GREEN PASTE
GARLIC | लहसुन 20 CLOVES
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 4-5 NOS. 
GINGER | अदरक 2 INCH
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A SMALL HANDFUL 
WATER | पानी VERY LITTLE 
Fried Paneer
PANEER | पनीर 500 GRAMS 
OIL | तेल 2-3 TBSP (FOR SHALLOW FRYING)
Final Cooking
OIL | तेल 2-3 TBSP
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा A LARGE PINCH 
GARLIC | लहसुन 20 CLOVES
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/4 TSP
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1 TSP
GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला 1/4 TSP
AGRI MASALA | आगरी मसाला 1 TBSP
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
KOKUM AGAL | कोकम अगल 1 TBSP
HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED 
GHEE | घी 2 TBSP (MELTED)
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
Method:
Chop the onions & tomatoes separately using a chopper or a knife.
For the green paste, add the ginger, garlic, green chilli, fresh coriander & water into a mixer jar & grind it into a fine paste.
Cut the paneer into medium sized rectangular pieces & shallow fry them in a pan until light golden brown from two sides, shallow frying will ensure that the paneer pieces hold their shape during the final cooking process of the recipe.
Set a tawa over high heat, add the oil & let it get hot. 
Add the cumin seeds & onions, stir well & cook over medium high flame until the onions turn golden brown.
Once the onions turn light golden brown, add the green paste, garlic cloves & continue to cook over medium high flame until the onions turn golden brown while stirring. 
If the masala is getting too dry then you can add some hot water & continue to cook it further. 
Lower the flame & add all the powdered spices, a splash of hot water, stir well & cook over high flame for 1-2 minutes or until the oil separates.
Agri masala suits this recipe the best but if you don’t have it, you can substitute it by using ½ a tablespoon of garam masala & ½ to 1 tbsp of spicy red chilli powder depending on your spice preference. 
Further add tomato & salt, stir & cook until the oil separates again, you can add splashes of hot water while cooking if the masala gets too dry.
Next, add kokum agal & cook for 2-3 minutes, you can also use tamarind water as a substitute but try using kokum for the best results.
Once the masala is cooked, add some hot water to adjust the consistency & make a semi thin gravy at this stage so that it can coat the paneer pieces.
Place the fried paneer pieces in the tawa, spread evenly & flip the pieces & coat them with the masala.
Add more hot water to adjust the consistency if required then continue to cook for a few minutes until the masala coats the paneer nicely & becomes thick.
Lastly add melted ghee & fresh coriander, your delicious paneer tawa masala is ready. Serve hot with some pav or bhakri or even rice.

description:
Full written recipe - Lauki Chilla

Prep time: 5-10 minutes
Cooking time: 5-10 minutes
Serves: 5-6 people

Ingredients:
BOTTLE GOURD | लौकी 1/2 MEDIUM SIZED
CARROT | गाजर 1 MEDIUM SIZED. 
ONION | प्याज़ 1 NO. (CHOPPED)
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS. (CHOPPED)
GINGER | अदरक 2 TSP (CHOPPED)
CAROM SEEDS | अजवाइन 1/2 TSP (CRUSHED)
WHITE SESAME SEEDS | सफ़ेद तिल 1 TSP
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा A LARGE PINCH

RICE FLOUR | चावल का आटा 1/2 CUP
SORGHUM FLOUR | ज्वार का आटा 1/2 CUP

Method:
Peel the bottle gourd & carrot, then grate them into a large bowl using a large hole grater. 
Add all the other ingredients & mix well before adding any water.
Gradually add water while mixing & make a semi-thick batter, similar to dosa.
To cook the cheela, set a pan over high flame & let it get moderately hot.
Place a ladle full of batter & spread it to make the cheela, I like to keep it slightly thin, cook the cheela over medium low flame.
Once the batter sets on the top, add ghee & cook the cheela until the bottom layer is set.
Flip the cheela, add more ghee & cook until the cheela turns golden brown from both sides.
Your delicious, quick & easy lauki ka cheela is ready.

Full written recipe - Vrat Ka Paratha (Rajgira ka Paratha)

Prep time: 15-20 minutes
Cooking time: 20-25 minutes
Serves: 4-5 people

Ingredients:
Dough
PEANUTS | मूंगफली 1/2 CUP
AMARANTH FLOUR | राजगिरा का आटा 1.5 CUPS / 200 GRAMS
WHITE SESAME SEEDS | सफेद तिल 1 TSP
ROCK SALT | सेंधा नमक TO TASTE
BLACK PEPPER | काली मिर्च 1/2 TSP (FRESHLY CRUSHED)
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा A PINCH
GINGER GREEN CHILLI PASTE | अदरक और हरी मिर्च की पेस्ट 1 TSP
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
POTATO | आलू 1 LARGE SIZED (GRATED)
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED 
GHEE | घी AS REQUIRED (for cooking)
Green Chutney
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया 1 CUP
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS. 
ROASTED PEANUTS | भुनी हुई मूंगफली REMAINING 
LEMON JUICE | नींबू का रस OF HALF LEMON 
ROCK SALT | सेंधा नमक 1/2 TSP
SUGAR | शक्कर 1 TBSP 
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा A PINCH
ICE CUBE | बर्फ 1 NO. (FOR COLOUR)
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED 
Method:
Dry roast the peanuts in a hot pan until the turn golden brown, then transfer them into a bowl & let them cool down completely.
Chop the peanuts using a knife or a chopped into a coarse powder, do not make a fine powder.
Add the amaranth flour into a large bowl along with add the remaining ingredients of the dough, except for wter then mix all the ingredient using your hands.
Further, gradually add very little water & combine all the dry flour, you need the dough to be semi-stiff, it should not be too soft or it will b very challenging to roll it out into a paratha.
There is no gluten in amaranth flour so there is no need to knead it a lot.
Cover the dough with a slightly damp cloth & let it rest for 10 minutes.
Meanwhile, add all the ingredients of they chutney into a mixer jar & grind it into a fine chutney.
Before you start rolling the paratha, set a tawa over medium high heat & let it get hot.
Take a small portion of the dough & form it into a roundel then slight press it to flatten it.
Coat it with some dry amaranth flour & then gently roll it out into a paratha.
Once the tawa gets hot, gently lift & place the paratha on the tawa & let it cook from one side over medium high flame, do not add ghee at this stage, cook it dry.
Flip the paratha & cook it similarly on the other side until light golden-brown spots start to appear.
Then add ghee & cook until both the sides turn golden brown.
Your delicious Rajgira Paratha is ready, serve it with the special green chutney & sweet curd.

title: Mutton Kharda Recipe | Spicy Maharashtrian Green Mutton | महाराष्ट्र मटन खर्डा | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
Kala Mutton Recipe link - https://youtu.be/7oJZ1rxLkIQ?si=NMy9B9L6bBh84EP9

Written recipe - Mutton Kharda 

Prep time: 20-25 minutes
Cooking time: 1 hr 30 minutes
Serves: 4-5 people

Ingredients:
Green Paste
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 6 NOS. 
GARLIC | लहसुन 15 CLOVES
GINGER | अदरक 1 INCH
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया 2 TBSP 
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED 
Marination
MUTTON | मटन 1 KG
GREEN PASTE | हरी पेस्ट 2 TBSP
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/2 TSP
Cooking Mutton
OIL | तेल 2 TBSP
ONION | प्याज़ 1 MEDIUM SIZED. (CHOPPED)
HOT WATER  | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED 
Making Kharda (paste)
DRY COCONUT | सुखा नारियल 1/2 CUP (GRATED)
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 20 NOS. 
GARLIC | लहसुन 30 CLOVES
GINGER | अदरक 2 INCH
OIL | तेल 1 TSP
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा A PINCH
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A LARGE HANDFUL 
FRESH COCONUT | ताज़ा नारियल 1/4 CUP (GRATED)
MINT | पुदीना 8-10 LEAVES 
WATER | प्याज़ AS REQUIRED 
Final Cooking
GHEE | घी AS REQUIRED 
OIL | तेल 1 TBSP
ONION | प्याज़ 1 MEDIUM SIZE. (CHOPPED)
GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला A PINCH
FRESH CORIANDER  | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)

Method:
To make the green paste add the green chilli, garlic, ginger & fresh coriander into a mixer jar & grind it into a fine paste, some portion of this paste will be used in the marinade & the remaining will be added directly into the kharda in the end. 
Add the washed & clean mutton into a large bowl, add the green paste, salt & turmeric powder & mix well.
Now to cook the mutton, set a pressure cooker over high flame, add oil & let it get hot. 
Add onion, stir well & cook over high flame until the onions turn slightly golden brown.
Next, add the mutton & stir, cook over high flame for 4-5 minutes until you start seeing some colour on the mutton.
Further add hot water, the water level should be just below half of the mutton, we do not need a lot of stock for this recipe but if you like having mutton soup then you can add more hot water. 
Put the lid on & pressure cook the mutton over medium high flame for 5-6 whistles, we only have to cook the mutton until it's 80-90% done.
Once cooked, switch off the flame & let the cooker depressurize naturally, then open the lid & check the mutton, it should be slightly under done, if you cook it fully at this stage then when you add it to the kharda it will just disintegrate. 
Strain the mutton pieces & add them into a separate bowl & remove the stock into a separate bowl, cover & set aside until you make the kharda.
Set a pan over high flame & heat it up. 
Once hot, lower the flame & add the dry coconut & dry roast it while stirring continuously until it turns golden brown, then transfer it into a bowl. 
In the same pan add the garlic, green chillies & ginger, & cook over high flame while stirring until some light golden brown spots start to appear on the chillies.
Then add the oil & cumin seeds, stir well & cook for a few more seconds then transfer this mixture into the same bowl & let all the ingredients cool down completely. 
Next, add the ingredients into a mixer jar along with fresh coriander, fresh coconut, mint leaves, some water & grind it into a paste.
For the final cooking, set a kadhai or a flat bottomed vessel over high flame, add ghee & oil, let it get hot. 
Add the onions, stir well & cook until it turns slightly light golden brown.
Next, add the mutton & cook for 1-2 minutes over high flame while stirring continuously.
Then add the prepared kharda, stir well & add 3-4 ladles (600-700ml) of the stock & cook over high flame for 8-10 minutes, the masala will get cooked & it will reach a beautiful coating consistency.
During this cooking process the mutton will also get fully cooked. 
Once the masala has reached a coating consistency & the mutton is cooked, taste & adjust salt if required along with garam masala & the remaining green paste that was reserved in the beginning, stir well & lastly add fresh coriander.
Your delicious & spicy Mutton Kharda is ready, serve it with your choice of bhakri and rice.

title: Cheesy Paneer Bread Roll Recipe | Perfect Tips | कुरकुरे चीज़ पनीर ब्रेड रोल्स | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
Full written recipe - Cheesy Paneer Bread Roll

Prep time: 5-10 minutes
Cooking time: 15-20 minutes
Serves: 4-5 people

Ingredients:

Filling

PANEER | पनीर 250 GRAMS
PROCESSED CHEESE | प्रोसेस्ड चीज़ 50-100 GRAMS
MIXED BELL PEPPERS | मिक्सड बेल पेपर्स 3/4 CUP (CHOPPED) 
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS. (CHOPPED)
GINGER | अदरक 1 TSP (CHOPPED)
SPRING ONION GREENS | हरे प्याज़ के पत्ते A HANDFUL 
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
OREGANO | ऑरिगेनो A PINCH 
CHILLI FLAKES | चिली फ्लैक्स A PINCH
DRY MANGO POWDER | आमचूर पाउडर A PINCH
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
SANDWICH BREAD SLICES | सैंडविच ब्रेड स्लाइसिस AS REQUIRED (to make breadrolls)

Method:
Crush & add the paneer into a large bowl then grate & add the cheese using a large hole grate. 
Add all the ingredients of the filling & mix well using a spoon, the filling is ready.
To shape the bread rolls, take the bread slices & make sure you use the large size, stack them onto a chopping board & trim off the sides, this step is very important because if you keep the sides then the bread roll won't seal properly which will make the bread roll oily.
Now to shape the bread roll, take one slice & dip it into water, make sure it wet on both the sides, then place the slice between your palms & squeeze out the excess moisture, squeeze it enough to flatten the bread completely.
Flatten the bread slice is crucial as it eliminates the porous texture of the bread, it will again soak in oil while frying if it's not flattened completely. 
Now take a handful of the filling, gently squeeze it to bring it together, place it in the center of the bread & bring the corners together, then using your palm & finger press it while rotating it to form an oval / egg shaped bread roll.
There should be no cracks, shape all the bread rolls similarly & let them rest for 10 minutes. 
Meanwhile, heat the oil in a deep pan/kadhai until it gets moderately hot (175° C).
Once rested, add 3-4 bread rolls into the hot oil carefully & fry over high flame until crisp & golden brown. 
Strain then into a sieve & serve hot with ketchup or any dip of your choice.

title: Instant Vegetable Rava Dhokla | Quick Breakfast Recipe | स्वादिष्ट राव ढोकला | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:

Full written Recipe - Instant Rava Dhokla

Prep time: 10-15 minutes
Cooking time: 20-25 minutes
Serves: 5-6 people

Ingredients:
Batter
COARSE SEMOLINA | जड़ा राव 1 CUP 
SOUR CURD | खट्टा दही 1 CUP 
WATER | पानी 1/2 CUP
GINGER CHILLI PASTE | अदरक और मिर्च की पेस्ट 1 TBSP
SUGAR | शक्कर 1/2 TSP
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
CARROT | गाजर 1 SMAL SIZED.
CAPSICUM | शिमला मिर्च 1 SMALL SIZED. 
GHEE | घी 1 TSP
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1 PINCH
OIL | तेल AS REQUIRED 
SWEET CORN | स्वीट कॉर्न 1/4 CUP (BOILED)
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED 
ENO | ईनो 1 TSP
WATER | पानी 1 TSP
Tempering 
GROUNDNUT OIL | मूंगफली का तेल 2-3 TBSP
MUSTARD SEEDS | राई 2 TSP
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 4 NOS. (SLICED)
WHITE SESAME SEEDS | सफेद तिल 2 TSP
ASAFOETIDA | हींग 1/2 TSP
CURRY LEAVES | कड़ी पत्ते 15 NOS.
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)

Method:
Add the semolina into a large bowl along with sour curd, water, ginger chilli paste, sugar, salt & whisk well, cover & let the batter rest while you do the further process.
Add the carrot & capsicum into a chopper & chop them up roughly, don't keep it too chunky.
Set a small pan over high heat, add ghee & let it heat up. 
Add cumin seeds & let them crackle, then add the chopped capsicum & carrot, stir & cook over high flame for 2 minutes then transfer into a bowl & cool it down completely. 
Meanwhile, set the steamer over high flame & keep it ready & also grease the dhokla trays with some oil. 
Once the veggies have cooled, add them into the batter along with sweet corn & mix well, add water if required to adjust the consistency, the batter should be semi- thick, it should be in pouring consistency but not running, just like thick idli batter. 
Add eno with some water, stir well & make sure that the eno gets completely with the batter.
Pour the batter into the dhokla trays, this recipe will make 2 trays. 
After adding the batter, spread it evenly on the trays then gently tap it a few times.
Place the trays in the steamer for 15 minutes. 
Once steamed, check by inserting a knife, it should come out clean. 
Remove the trays from the steamer & let them cool down.
Once cooked, cut as per your preference.
For the tempering set a pan over high flame, add oil & let it heat up.
Add mustard seeds, sesame seeds, asafoetida & let them crackle for a couple of seconds.
Then add curry leaves & pour the batter evenly onto the dhokla trays.
Your delicious rava dhokla is ready, peel them off the tray using a spatula & serve with green chutney.

Your tasty green chutney is ready.

title: Kolambi Bhaat Recipe | Maharashtrian Prawns Pulao | कोळंबी भात बनाने का तारीक | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
Full written recipe - Kolambi Bhaat (Prawns Pulao)
Prep time: 10-15 minutes
Cooking time: 40-45 minutes
Serves: 3-4 people

Ingredients:
BASMATI RICE | बासमती चावला 1 CUP / 200 GRAMS
PRAWNS | झींगे 1 CUP / 200 GRAMS (CLEANED & DEVIENED)
WATER | पानी 1 CUP / 225 ML
COCONUT MILK | नारियल का दूध 1/2 CUP / 125 ML
Green Paste (Vaatan)
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS. 
GARLIC | लहसुन 8-10 CLOVES
GINGER | अदरक 2 INCH
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A SMALL HANDFUL 
WATER | पानी VERY LITTLE 
Marination
PRAWNS | झींगे 
SALT | नमक A PINCH
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर A PINCH
LEMON JUICE | नींबू का रस OF HALF A LEMON 
GREEN VAATAN | हरा वाटन 1 TBSP
Cooking Process
OIL | तेल 2 TBSP
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1 TSP
STAR ANISE | चक्री फुल 1 NO. 
CINNAMON | दालचीनी 2 INCH
BAY LEAF | तेज पत्ता 1 NO. 
CLOVES | लौंग 2 NOS.
BLACK PEPPERCORNS | साबुत काली मिर्च 4-5 NOS. 
GREEN CARDAMOM | हरी इलायची 2 NOS.
BLACK CARDAMOM | बड़ी इलायची 1 NO. 
ONION | प्याज़ 2 MEDIUM SIZED. (SLICED)
GREEN PASTE | हरा वाटन REMAINING 
HOT WATER | गरम पानी A SPLASH
TOMATO 1 NO. (ROUGHLY CHOPPED)
SALT | नमक A PINCH 
HOT WATER | पानी A SPLASH
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1 TBSP
BIRYANI MASALA | बिरयानी मसाला 1 TSP
HOT WATER | गरम पानी A SPLASH
PRAWNS | झींगे (MARINATED)
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
MINT | पुदीना A FEW LEAVES (CHOPPED)
KOKUM | कोकम 2-3 NOS.
WATER | पानी 
COCONUT MILK | नारियल का दूध 
SALT | नमक TO TASTE 
GHEE | घी 2-3 TBSP
Method:
The perfect ratio of rice, prawns & water for making a prawns pulao is 1:1:1.5, the quantities for this recipe are as mentioned in the ingredients list. If you want to use only water then you can substitute coconut milk with water.
Wash the rice until the added water turns clear & let it soak while you are doing the further processes or for at least half an hour.
Add all the ingredients of the green paste into a mixer jar & grind it into a fine paste using very little water.
Marinate the prawns with salt, turmeric powder, lemon juice & green paste, set it in the fridge until you make the masala.
Set a flat-bottomed vessel over high flame & let it get hot, then add the oil & let it heat up as well.
Add all the whole spices, stir & let them crackle for a few seconds.
Add the onions, stir & cook them over medium high flame until they turn golden brown.
Next, add the green paste with a splash of hot water & cook for a minute.
Add the tomato with a pinch of salt, stir & cook until the tomato turns soft, if the masala becomes too dry to cook then add in a splash of hot water & continue to cook.
Once the tomato turns soft, lower the flame & add red chilli powder, biryani masala, hot water & stir well, cook over high flame until the oil gets separated.
Further add the prawns, fresh coriander, mint & kokum, gently stir them in the masala & cook over high flame for 2 minutes.
Drain all the water from the soaked rice & add the rice, stir gently & add the water & coconut milk.
Taste & adjust salt, bring the water to a simmer over high flame then cover the vessel with a lid, place a heavy weight on the lid, set the flame to low & let it cook for 12 minutes.
Then remove the lid & check, the pulao will be cooked perfectly with no liquid remaining, you can check from the sides, if there is stir liquid present then let it cook off over low flame without covering.
Do not stir the pulao at this stage, just switch off the flame, put the lid back on & let it rest for at least 20 minutes.
Once rested, gently stir the pulao using a spatula & serve hot.

title: Aloo Do Pyaza | Dhaba Style Recipe | ढाबे जैसा स्वादिष्ट आलू दो प्याज़ा | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
Writen Recipe - Aloo Do Pyaza
Prep time: 20-25 minutes
Cooking time: 45 minutes
Serves: 4-5 people

Ingredients:
Veggie Prep
POTATO | आलू 5 MEDIUM SIZED (PEELED)
ONION | प्याज़ 2 SMALL SIZED.
TOMATO | टमाटर 2 NOS. (FOR TOSSING)
CAPSICUM | शिमला मिर्च 1 LARGE SIZED.
ONION | प्याज़ 4 MEDIUM SIZED. (FOR GRAVY)
Frying Potato
MUSTARD OIL | सरसों का तेल AS REQUIRED (FOR SHALLOW FRYING)
Base Gravy
MUSTARD OIL | सरसों का तेल 2-3 TBSP
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा  1/2 TSP
CINNAMON STICK | दालचीनी 1 INCH
GREEN CARDAMOM | हरी इलायची 2 NOS. 
BLACK CARDAMOM | बड़ी इलायची 1 NO. 
CLOVES | लौंग 3 NOS. 
BLACK PEPPERCORNS | साबुत काली मिर्च 4-5 NOS. 
BAY LEAF | तेज पत्ता 1 NO. 
GARLIC | लहसुन 8-10 CLOVES
GINGER | अदरक 2 INCH
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS. 
CORIANDER STEMS | धनिए के डंठल A HANDFUL 
TOMATO TRIMMINGS| बचा हुआ टमाटर (ROUGHLY CHOPPED)
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
HOT WATER | गरम पानी A SPLASH 
CURD | दही 1/2 CUP 
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1 TBSP
SPICY RED CHILLI POWDER | तीखी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1 TSP
CORIANDER POWDER | धनिया पाउडर 1 TBSP
CUMIN POWDER | जीरा पाउडर 1 TSP
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/4 TSP
HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED 
Final Cooking
MUSTARD OIL | सरसों का तेल (REMAINING)
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा A PINCH 
CORIANDER SEEDS | साबुत धनिया1 TBSP (CRUSHED)
GINGER JULIENNE | अदरक के लच्छे 1 INCH
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS. (SLICED)
BLACK SALT | काला नमक A PINCH
CHAAT MASALA | चाट मसाला A PINCH
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
ROASTED KASURI METHI POWDER | भुनी कसूरी मेथी का पाउडर A PINCH
GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला A PINCH
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED
Method
Peel the potatoes, cut the potato in half then in quarters & cut the quarters into halves, you will get bite sized pieces, keep them submerged in water.
Cut the onions, capsicum & tomatoes (save the core & trimmings) into petals & roughly slice the remaining onions for the gravy.
In a deep pan, add the mustard oil & heat it over high flame until it begins to smoke, then switch off the flame & let the oil cool down slightly.
Once it is only moderately hot, strain the water from the potatoes & carefully add them into the oil, fry the potatoes over high flame while stirring at regular intervals until they get fully cooked & turn golden brown, then strain them out & transfer into a bowl & set aside.
Strain the remaining oil as well, this same oil will be used for the further cooking processes.
Add some of the oil back into the pan, heat it up & add all the whole spices, let them crackle for a few seconds & then add in the sliced onions, stir well & cook them over medium high flame until they turn golden brown.
Further, add garlic, ginger, green chilli, coriander stems, tomato trimmings, salt, stir well & then add a splash of hot water, cover & cook over medium to low flame until the tomatoes turn mushy.
Add all the powdered spices into the curd & mix well to make a smooth mixture, add this mixture into the pan once the tomatoes turn mushy, stir well & cook over high flame until the oil separates, keep adding splashes of hot water if the gravy becomes too dry to cook.
Once the oil separates, switch off the flame & let the mixture cool down completely, then add it into a mixer grinder jar along with some water & grind it into a smooth fine gravy.
In a pan or kadhai, add the remaining mustard oil & heat it up.
Add cumin seeds, coriander seeds, ginger, green chillies & the onion, tomato, capsicum petals, toss over high flame for a minute.
Then add the fried potatoes, saute for a couple of seconds over high flame & then add the prepared gravy along with black salt & chaat masala, stir well & cook for 2-3 minutes.
Taste & adjust salt if required & lastly add kasuri methi powder, garam masala, fresh coriander & stir well.
You can adjust the consistency of the dish as per your preference, if you want the gravy to be thick then do not add any hot water otherwise add some hot water.
Dhaba style Aloo Do Pyaza is ready, serve it with some tandoori roti & salad.

title: Korean Fried Crispy Potatoes Recipe | Sweet & Spicy Potato | कोरियन क्रिस्पी आलू | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
Gochujang Replacement: https://youtu.be/Ftd1Ex34hJQ?t=88

Full Recipe - Korean Crispy Fried Potato

Prep time: 10-15 minutes
Cooking time: 30-35 minutes
Serves: 3-4 people

Ingredients:
Crispy Potato
POTATO | आलू 4-5 MEDIUM SIZED. / 500 GRAMS
VINEGAR | सिरका 1 TSP
SALT | नमक A PINCH
CORN FLOUR | कॉर्न फ्लोर 1/4 CUP
REFINED FLOUR | मैदा 1/4 CUP
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
BLACK PEPPER | काली मिर्च A PINCH (OPTIONAL)
BAKING SODA | बेकिंग सोड़ा A PINCH
ICE COLD WATER | बर्फ वाला पानी AS REQUIRED
Sauce & Final Cooking 
GOCHUJANG | होचुजांग 2 TBSP
MIRIN | मिरिन 2 TBSP
LIGHT SOY SAUCE | लाइट सोया सॉस 1 TBSP
HONEY | शहद 2 TBSP
SESAME OIL | तिल का तेल 2 TSP
GINGER | अदरक 1 TSP (CHOPPED)
GARLIC | लहसुन 1 TSP (CHOPPED)
WHITE SESAME SEEDS | सफेद तिल (FOR GARNISH)
SPRING ONION GREENS | हरे प्याज़ के पत्ते (FOR GARNISH)

Method:
Cut the peeled potatoes into thick batons as shown in the video.
Bring water to a rolling boil in a stock pot, add vinegar & salt along with the cut potatoes.
Cook the potatoes for 3-4 minutes, then remove the potatoes from the water using a large spider & transfer them onto a sieve & let them cool down completely.
Make sure you don't overcook the potatoes, you only have to parboil them.
To make the batter add the refined flour, corn flour, salt, black pepper powder, baking soda & chilled water into a large bowl & whisk continuously to make a semi thick batter, make sure you add the water gradually while whisking continuously.
The consistency of the batter should be semi-thick as mentioned, do not make the batter extremely thick or thin. 
Further dip & coat the blanched potatoes in the batter & fry them in moderately hot oil (165 C) over medium high flame until light golden brown.
Once the potatoes turn light golden brown, transfer them into a sieve & let them rest.
Fry the potatoes in batches, do not overcrowd the oil.
Set the oil on a separate stove over low flame & let it heat up gradually until it reaches a high temperature (180° C), prepare the sauce first & then flash fry the potatoes before adding them into the sauce for that extra crispiness. 
To make the sauce, take a wok, add the gochujang and the remaining ingredients of the sauce, mix well & now switch on the flame & bring the sauce to a boil, once the sauce comes to a simmer, cook it briefly until it thickens up. 
Then switch off the flame & flash fry the potatoes in hot oil for 30 seconds to a minute & add them into the sauce.
Toss it well to coat the sauce evenly, do not cook at this stage.
Your delicious Korean Crispy Fried Potatoes are ready, serve hot & garnish them with white sesame seeds & spring onion greens.

title: Punjabi Atte Ki Pinni | Meri Maa Ki Special Pinni Ki Recipe | आटे की पिन्नी | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
Full written recipe - Atte ki Pinni
Prep time: 5-10 minutes
Cooking time: 1 hour 15 minutes
Serves: 2 kg (40 medium size pieces)

Ingredients:
EDIBLE GUM | गोंद 200 GRAMS
ALMOND | बादाम 150 GRAMS
CASHEW | काजू 150 GRAMS
GHEE | घी 400 GRAMS
WHEAT FLOUR | गेहूं का आटा 500 GRAMS
RAISIN | किशमिश 100 GRAMS
MELON SEEDS | मैगज़ 75 GRAMS 
GINGER POWDER | सोंठ 75 GRAMS 
CARDAMOM POWDER | इलायची पाउडर 35 GRAMS
POWDERED SUGAR | पीसी हुई शक्कर 400 GRAM
ALMOND | बादाम AS REQUIRED (SPLIT & FRIED)
Method:
Add the edible gum granules into a mixer jar & grind it into a fine powder, set it aside for further use.
Add the nuts into a chopped & grind them coarsely, you can also do the same using a knife.
Set a deep base heavy bottom kadhai on medium heat, add ghee & let it melt, I don’t add all the ghee at once in the beginning as its requirement depends on the weather, so out of the 400 grams I only add 250 grams in the beginning.
Once the ghee melts, add the wheat flour & stir it, combine well with the ghee, the mixture may seem too dry at this stage so add the remaining ghee, the wheat flour should become soft & resemble wet sand. 
Keep stirring using the lazy 8 motion & roast the flour over low flame until the colour changes slightly light brown or biscuity. This is the most important step of making pinni, you have to cook the wheat flour patiently until the right colour is achieved.
The mixture will slowly start to loosen up as you cook it.
As soon as you get the light brown biscuit like colour add the prepared edible gum powder & stir it, the gum will crackle as it gets fried & the texture of the mixture will also change drastically, switch off the flame once all the gum gets mixed.
Add the chopped nuts along with raisins & melon seeds, keep stirring the mixture in the kadhai itself for a few minutes so that the nuts also get cooked slightly, using a wooden spatula you can also mash the mixture, this helps to mix it properly.
Once the temperature drops slightly add the ginger powder, cardamom powder & powdered sugar, stir again while mashing it continuously, then transfer the mixture into a large paraat & mash / beat it using the spatula to help the ghee release from the mixture.
After doing this for a couple of minutes, take a spoonful of the mixture & try to shape a pinni by pressing it in your fist & forming an oval shape laddoo, if the mixture is not binding at this stage, you can add a few spoons of melted ghee & then try to shape it again.
Once the mixture is ready, shape the pinnis, I also like to stick a piece of halved fried almond by pressing it lightly on the pinni, it's completely optional.
This recipe will yield about 40 pieces of pinni, 50 grams each & 2 kgs in total.
Store the pinnis in an airtight container & keep it in a cool dry & hygienic place, enjoy them throughout winters.

title: Veg Sizzler with Garlic Pepper Sauce Recipe | वेग सिज़्ज़लर गार्लिक पैपर सॉस | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
Full Written Recipe - Veg Sizzler with Garlic Pepper Sauce
Prep time: 40-45 minutes (including the preparation of other assembly components)
Cooking time: 20-25 minutes
Serves: 4-5 people

Ingredients:
Garlic Pepper Sauce
BUTTER | मक्खन 4 TBSP
REFINED FLOUR | मैदा 4 TBSP
HOT WATER | गरम पानी 1 -1.5 LITRE
VEG STOCK CUBE | वेज स्टॉक क्यूब 1 NO. 
CELERY | सेलरी 1 TBSP (CHOPPED)
GARLIC | लहसुन 1 TBSP (CHOPPED)
GARLIC | लहसुन 1 TBSP (PASTE)
GREEN CHILLI SAUCE | ग्रीन चिली सॉस 1 TBSP 
SUGAR | शक्कर 2 TBSP 
DARK SOY SAUCE | डार्क सोया सॉस 1 TBSP
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
BLACK PEPPER | काली मिर्च 1 TSP (FRESHLY CRUSHED)
Method:
Set a sauce pan on high heat, add butter, allow it to melt & further cook until its brown in colour.
Then add maida, stir & cook the maida for 4-5 minutes on low flame, further add hot water gradually while stirring continuously to avoid any lump formation, then add the stock cube & stir well.
Further add celery, chopped garlic, garlic paste & all the remaining ingredients of the sauce stir & cook on high flame for 7-8 minutes, make sure to use black pepper that’s freshly crushed for best results.
While cooking the sauce, scum/froth will form on top, remove it with a spoon, cook until the sauce reduces & coats the back of the spoon nicely. The garlic pepper sauce is ready, keep aside, to be served later with other components over the sizzler pa.
Charred Onions
OIL | तेल 2 TSP
ONION | प्याज 4 MEDIUM SIZED. (THICK SLICES) - showing 
SALT | नमक A PINCH
Method:
Set a pan on high heat, add oil & let it get hot, then add the onions & salt, stir once and let the onions char (dark browning) for couple of minutes on high flame from one side.
Further stir & char from other side as well. Your charred onions are ready.
Veggies
OIL | तेल 1 TSP
BUTTER | मक्खन 1 TBSP
GARLIC | लहसुन 1 TBSP (CHOPPED)
MUSHROOM | मशरुम 1.5 CUP / 200 GRAMS (QUARTERS)
SALT | नमक A PINCH 
CAPSICUM | शिमला मिर्च 1 LARGE (THICK JULIENNE)
CARROT | गाजर 1 LARGE (BATONS)
FRENCH BEANS | फ्रेंच बीन्स 10-12 NOS.
BROCCOLI | ब्रॉकली 1/2 CUP (FLORETS)
BLACK PEPPER | काली मिर्च A LARGE PINCH
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
SUGAR | शक्कर A PINCH
RED CHILLI FLAKES | रेड चिली फलैक्स A PINCH 
Method:
In the same pan that you used to make the onions add oil & butter, let it get hot & then add garlic, mushrooms, salt & stir well & cook over high flame until the mushrooms release their moisture & let that moisture evaporate, the mushrooms will get slightly brown during this process.
Next, add all the remaining veggies with salt, sugar & red chilli flakes, stir well & cook all the veggies over high flame for 3-4 minutes, they should get cooked but still retain their crunch. The sauteed veggies are ready.
Additional components for assembly
CABBAGE | पत्ता गोबी (ROUGHLY CUT)
SWEET CORN | स्वीट कॉर्न 1 CUP (BOILED)
COOKED SELLA BASMATI RICE | पके हुए सेला बासमती चावल 4 CUPS
FRENCH FRIES | फ्रेंच फ्राइज़ AS REQUIRED 
Assembly (on sizzler plate)
CABBAGE | पत्ता गोबी
RICE | चावल
VEGETBALES | सब्ज़ियां
CHARRED ONIONS | चार्ड ओनियंस
SWEET CORN | स्वीट कॉर्न 
FRENCH FRIES | फ्रेंच फ्राइज़SAUCE
PROCESSED CHEESE | प्रोसेस्ड चीज़ 
BUTTER | मख्हन  
Method:
Set a sizzler pan on highest flame possible, heat it really well for a couple of minutes, be very careful while handling the hot sizzler pan, use 2 tongs if required.
Set the hot sizzler pan in the wooden case using 2 tongs for the assembly.
Spread a bed of cabbage leaves over the hot sizzler pan, place the cooker rice, then add a portion of the stir-fried veggies, charred onions, french fries, sweet corn & french fries on the side.
Then pour hot garlic pepper sauce over all the components. Garnish it by grating some processed cheese & serve more sauce on the side.

title: Gajar Ka Halwa Recipe in Pressure Cooker | कुकर में आसानी से बनाइये गाजर का हलवा | Chef Sanjoyt Keer
description:
Full Written Recipe - Gajar Ka Halwa (Pressure Cooker)

Prep time: 10 minutes
Cooking time: 35-40 minutes
Serves: 5-6 people

Ingredients:
RED CARROT | लाल गाजर 1/2 KG
GHEE | घी 1 TBSP
MILK | दूध 500 ML (FULL FAT)
SUGAR | शक्कर 1/2 CUP / 100 GRAMS
GREEN CARDAMOM POWDER | इलायची पाउडर A PINCH 
GHEE | घी 2 TBSP (for frying nuts)
CASHEW | काजू 2 TBSP (ROUGHLY CHOPPED)
ALMOND | बादाम 2 TBSP (SLICED)
RAISINS | किशमिश 1 TBSP
Method:
Wash the carrots & then trim their tops & bottoms, further peel the carrots & grate them using a large hole grater then transfer them into a bowl.
Set a pressure cooker over high heat & add the ghee, let it get slightly hot & then add all the grated carrot, stir well & cook for 3-4 minutes.
The carrots will release its moisture so cook until the water evaporates.
Further add the milk, stir once then pressure cook over medium flame only for 1 whistle then switch off the flame & let the cooker depressurize naturally.
Next, check the whistle & open the lid, stir well & then switch on the flame to medium high & cook until the milk reduces, this process will take 10-12 minutes, make sure you keep stirring the halwa.
Once almost all the milk has reduced, add the sugar & cardamom powder, stir well & cook it again for 4-5 minutes, the sugar will release its moisture & the halwa will get slightly runny again so cook until it thickens up again.
Do not overcook the halwa or it will become very tight, almost into barfi texture, only until the water from sugar evaporates & the halwa looks a bit shiny then switch off the flame.
Heat a pan, add the ghee into it & let it get slightly hot, then add the almonds & cashews & fry them until they turn light golden brown, keep stirring them.
Once the nuts are almost about to turn light golden brown add the raisins & stir briefly.
Pour the ghee & nuts mixture over the halwa & stir well.
Your gajar ka halwa is ready, let it rest for a couple of minutes, it will soak up all the ghee, heat it up before serving or have as it is, I personally like to have hot halwa with a scoop of vanilla ice cream.

Veg Schezwan Noodles
Prep time: 20-25 minutes
Cooking time: 25-30 minutes
Serves: 3-4 people

Ingredients:
Cooked Red Chilli Paste
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च 20 NOS. (DESEEDED & SOAKED IN HOT WATER)
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED 
OIL | तेल 4-5 TBSP 
RED CHILLI PASTE | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च की पेस्ट 
SALT | नमक A LARGE PINCH 
VINEGAR | सिरका 1 TBSP
Boiling Noodle
SALT | नमक A LARGE PINCH
OIL | तेल 1 TSP 
NOODLES | नूडल्स 1 PACKET / 200 GRAMS 
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED 
OIL | तेल 2 TSP
Final Cooking
OIL | तेल 1 TBSP
RED CHILLI | लाल मिर्च 1 NO. 
GARLIC | लहसुन 1 TBSP (CHOPPED)
GINGER | अदरक 1 TSP (CHOPPED)
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 3 NOS.
COOKED RED CHILLI PASTE | पकी हुई लाल मिर्च की पेस्ट 1 TBSP (WITH OIL)
LIGHT SOY SAUCE | लाइट सोया सॉस 1/2 TSP
ONION | प्याज़ 1/2 NO. (SLICED)
CAPSICUM | शिमला मिर्च 1 NO. (JULIENNE)
CARROT | गाजर 1 MEDIUM SIZE. (JULIENNE) 
CABBAGE | पत्ता गोबी 1/2 SMALL SIZE. (CHIFFONADE)
BOILED NOODLES | उबले हुए नूडल्स
WHITE PEPPER POWDER | सफेद मिर्च का पाउडर A LARGE PINCH 
SUGAR | शक्कर A PINCH
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
SPRING ONION GREENS | हरे प्याज़ के पत्ते A HANDFUL (BATONS)
Method:
Deseed & soak the kashmiri red chillies in hot water for an hour then add them into a mixer grinder jar with a splash of water & grind them into a fine paste, make sure that the paste is not too runny.
Set a kadhai over high flame & once it gets hot, add the oil & let the oil heat up as well.
Further add the red chilli paste, stir well & cook over medium high flame until the paste turns crumbly in texture & all the oil is released, this process will take 7-8 minutes.
Once cooked, add salt & vinegar, stir well & your cooked red chilli paste is ready, transfer it into a bowl & cool it down completely.
You can store it in an airtight container, refrigerate it & use it for upto 4 months, you will only require 1 tablespoon of this paste for this recipe but you can also use the same paste to make other Indo Chinese dishes like schezwan fried rice with any red gravy, schezwan paneer, veg crispy etc.
If you only want to make red chilli paste for one portion then follow the same process using 3-5 kashmiri red chillies.
Bring water to a rolling boil, add salt & oil.
Add noodles in the boiling water & only boil them for 30 seconds or until all the noodles have separated.
Further, switch off the flame & cover it for 2-3 minutes.
Then remove them using a pair of tongs & transfer them into a sieve, rinse them with cold water immediately to stop the cooking process & drizzle some oil over them to prevent them from sticking to each other.
Set the kadhai on high flame & let it get hot, then add oil & let it get hot.
Further add the red chilli, garlic, ginger & green chilli, stir & cook over high flame for 30 seconds.
Add the cooked red chilli paste & light soy sauce & give it a stir.
Next, add all the veggies, stir well & cook over high flame for 2 minutes, make sure that the crunch in the veggies is maintained.
Add the boiled noodles, white pepper powder, sugar, salt & the spring onion greens, toss & mix everything using two spatulas or a pair of tongs, you don’t have to cook a lot at this stage, just mix everything over high flame.
Restaurant style veg schezwan noodles are ready.

title: Surti Vatidal Khaman Recipe | Perfect Tips & Measurement | बाजार जैसे गुजरती खमण | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
Written Recipe - Vatidal Na Khaman 
Prep time: 5-10 minutes
Soaking time: 6-8 hours
Fermentation time: 8-12 hours
Serves: 6-8 people

Ingredients:
Base Batter
CHANA DAL | चना दाल 1 CUP / 215 GRAMS
WATER | पानी 1/2 CUP (FOR GRINDING)
WATER | पानी 3 TBSP (FOR GRINDING)
GRAM FLOUR | बेसन 2 TBSP
CITRIC ACID | नींबू का सत 1/4 TSP
Final Batter
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 4 NOS.
GINGER | अदरक 1 INCH
GROUNDNUT OIL | मूंगफली का तेल 1.5 TBSP
ASAFOETIDA | हींग 1/4 TSP
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/2 TSP
SUGAR | शक्कर A LARGE PINCH
Cooking Khaman 
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
ENO | ईनो 1/2 TSP (for 1 tray)
WATER | पानी 1 TSP
Tempering
GROUNDNUT OIL | मूंगफली का तेल 2-3 TBSP
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 7-8 SLIT (LESS SPICY)
MUSTARD SEEDS | राई 2 TSP
CURRY LEAVES | कड़ी पत्ता = 10-12 NOS. 
WATER | पानी 1/4 CUP
SALT | नमक A PINCH
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A LARGE HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
Method:
Add the chana dal into a bowl & wash it well with water until it turns clear, then add fresh water & let the dal soak for 5-6 hours or overnight.
Once soaked, discard the water & add the soaked dal into a mixer grinder jar along with some water & grind it into a thick semi-fine batter, I have given the quantity of the water as per my measurements, just make sure that the batter is not too runny & once grinded the made should feel slightly grainy, just like fine semolina.
Transfer the batter into a large bowl or a tall vessel & add the gram flour, citric acid & whisk the batter for 2-3 minutes.
Then cover & place it in a warm corner of the kitchen & let it ferment for 8-10 hours or overnight.
During winters the batter takes a longer time to ferment so you can wrap the bowl/vessel using a thick cloth.
Once fermented, the batter will become airy & almost rise to double in size.
Mix the fermented batter gently to release all the excess air & then add the ginger & green chilli paste, groundnut oil, asafoetida, turmeric powder & sugar, whisk well for a minute until all the ingredients get incorporated.
The base batter for khaman is ready, if you are planning to make only half of it then you can divide & store the remaining before adding the salt & eno (fruit salt).
Before adding the eno, make sure that the steamer is ready & also grease the trays (thalis) with oil in which you are going to steam the khaman.
I usually like to steam 1 thali at a time as it allows the khaman to cook more evenly & also prevents the surface of the khaman from getting wet due to condensation.
Add salt to the batter & mix well, then transfer half of it into a bowl & add ½ tsp eno along with a tsp of water & mix it thoroughly.
Then pour the batter into the greased tray & place it in the steamer, let the khaman steam for 15 minutes & do not open the lid before that as it may affect the rise.
After 15 mintues, open the lid & check if the khaman is cooked or not by inserting a knife in the center, it should come out clean.
Once cooked, remov the tray from the steamer & let the khaman cool down completely, meanwhile add ½ tsp eno to the remaining batter along with a tsp of water & then steam it similarly.
Once cooled, cut the khaman as per your preference & remove them from the tray by scraping with the help of a metal spatula, reserve some smaller & irregular pieces for the chutney.
Add all the khaman into a large bowl & prepare the tempering.
Set a pan over high flame, add in the oil & let it get hot. 
Further add the green chillies & fry them briefly, then add the mustard seeds, curry leaves, water & a pinch of salt, make sure that the oil is not extremely hot before adding the water & use a deep pan.
Add the tempering over the khaman along with fresh coriander & mix well.
Your delicious Vatidal Na Khaman are ready, serve it with some special green chutney & sev.

Special Green chutney
Ingredients:
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया 1 CUP
MINT | पुदीना ½ CUP
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2-3 NOS.
GINGER | अदरक ½ INCH
KHAMAN | खमण 2-3 PIECES
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1 TSP
SUGAR | शक्कर 1 TBSP
LEMON JUICE | नींबू का रस OF A LEMON
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
ICE CUBE | बर्फ 2 NOS.
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED
Method:
Add all the ingredients in a mixer grinder jar & grind it into a fine chutney, add very little water, this chutney needs to be thick in consistency.
Your green chutney is ready.

title: Perfect Mohanthal Recipe | Danedar Mohanthal | बाज़ार से बेहतर मोहनताल बनाने का तरीका | Sanjyot Keer
description:
Full written recipe for Mohanthal

Prep time: 5-10 minutes
Cooking time: 35-40 minutes (3-4 hours for the mohanthal to set)
Serves: 650 grams (approx.)

Ingredients:
Grainy gram flour mixture
COARSE GRAM FLOUR | जाड़ा बेसन 2 CUPS / 270 GRAMS
GHEE | घी 2 TBSP / 25 GRAMS
MILK | दूध 2 TBSP / 30 ML
Cooking gram flour
GHEE | घी 3/4 CUP / 150 GRAMS
MILK | दूध 1/2 CUP / 115 ML
Sugar Syrup
SUGAR | शक्कर 1 CUP / 200 GRAMS
WATER | पानी 1/2 CUP / 115 ML
CARDAMOM POWDER | इलाइची पाउडर 1/2 TSP
SAFFRON | केसर A FEW STRANDS
NUTMEG | जायफल A PINCH (GRATED)
For garnish
ALMONDS | बादाम AS REQUIRED (SLICED)
PISTACHIO | पिस्ता AS REQUIRED (SLICED)
Method:
Add the gram flour into a large bowl along with ghee & milk, mix & rub the gram flour together between your hands then press all the gram flour down onto the bowl together, cover & let it rest for 15-20 minutes.
Once rested, break the set flour using your hands then sieve it into another large bowl using a large hole sieve (wheat strainer), do not use a fine sieve otherwise you will not be able to achieve the right texture, if any hard lumps of the flour remain, just pulse them using a mixer grinder & then sieve.
If you do not have the sieve then you can directly pulse the mixer into tiny grains using the mixer grinder.
Before cooking the gram flour, line a tray or a large thali using ghee & butter paper, I used a 9x7 inch aluminum cake tin. 
Add half quantity of the ghee into a kadhai & let it melt over high flame, then add the prepared gram flour mixture & stir well, once the flour soaks up all the ghee then add the remaining & stir well.
Cook the gram flour over low flame while stirring continuously until it turns biscuity in colour, the mixture will loosen up a bit & foam up during the cooking process, this will take about 20-25 minutes.
Once it turns biscuity in colour, switch off the flame & add the milk gradually, just a spoonful at a time initially as the mixture will bubble up, so be extremely carefully.
Once you have added a few spoons & the mixture has stopped bubbling then you can add all the remaining milk a once, make sure you keep stirring.
Switch on the flame to low again & cook the mixture for 4-5 minutes or until a slight glaze appears, the ghee should not separate completely.
Then switch off the flame, keep stirring the mixture for 2-3 minutes & let it cool down slightly, if you let it sit in the kadhai then the mixture will darken due to residual heat.
In a separate pan, add all the ingredients of the sugar syrup & bring it to a boil over high flame, then lower the flame & cook the syrup once it reaches one-string consistency.
To check, take a small amount of syrup on a spoon and let it cool for a few seconds to avoid burns, pinch the syrup between your thumb & index finger, then pull your fingers apart gently, if a single thin string forms between your fingers, the syrup has reached one-string consistency.
Switch off the flame & keep stirring the syrup & let it cool down for 2-3 minutes.
Add the syrup into the cooked gram flour, stir it for 1-2 minutes until everything gets combined.
Then transfer the mohanthal mixture into the tray & spread it evenly then even out the surface using a spatula or a flat bowl.
You can optionally add slivered nuts on the mohanthal at this stage & let it set for at least 3 hours of overnight.
Once set, remove it using the butter paper & then cut into pieces.
Your delicious & melt in the mouth mohanthal is ready.

title: Punjabi Tandal Recipe | Free में Gobhi के डंठल लाइयें Aur बनाइए Delicious Recipe | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
Written Recipe - Gobhi Ke Danthal Ki Sabzi (Punjabi Tandal)

Prep time: 15-20 mins
Cooking time: 30-35 mins
Serves: 4-5 people 

Ingredients:

CAULIFLOWER STEM | फूलगोबी की डंठल 500 GRAMS
GHEE | घी 2 TBSP
OIL | तेल 2 TSP
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1 TSP
CINNAMON | दालचीनी 2 INCH
BLACK CARDAMOM | बड़ी इलाइची 1 NO. 
GREEN CARDAMOM | हरी इलाइची 2 NO. 
BAY LEAF | तेज पत्ता 1 NO. 
ONION | प्याज़ 5 MEDIUM SIZED. (CHOPPED)
GINGER GARLIC GREEN CHILLI PASTE | अदरक लहसुन हरी मिर्च की पेस्ट 2 TBSP
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/4 TSP
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1 TSP
SPICY RED CHILLI | तीखी लाल मिर्च पाउडर  1-2 TSP
CORIANDER POWDER | धनिया पाउडर 2 TBSP
HOT WATER | गरम पानी A SPLASH
TOMATO | टमाटर 3 NOS. (ROUGHLY CHOPPED)
SALT | नमक TO TASTE 
HOT WATER | गरम पानी 150-200 ML
SALT | नमक IF REQUIRED 
ANARDANA POWDER | अनारदाना पाउडर 1 TSP
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
MINT | पुदीना A FEW LEAVES (CHOPPED)
GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला A PINCH
ROASTED KASURI METHI POWDER A PINCH | भुनी कसूरी मेथी पाउडर A PINCH

Method:
You can source the stems from you local vegetable vendor, they sometimes clean the stems themselves and then sell. 
Once you have acquired the stems, cut the leafy parts from the sides & prep the remaining ingredients the same manner.
Further cut the stems into quarters, I like to keep them 2-3 inches long.
The inner portion of the stem is edible & the covering is hard & wood like.
Wash the cut stems using water, for this recipe you will need about 500 grams of them. 
Set a pressure cooker over high heat, add ghee & oil, let it get hot. 
Add cumin seeds along with all the remaining whole spices & onions, stir well & cook the onions over high flame until they turn golden brown.
Once the onions turn light golden brown, add the ginger garlic green chilli paste and continue to cook further.
Then lower the flame & add all the powdered spices along with a splash of hot water & cook the spices until the ghee separates.
Then add tomatoes along with salt & cook until the tomato turns soft, if the mixture gets too dry you can add hot water & continue to cook further.
Once the tomatoes have cooked & the ghee has separated add the stems, stir & cook over high flame for a few minutes.
Then add the water & pressure cooker over high flame for one whistle then cook over low flame for 7-8 minutes.
Once cooked, switch off the flame & let the cooker depressurize naturally.
Open the lid & stir well, switch on the flame to high again & taste & adjust salt if required.
Add anardana powder, fresh coriander, mint, garam masala & kasuri methi powder.
The fleshy side of the stem will turn mushy, just like a boiled potato.
Your delicious Punjabi Tandal is ready, serve it with tandoori roti or bread of your choice, once you eat the mushy part of the stem, eat it like a drumstick.

title: Kashmiri Laal Paneer Recipe | Waze Tchaman | कश्मीरी लाल पनीर | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
Written Recipe - Kashmiri Laal Paneer (Waze Tchaman)
Prep time: 15-20 minutes (excluding chilli soaking time)
Cooking time: 45 minutes – 1 hour
Serves: 4-5 people

Ingredients:
Cooking Paneer
PANEER | पनीर 500 GRAMS 
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1 TSP
MUSTARD OIL | सरसों का तेल AS REQUIRED (FOR SHALLOW FRYING)
Fried Onion Paste
FRIED ONION | बरिस्ता 2 MEDIUM SIZED.
WATER | पानी VERY LITTLE 
Red Chilli Paste
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च 7-8 NOS. (DESEEDED & SOAKED FOR AN HOUR)
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED  
Final Cooking
REMAINING MUSTARD OIL | बचा हुआ सरसों का तेल 
ASAFOETIDA | हींग 1/2 TSP
TOMATO PUREE | टमाटर की प्यूरी 400 GRAMS
KASHMIRI CHILLI PASTE | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च की पेस्ट 
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/2 TSP
BLACK CARDAMOM | बड़ी इलाइची 1 NO.
GREEN CARDAMOM | हरी इलाइची 3 NOS. 
CLOVES | लौंग 3 NOS. 
CINNAMON | दालचीनी 2 INCH
FENNEL SEED POWDER | सौंफ का पाउडर 1.5 TBSP
GINGER POWDER | सोंठ पाउडर 2 TSP
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
HOT WATER | गरम पानी 500 ML
FRIED ONION PASTE | बरिस्ता की पेस्ट 
ROASTED CUMIN POWDER | भुने जीरे का पाउडर A LARGE PINCH
Method:
Cut the paneer block into thick slices, approximately 2-3 cm thick, you can choose to keep it in a square shape or rectangles, paneer pieces are usually cut & prepared like this in Kashmir.
Bring water to a rolling boil in a stock pot & add turmeric powder, then add the paneer & let it simmer for 5-6 minutes.
Then carefully transfer the paneer pieces onto a plate & let them cool down & dry-off slightly.
Set a pan over high heat, add the mustard oil & let it get hot until it starts smoking then switch off the flamr & let the oil cool down slightly.
Add the paneer pieces & fry them over medium high flame until they turn golden brown from both the sides, then set them aside & reserve the oil to be used further in the recipe.
Add the fried onions into a small mixer jar & grind it into a fine paste using water as required.
Similarly, add the soaked Kashmiri chillies into a mixer jar along with some water & grind it into a fine paste.
Add the reserved mustard oil into a kadhai & let it get hot.
Further add asafoetida, tomato puree, red chilli paste, turmeric powder, all the whole spices, fennel & ginger powder, salt & hot water, stir well & let it come to a boil.
Add the fried onion paste & cook the gravy over medium high flame for 12-15 minutes until the tomato puree gets fully cooked & the gravy reduces.
Then add the fried paneer pieces, stir well, cover & cook over medium low flame for 7-8 minutes.
Once cooked you can adjust the consistency of the gravy as per your preference, authentically it is not too runny.
Lastly just taste & adjust seasoning if required & add roasted cumin powder
Your delicious Kashmiri Laal Paneer is ready, serve it hot with some rice or any bread of your choice.
